review_title: The "Marketplace" excuse is a trap to dodge product liability. Avoid.
review_text: Amazon UK’s customer safety standards are an absolute façade. I purchased a dedicated aquarium light that suffered a catastrophic manufacturing failure, breached its own seals, short-circuited, and discharged an electrical current directly into my tank—instantly destroying an incredibly rare, premium clutch of livestock worth thousands of pounds.When you try to hold the third-party seller accountable, Amazon’s automated "Seller Messaging Assistant" deliberately intercepts the pathway. It locks you out of contacting the vendor directly and explicitly tells you: “Amazon handles fulfillment for this order. Contact Amazon Customer Service for assistance—they can resolve this quickly.”This is a calculated trap. Amazon forces themselves as the sole gatekeeper for the transaction, but the moment a product catastrophically fails and causes massive property damage, they immediately try to hide behind the "third-party marketplace" loophole to dodge the Consumer Protection Act. They pass your claim off to an aggressive external insurance handler whose sole job is to ignore evidence, distort facts, and stonewall you until you give up.If you buy anything electronic on this platform, know that if it malfunctions and burns your house down or destroys your property, Amazon will actively block you from reaching the seller while washing their hands of any statutory supplier liability. Disgraceful corporate ethics. See you in the County Court.

1 Star – Extremely Disappointing Customer ServiceAfter a recent experience with a damaged Amazon gift card, I wouldn't use or recommend using this company ever again. It has genuinely been one of the most frustrating customer service experiences I have ever encountered.The gift card was received as a Christmas gift and was physically damaged, making the code unreadable and unusable. I reported the issue in January and have spent approximately five months trying to resolve it through Amazon customer support.During that time, I contacted Amazon (via email and phone) more than 20 times, was told the issue had been escalated on multiple occasions, and repeatedly had to explain the situation from the beginning. Months passed with little to no meaningful communication.Only after filing a complaint through the Better Business Bureau did I finally receive any sort of 'closure'. Amazon then advised that they required the original purchase receipt before they could investigate further. As the card was received as a gift from a parent, I do not have access to the purchaser's receipt and had explained this numerous times throughout the process.What is most disappointing is not only the final decision, but the amount of time, effort, and frustration required simply to obtain a clear answer. If proof of purchase was always required, this could have been communicated at the start rather than after five months of emails and escalations.Amazon is a company with a $3trillion market cap, yet can't refund a $100 gift voucher (the closest thing we get to a 'tip') to a poorly paid teacher. If ever there was a sign of the times - this is it.Based on this experience, I have lost all confidence in Amazon's customer support and resolution process.

review_title: I’ve ordered products from Amazon for…
review_text: I’ve ordered products from Amazon for years, having all my deliveries sent to my P.O. Box because of the concern that porch pirates would steal my pkgs from my porch b4 I get the chance to get them. About a month ago, I cancelled my P.O. Box & had all my pkgs delivered to my apt. The only delivery instruction option that comes close to putting the pkgs inside the front door was ‘Mailroom’, which Amazon Customer Service has continually told me is in my account. 3 of my last 4 deliveries were left on the porch. The only thing good about the delivery is that the driver sends me a confirmation picture of the pkg & where it’s left. So much for following the instructions to put it inside the front door, which Amazon calls the ‘Mailroom’. I received a message on 5/23/26 that the delivery was just made & the picture I was sent clearly shows the pkg in the porch, AGAIN. I went downstairs & confronted the driver who was still across the street, while I filmed the conversation. I contacted Customer Service & they said they’ll escalate the issue. I was finally emailed by Amazon to call their Incident Response Team @ (844) 606-5509 PIN 2019. The Representative Magda who answered the phone was more concerned about the driver’s complaint against me more than my complaint against the driver & the drivers who delivered the previous 2 deliveries which were also left on my porch on 5/5/26 & 5/22/26, even though she knows I videotaped the conversation with the lazy driver on 5/23/26. Obviously nothing is going to be resolved to my satisfaction, since to date, nobody has asked me to sent them my video. Clearly Amazon prefers to back up their drivers who are too lazy to read the delivery instructions. Porch pirates are everywhere. Don’t be surprised if u leave delivery instructions & their lazy Amazon drivers instead just decide to toss the pkg on the porch & take a picture of it. Time for me to order through shoppers directly instead of through Amazon, because although they get your pkgs to u quicker, they just don’t seem to care by not taking your concerns seriously. Tom Ruggirello

review_title: Failed Repair, No Refund, and Poor Customer Service from Amazon
review_text: Very disappointed with Amazon’s handling of a faulty De’Longhi coffee machine.The item developed a fault and was sent to Amazon for repair before the warranty expired. Amazon kept the machine for over a month, then returned it still broken and unusable. Despite this failed repair attempt, customer service has continued to refer me to the standard 30-day returns policy instead of addressing my rights under the UK Consumer Rights Act 2015.Under UK consumer law, when a repair fails, the customer is entitled to a refund or replacement from the retailer. The issue was reported within warranty and the item was in Amazon’s custody during that period, yet resolving this has become unnecessarily difficult.What is most frustrating is the lack of response and accountability after multiple follow-ups. I expected better customer service from a company of Amazon’s size and reputation.

review_text: I became aware of a driver of a white van in my street, who on talking to him a few minutes later, turned out to be from Amazon, struggling with repeated attempts to keep the side door of his van closed this afternoon, so I ultimately went down to try to help him, this also happening in the midst of a current heatwave here in the U.K.! Some of these Amazon delivery drivers are really something else and definitely not for the faint hearted to get involved with! Regards, Brent Collins.

If there were "zero" stars, that would be my pick.My husband and I both have experienced the same type of issue with different products...Here is the issue.  I ordered two different pull-out shelves for kitchen cabinets, on the same order. When they arrived, in separate packaging, I opened both and tried them out. Neither worked for me.I began the return for both items.  They both required that I re-package in original boxes, and get a return label, but that I could take them to a UPS store for shipping, free of charge, back to Amazon.  I did just that, returning both packages, on the same day (this seems to be where the problem arises), to a UPS store.Once UPS sent off the packages, I received refunds on BOTH items.Now, 6 weeks later, Amazon sends a series of e-mails to me concerning one of the returns, stating that the product wasn't received and my refund is not valid.  Next e-mail says "We've received more items than we were expecting for your return. We've issued a refund for the items you originally purchased from Amazon. We understand that you received extra items by mistake. These extra items are not eligible for a refund, but we appreciate you returning them."SO ...1) they DID get more than one box sent back to them2) some underpaid bored worker marked the two items as the same code rather than the two distinct different codes.3) of course, the e-mail is from an outgoing only e-mail address at Amazon.  All my efforts to get an e-mail address to respond and point out the error on their part have been fruitless. I spent 30 minutes doing the round-robin on Amazon customer service where none of the links gets me to a useful place.My husband's experience was two sets of hub caps ordered on same order and returned the same day. Same exact experience with Amazon not acknowledging that two items were returned, not a duplicate of one item returned.Lessons learned:1)don't ever order two different items, for a trial look at them, on the same order.2) And always return anything on separate days.

review_title: Scams involving Amazon gift cards purchased in Canada at Shoppers Drug Mart.pproximately two months ago
review_text: Approximately two months ago, I purchased an Amazon gift card from a cashier at Shoppers Drug Mart rather than from the public display. When I attempted to activate the card, Amazon informed me that the card had already been activated.Shoppers Drug Mart advised me to contact Amazon directly, citing its policy of not accepting returns or refunds on gift cards. I then contacted Amazon and provided all requested documentation, including proof of purchase and photographs of the card.Over the following two months, I repeatedly followed up with Amazon regarding the issue. Although my case was escalated multiple times, no resolution was provided. Now, nearly three months later, I have been told by an Amazon representative that I must dispute the matter with Shoppers Drug Mart instead.Unfortunately, both companies appear to rely on their internal policies, leaving consumers without protection or resolution. Meanwhile, fraudulent activity continues, and customers bear the financial loss.Please be cautious when purchasing gift cards, as this situation could happen to anyone.

review_title: I purchased a De'Longhi Magnifica S…
review_text: I purchased a De'Longhi Magnifica S coffee maker for €300 and it stopped working within the warranty period. Amazon agreed to a warranty repair, but the problem is that I live in Tenerife – which is a special customs territory – and sending the package to Madrid requires a customs declaration.Amazon's return label did not include any customs documentation, so the post office refused to accept the package. I contacted Amazon customer service multiple times to resolve this simple issue. Instead of helping, a manager joined the chat and ended it without any explanation or resolution.A €300 product under warranty, and Amazon cannot provide the correct shipping documentation. This is not a customer service – this is negligence.I will now be contacting the Spanish consumer organization OCU and filing a formal complaint. I strongly advise anyone on the Canary Islands to think twice before purchasing from Amazon.es.

On Friday, I issued an order to Amazon for food from Whole Foods. The order went through; the amount was 32 some-odd dollars, and the food was delivered in a reasonable timeframe. So far, so good! It was raining cats and dogs, so I gave the delivery man a very large tip in cash, thanking him for braving the bad weather.One day later, a second charge appeared on my Discover card, attributed to "Amazon tips." It turns out that Amazon decided, without any notification to me or any approval by me, to go ahead and charge a tip for the man who delivered the product. In other words, there was a second charge that I had no knowledge of and did not authorize, but they went ahead and applied it to my credit card anyway.Nowhere in the checkout process was there an indication that Amazon would either apply a tip or was looking to me to apply a tip.According to both Amazon and the credit card company, this does NOT constitute fraud. I must disagree. Just because I have a credit card on file does not entitle the holder of that card to make charges without my knowledge or consent. I consider that fraud.So, be on the lookout for "Amazon tips," and there is a tip for you.

review_title: Nothing's perfect, but Amazon is Great for us!
review_text: Nothing's perfect, but overall the 2 people in our household LOVE Amazon. We live in a small "city" (i think of it more as a larger town) in a semi-rural area.  Even if we lived in a megalopolis, we'd be happy with it.  Yes, deliveries are sometimes late.  Yes, sometimes something arrives damaged. Yes, some third party products are crap (buyer beware:  read reviews carefully and we buy almost exclusively only when there are many reviews.)  But here's what matters:  unmatched selection; you get to read listings and reviews to learn the real scoop, product dimensions, etc.; reasonable prices; usually free delivery & returns with PRIME (yes,it costs, but it's done well for us overall); GREAT customer service (use the chat function and they do make good when a problem with the item); most deliveries arrive on time; and most (though not all) 3rd party sellers want to avoid bad reviews or complaints to Amazon, so they likewise bend over backwards to please if you're nice when contacting them (use the "ask a question" feature on the seller's page).  It takes some time to learn how to do things through the desktop version or the app, but overall it's been time well-spent.  Not only can we usually find what we're looking for, but we also discover and have bought SO many things that we never imagined (didn't thing existed) that have made our lives better.   Our lives are SO much easier -- and no, I'm not an influencer or someone who spends all their time shopping on Amazon.  Personally, we think Amazon is a huge plus for America!

Title: Repeated delivery manipulation, refund delays, and poor customer serviceI have been an Amazon customer for years, but my recent experiences have become increasingly frustrating and disappointing.I have noticed a repeated pattern when purchasing multiple items in the same order with different delivery dates available. Even when I specifically select faster delivery options for certain items, Amazon often delays the entire shipment and sends everything together using the latest delivery date, effectively ignoring the delivery choices made by the customer.This creates a misleading buying experience because customers believe they are selecting earlier delivery options that are later disregarded by Amazon’s system.The situation becomes even worse when trying to cancel delayed orders. Once Amazon changes or misses the promised delivery timeline, customers are frequently not allowed to cancel the shipment before arrival. Instead, they are forced to wait for the package, then process a return, and only after that wait again for a refund.This entire process can take 10 to 15 days or more, involving unnecessary waiting, repeated customer service calls, and multiple complaints just to recover money for orders that were not delivered as originally promised.Customer service representatives often provide inconsistent information depending on who answers the call, making the experience even more frustrating.Occasional delays are understandable, but repeated delivery manipulation, ignored shipping selections, cancellation obstacles, and refund delays demonstrate serious problems in Amazon’s logistics and customer service practices.Amazon needs to improve transparency, respect customer-selected delivery options, allow reasonable cancellations when delivery commitments are not met, and significantly improve refund handling and customer support consistency.

⚠️ Warning About Keeping Large Amazon Gift Card BalancesI want to share my recent experience with "amazon.com" to warn others.Over the past few years, I accumulated approximately $960 in Amazon gift cards from legitimate sources:- $500 and $300 rewards from wso2 as part of their Ambassador Program.When I attempted to place an order, Amazon flagged my account for “unusual activity” and requested proof of ownership of the gift cards.I provided:- My government-issued ID.- Official emails proving the gift cards were legitimate rewards.Despite this:- My orders were canceled.- My account was restricted and later closed.- I lost access to my $960 gift card balance.At one point, Amazon even emailed me saying my appeal had a positive outcome and that I could place a new order, but shortly afterward my account was restricted again.My AdviceTo reduce risk, consider redeeming gift cards only when you are ready to use them rather than storing large balances on your account.Questions for the Community- Has anyone experienced something similar?- Were you able to recover your account or balance?- What steps helped resolve the issue?I hope sharing this experience helps others avoid similar problems.

review_title: I Can No Longer Trust Amazon After This…
review_text: I Can No Longer Trust Amazon After This ExperienceAs part of an earlier issue, I had filed an A-to-Z claim regarding a problematic order experience. During the resolution process, an Amazon Customer Service representative offered what was described as a one-time reimbursement exception for a future shipped-by-Amazon order under a specified amount. I received this promise in writing and carefully followed every instruction provided:•   purchased only shipped-by-Amazon items•   kept the total within the required limit•   waited until the order was fully delivered•   contacted Amazon again exactly as instructedHowever, when I followed up after delivery, another supervisor informed me that the previous representative had provided “false information” and that Amazon would not honor the written promise made to me.For me, the issue is not simply financial. Trust is one of the most important values in any relationship between a company and its customers. When a company gives written assurances and later refuses to stand behind them, customer trust is broken.I remained calm and respectful throughout the entire process, but after this experience I personally no longer feel comfortable doing business with a company that does not honor its own written commitments. Because of this, I have decided to close my Amazon account and move my future purchases to businesses that value transparency, accountability, and fair treatment of their customers.

review_title: Amazon is BY FAR the most disfunctional and customer unfriendly online shop.
review_text: Amazon is BY FAR the last garbage on earth.The only advantage it gives is the fast shipping.But the UX and UI of the website are THE WORST EVER, completely DYSFUNCTIONAL, random, illogical.More over, the level of UNFRIENDLINESS against people with difficult conditions like handicapped people who cannot leave home to go to the packet shop is UNPRECEDENTED, INHUMAN, UNFORGIVABLE.Even in Germany, a Country with IN THEORY a very high level of attention to non-discrimination against differently-able people, Amazon doesn't comply IN THE F. SLIGHTEST.One example: there are two kinds of QR Code for DHL, which are free of cost, as it MUST BE, because Amazon PROMISES free of cost returns for most articles for prime customers.BUT one of these two QR Codes, the one that says "label free and box free", only works when you personally go to the post. When DHL picks up the parcel at your place, it does not work. It also doesn't work in 70% of packet shops, e.g. the small ones like the tobacco shop or the drinks kiosk that you find in most blocks. I.e. this QR Code FORCES PEOPLE TO GO LOOK FOR A SPECIFIC KIND OF DHL PLACE, it's VERY UNFRIENDLY towards professionally and personally busy people, workers, mothers, AND EVEN MORE UNFRIENDLY against old people who can't walk well, or handicapped people like me who can't walk AT ALL.Just a few years ago Amazon used to give us the choice, i.e. BOTH QR Codes were given as option when you started a return, and if you still had the box you would choose "label free", otherwise you would choose "label free and box free".But since one year 90% of the times Amazon ONLY offers "label free box free".I was made fun of, insulted, offended, and attacked at the telephone from several Amazon representatives who victim blamed me for being an handicapped person, told "it's your problem, Amazon already gives you this QR Code, it's your problem if you can't use it".YOU ARE NOT PEOPLE! YOU ARE NOT HUMAN!!!WHAT YOU DO IS DISCRIMINATION AGAINST OLD, SICK AND DISABLE PEOPLE! THIS IS INHUMAN!!!And totally unnecessary!!!You have both QR Codes in the system!!! And sometimes you still offer both! There is no technical, logistical, economical or ANY other reason to only offer the difficult one!!!If you have to only offer one, offer "label free" which works always and everywhere and let the idiots who threw away the package pay for their idiocy by buying a package themselves from DHL!!!To make the life easier for these idiots you make it difficult for challenged categories which you should protect and respect!!!The rotten cherry on the spoiled cake:since one year you have to pay if you want to print a label!!!So, not only they stopped giving the QR Code that busy, old, sick and disabled people can use, but they leave them only with a paid option!!!And when you ask why they say "it's to discourage printing, as it's environmentally unfriendly"DID THEY EVER GO TO THE POST WITH A QR CODE!??!WHEN YOU USE THE QR CODE YOU STILL NEED TO GET THE F. LABEL PRINTED!!!! IT'S EVEN WORSE BECAUSE IT'S A MORE POLLUTING GLOSSY PAPER WITH FULL GLUE ON THE BACK SIDE!!!!IT'S WORSE FOR THE ENVIRONMENT AND IT IS A F. NIGHTMARE FOR THE CUSTOMERS AND THE POST THAT FOR EVERY PACKET MUST PRINT THREE LABELS SLOWLY WHILE ALL CUSTOMERS WAIT IN THE CUE!!!!!!!!THIS IS THE MOST IDIOTIC CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE IN THE WORLD!!!AS I SAID AT THE BEGINNING; AMAZON IS THE ONE SINGLE MOST DISFUNCTIONAL ONLINE SHOP ON EARTH!!!!

Amazon.esMy issue was finally resolved, but only by fluke and my own extreme persistence, certainly not because of any actual help from Amazon’s support teams. Since Saturday the 9th, I have been trapped in a truly Kafkaesque nightmare of loops and red tape handled with total incompetence.​The "Service" I received:​KYC Verification Loop: I was stuck in a constant cycle of errors despite providing all requested documentation. The system repeatedly failed to recognise valid information.​Restricted Vouchers: This error left me unable to use my gift vouchers to make a purchase with a third-party Marketplace seller. I had the funds, but Amazon’s broken process prevented me from using them.​Ignored Escalations: I sent messages to multiple different email addresses to escalate this as a formal complaint. I have yet to receive a single human reply to any of them.​Automated Walls: Every interaction was met with automated template responses. It was clear no one was actually reading my messages or listening to the specific problem.​The Social Media Runaround: I reached out to Instagram and Facebook. The agents there did nothing but push me back to the same broken phone lines that caused the issue in the first place.At one point, the social media team even provided a phone number that was "invalid" and couldn't even be dialled.​Dropped Connections: I lost count of how many times I was cut off mid-call or had a chat window abruptly ended by an agent without a resolution.​It is appalling that a multi-billion dollar company has zero "ownership" of customer problems. I had to fight for days just to be allowed to spend my own money. If you have a complex issue, don't expect a human to help you, expect to be ignored until the system eventually glitches in your favor. Truly the most atrocious service I have ever experienced.

review_title: I ordered earbuds on May 6th for…

"We’re sorry for the delay. Due to an unexpected issue, your package hasn’t shipped yet. We know you expected your package today and that this may be frustrating. We appreciate your patience. We’ll update you once it’s on the way, and you won’t be charged until your package ships. If you prefer not to wait, you can request a refund."I called customer service to get a delivery date and they couldn't give me one. I then asked to have it cancelled so I could reorder. review_title: Amazon and the "2026 Madness": How a NASA-level giant can delete your life in one click.
review_text: Amazon and the "2026 Madness": How a NASA-level giant can delete your life in one click.If you think you're a protected customer of a modern company, think again. You are just a number in a database that can be pulverized by a simple mistake.The Fact: I made the error of trying to close an old, unused Amazon.com (USA) account while keeping my Amazon.it (Italy) one. In 2026, you’d expect a company that ships packages before you even order them to know the difference between America and Italy. Nope. One click and, without any serious warning, the system triggered a global self-destruction protocol. The result? Over ten years of history on Amazon.it deleted. Orders, history, and warranties: all gone into thin air.The "NASA" Technology:What infuriates me is the stone wall of customer support. We are talking about people who manage NASA’s servers, who plan to colonize Mars, and who boast about a "space-age" AI... yet, when faced with a human error, they behave like a broken intercom.I was answered by cold calculators (human and not) only capable of repeating: "The system cannot go back." It’s ridiculous. They know what I’ll dream about tonight, they know what brand of socks I’ll buy in two years, but they don't know how to find the "Undo" button to fix a wrong click? You are silicon giants, but you lack the common sense of a child—and above all, you lack a heart.The Kidnapping of Rights:The final insult is that by deleting the account, Amazon has turned into a medieval tyrant. I have blocked returns and, more seriously, legal warranties on expensive items that have now become wastepaper because, for their "Sacred Algorithm," I no longer exist. It is a full-blown digital kidnapping managed with the sensitivity of a broken refrigerator.Conclusion:After ten years of lining your pockets, I discovered that to you, I am just a bit. If the bit stumbles, you delete it and say goodbye. It’s almost poetic: NASA will take man to the stars, but Amazon still hasn't figured out how to help a human being who got locked out of their house because of a distraction. Congratulations on your "Customer Obsession": you are so obsessed with the customer that as soon as they have a problem, you make them vanish from the database so they don't bother you.Not recommended for anyone who thinks they are dealing with human beings.

I was informed that for the inconvenience I would receive a full refund as compensation and told to keep the dash cam when I eventually receive a collection code and can collect it from the locker. I have not received that compensation. A few days later and after further chats with Amazon I received notification of a full refund for non delivery of the dash cam. I have now had chats with three Amazon staff and telephone conversations with two others. They informed me that as I have received a refund for the non delivery, Amazon will not pay the original delay compensation. review_title: Horrible packing results in damage in many deliveries!
review_text: First, UPS delivery is great here. The drivers always ring the doorbell when delivering. Amazon itself is another story. Amazon sends fragile items and crushable items in paper envelopes! Nearly half of the deliveries we receive are damaged due to pathetic packing. I have reported it numerous times. Amazon doesn’t care! They will send crackers in an envelope! Today they shipped two $15  boxed containers of plant fertilizer in an envelope! Both were crushed!Another thing Amazon ignores is fraud. e.g. garden seeds - seed sellers regularly advertise supposed hybrid seeds by name when they are really seeds from hybrids they collected.  Seeds from which can’t legally be called by the copyrighted and patented name. The resulting produce is usually not even close to the advertised seed name! I have reported this numerous times and Amazon ignores it. e.g. Carnival squash and Sweet Success cucumbers. It is the same with a lot of fraudulent software sold on Amazon.

review_title: Amazon allows misleading publicity on its plataform
review_text: I bought a product on Amazon that said it had free returns. When I received it and saw it wasn’t what I needed, I started a return, but they were deducting $15 from what I paid for shipping. I contacted the seller and told them that was misleading advertising, and they told me not to worry—that once I returned the product, I would get a full refund. I did that, but they only refunded what I paid, deducting the $15 return shipping fee.When I contacted the seller again to complain, they told me they could no longer refund the $15 because Amazon didn’t allow it. So I called Amazon, and they told me they would apply the guarantee and that I would definitely get my money back. But I just received an email saying they will not refund me because it wasn’t the fault of either the customer or Amazon.And of course it was their fault—both the seller, for using misleading advertising by claiming “free returns” when that’s not true, and Amazon, for allowing third-party sellers to use their platform to mislead customers. Amazon is complicit. I will no longer shop on Amazon, and I suggest others do the same, because even AI is saying that Amazon is trying to cut costs on customer service and making the process more difficult so people get discouraged from returning products.

review_title: Amazon’s "Premium" Delivery is a Transparent Scam
review_text: Don’t be fooled by the "Premium Delivery" option. It is a calculated, misleading omission designed to bully you into an Amazon Prime subscription.​I attempted to purchase studio headphones and a piano bench. The site proudly displayed "Next Day" delivery for Prime members for the Headphones. I opted for the "Premium Delivery" at a steep £5.99 (nearly the cost of a full month's subscription).Amazon deliberately obscured the actual delivery date per-item for this "Premium" service until AFTER I had hit the "Buy Now" button and paid.​Only then did they reveal that BOTH items wouldn't arrive for 48 hours, despite the headphones clearly being available for Monday delivery (with Prime).When I challenged this on online chat, I was met with a wall of corporate nonsense. An advisor named Jayashree flat-out lied, claiming my parcel would be "automatically upgraded," if possible but refused twice to guarantee it. A claim the Manager (Mohammed) later said was impossible.​When I asked for a formal complaint reference or a transcript of these dishonest promises from the Manager, Mohammed? Refused.​This is "sharp practice" at its finest. They hide the dates to pressure you into "just in case" Prime subs and hope you won't bother to cancel once you've invested the time. I’ve cancelled my entire order on principle and reported them to CAB Consumer Advice (Trading Standards).​Amazon used to be about convenience; now they are about deceptive workflows and dishonest customer service. I'm voting with my feet and heading back to the High Street.And - It gets worse. After requesting an escalation above the Manager (Mohammed) due to his refusal to provide a complaint reference, Emailed transcript or actually do anything to resolve the issue, I received an "escalated" response.The person who replied to the escalation? Mohammed himself. Amazon is literally "marking its own homework." The response solely served to repeat what Mohammed had already said and was in no way a genuine attempt to resolve the issue.When you ask for a higher authority to review a manager's failure, they just route the email back to the same manager to dismiss it. This isn't a customer service department; it's an echo chamber designed to silence legitimate grievances.If you think "Management" will help you here, think again—they are too busy covering their own tracks.

review_title: Long-Delayed Order Canceled Without Payment Retry Opportunity
review_text: I am writing to share a disappointing experience with Amazon regarding a Black Friday order.I placed an order during Black Friday with an estimated delivery date in June and patiently waited for several months. However, I later discovered that the order had been canceled in April due to a declined payment method.What makes this situation particularly frustrating is that the original payment was successfully authorized via my Visa card at the time of purchase. Due to the long delay before Amazon attempted to capture the payment, a re-authorization was required. Unfortunately, I was never notified or given the opportunity to update or confirm my payment details.Had I been informed earlier, I would have purchased the item from another retailer that was offering a slightly higher price at the time. Now, that price is no longer available, and I have effectively lost the opportunity entirely.When I contacted customer support, I was simply told that the order could not be reinstated, without any meaningful attempt to resolve the issue or offer compensation.This experience reflects poorly on how Amazon handles long-term orders and payment re-authorizations. At the very least, customers should be given a fair chance to resolve payment issues—especially after waiting for months.I hope Amazon takes this feedback seriously and improves its communication and customer support processes in similar situat

review_title: Amazon customer service is designed to wear you out. They train agents to stall until your rights expire.
review_text: I purchased a defective keyboard through Amazon from a third party seller called LowFree. The battery was dead on arrival. I contacted Amazon customer service multiple times to resolve a simple return and refund situation. What followed was one of the most frustrating experiences I have ever had with any company.Agents repeatedly hung up on me. Every time I called back I had to start from zero and explain everything again. I was bounced between Amazon and the seller with each one pointing at the other. When I specifically requested an A-to-Z Guarantee claim — which is my right as a buyer on their platform — agents told me they did not have that option. That is either a lie or a training failure. Either way it is unacceptable.The return label Amazon provided came with a $65 shipping fee at DHL for a defective product that was never functional to begin with. I should not be paying a single dollar to return something that arrived broken. When I raised this, agents acknowledged the issue and said it would be reimbursed but refused to put anything in writing or escalate to a manager despite multiple requests.Amazon is the biggest marketplace in the world. They have a responsibility to protect buyers not just sellers. Their customer service model is built to exhaust you until you give up. Do not give up. File the A-to-Z Guarantee claim, dispute the charge with your bank, and document everything from the first contact.I will be updating this review with the final outcome.

Amazon usually has top notch support and is absolutely amazing with everything, so it's unfortunate that I have to write this review about some changes to their customer service and/or policies surrounding returns.The other day I ordered a RODE gen 3 lapel wireless mic system because my Webcam doesn't have a mic in it, and I use my MacBook Pro in clamshell mode now for ergonomic reasons, and so the internal mics can't be used anymore.I later realized that the Rode's AI would likely not be suitable for this, and I found much better options for both Zoom and for recording desktop videos.One of Amazon's extremely annoying quirks is that anytime ANYTHING has proceeded to the shipping process even if it's just been marked to be shipped, the ability to cancel the order is almost impossible.And as it stands the order is set to arrive tomorrow. It's been shipped already, cancellation is a guaranteed fail at this point.I started a chat, and told the agent I wanted to return the item. The agent then said he was going to try to cancel the item. I didn't really question it, even though it is NOT what I asked him to do, because I figured that if he was trying to cancel it, he had confidence it would succeed.He said he raised a request to cancel it, which means it's the same thing that happens when you try to cancel it yourself.So I told him I don't want it cancelled, I never asked for that, I want to open a return so when it gets here I can just take it to the UPS store.The guy must have been awful with English or completely insubordinate because he kept on telling me he was going to try to get the order cancelled, even after I told him over and over again I wanted to return it.I ended the chat and started a new chat. Then this person said they would return it, but then said they needed to transfer me to a "concerns rep" .. I told that rep I wanted it returned and I know it hasn't arrived yet but that it was NEVER an issue in the past to open a return before the item is delivered and then just take the package to UPS store to drop it off.I told him this TWICE. Despite this, the agent STILL replied with:We cannot open a return for an item that has not yet been marked delivered, even though they did this in the past.I am fed up with companies lying like this and ignoring direct truths stated by customers and that's why I am leaving this scathing review.I spent 1 hour and a half in chat with them , and NOW I have do it AGAIN!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! After it's delivered and I am IRATE.

I have been an Amazon customer since the late '90s, when they were literally an online book and media store. I've watched them grow and grow, and have been so proud of this corporation. Amazon became a way of life. I’m sure many folks can relate to that. Of course, there were other companies that I bought a few specialty items from, but Amazon was my default go-to for nearly everything.But that just changed, as I made the switch to eBay and Etsy. Many of us have felt the frustration of paying for Prime membership and watching the service decline. Deliveries took a week to Montana, Prime or not. But, I still liked Amazon. And, I forgave them for the times they sent me used (and sometimes dirty) products, which were supposed to be new. There were problems that needed to be addressed, but I remained faithful to, and pleased with, this one-stop shop.What I’m about to share with you is the reason I pulled the plug on Amazon for good, ending a relationship of almost 30 years. Amazon did something that broke my trust, and it left me questioning their integrity.I have written Amazon product reviews as far back as I can remember, under all three Amazon accounts that I had over the years (business, married, single). I am not a Vine member, so these reviews were for products that I purchased with my own money. I am a busy person who works long hours, yet I spent considerable time writing quality reviews. Others’ reviews have often helped me so much, and I wanted to contribute in the same way. I got nothing out of this monetarily; it was merely a labor of love.I strove to write well, and to represent each item honestly and fairly. Many of my reviews were glowing; a smaller percentage were not. A few items were really lousy, and I shared this fact. This helps not only potential purchasers, but sellers, as well, because the feedback urges them to improve the quality of their inventory.On April 21, 2026, I received an email from Amazon that stated (quote): "You have repeatedly posted content that violates our Community Guidelines... Please note that your ability to participate in Community features has been removed."Amazon deleted ALL of my reviews, literally YEARS of reviews. A block was placed on my account, so that I could no longer write a review of any product. I was still free to spend my money with Amazon, but I was completely banished from all “community features.”I have always held myself to high standards, and my writing reflects this. I am mindful to be respectful, and careful to follow guidelines. I cannot fathom what I did wrong. I contacted Amazon by email FOUR TIMES regarding this matter. I received NO REPLY WHATSOEVER.I spent an evening doing some research on the internet, seeking answers. I discovered that I'm not alone; Amazon has done this to countless people. Apparently, sellers can get reviewers banned by "reporting" them. A few Reddit members talked about discussion boards they'd been on, where sellers were urging other sellers to get a bad review deleted by "reporting" it. Then that same seller, if malicious, could retaliate by clicking on that reviewer's page and reporting several of his/her reviews, thereby getting him/her wiped from ever writing reviews again.Is this what's happening on Amazon? I cannot say. I wish I understood more. At this point, it’s mere speculation, but some people are suspicious that it is indeed the case. And, this happened to me right after I wrote a negative review of a pricey product that had been a disappointment.To treat faithful customers like this, who have long-standing trustworthy reputations— and suddenly accuse them of inappropriate conduct, and then take punitive action against them, seemingly without investigation and with no explanation whatsoever— feels criminal. And then ghosting them, when they request answers, is unforgivable. It’s appalling. When you accuse somebody of something, and you take action against them, you better have some facts and be able to state your case.Update: I closed my Amazon account for good, and I followed the suggestion of taking my business to eBay. Though determined that I was DONE with Amazon, I expected to miss it, a lot. This was a company that I spent literally thousands of dollars on over the years. It was that much a part of my life. Interestingly, I haven’t missed it at all. I’m genuinely surprised at this. I’ve been able to get everything on eBay that I previously purchased on Amazon, and I can usually find a seller who offers free shipping. And, I’m more conscientious now. I don’t impulsively buy as much. On Amazon, there were so many suggestions, offered in a media-style feed-format. eBay does much less of that. So, I’m actually grateful this happened. It may prove to be life-changing, in a good way.

I was really excited to join the Vine Program about a year ago. The first item I reviewed was a dog fence; a $50 value and a high quality item. Things got disappointing after that. For reference, the majority of my purchases are made on Amazon and I’ve been a Prime Member for several years. My purchases have consisted of items from nearly every category. I was disappointed and puzzled to find the “items for you” consistently contain no more than 10 items that have zero to do with any of my past purchases. Same thing with the “items for all” category. Unfortunately, there is no questionnaire or any other way for reviewers to state their interests. Even more frustrating and puzzling is the fact I regularly see items that would pertain to my past purchases being reviewed by other Vine reviewers. When I called Amazon to get some clarity, I was told to check items regularly as new items are added everyday. I’ve done this over several weeks and still experience this problem. I’ve found the majority of items I’ve been offered are replacement parts for various items although, to be fair, I have found a few useful inexpensive items but they are nothing I really needed or would have ever ordered.The program is free to join and there’s no charge for shipping. I would recommend becoming familiar with the rules and guidelines to avoid any possible charges. As long as you do this, I’d say it can’t hurt to give the program a try and hopefully you’ll have a better experience than I’ve had.

review_title: Misleading agents, ignored policies, and a complete failure of customer service
review_text: My recent experience with Amazon UK has been deeply frustrating. What started as a simple missed guaranteed delivery date escalated into a stressful ordeal involving misleading information, ignored policies, and being passed between multiple agents and senior management like a parcel.I placed two separate orders: one on 14 April 2026 for a mouse with free delivery, and another on 16 April 2026 for a face mask, for which I paid a £1.99 delivery charge to guarantee delivery on 16 April. Without any notification, Amazon consolidated the mouse into the mask shipment. The guaranteed delivery date was missed, and both items arrived a day late on 17 April.When I contacted customer service on 17 April, the agent I spoke with provided information that was completely inaccurate. He twice claimed the items would arrive between 9:45 AM and 11:45 AM, and explicitly instructed me to ignore Amazon's own tracking page. This conduct was not only unhelpful but potentially constitutes a misleading action under Regulation 5 of the Consumer Protection from Unfair Trading Regulations 2008.When I escalated the matter to the customer service manager, he initially denied that any delivery charge had been taken, claiming delivery was free. It was only after I produced the invoice proving I had paid the £1.99 charge that he acknowledged it.I then had to escalate the complaint through three further levels of management: a Senior Manager, a member of the Management team, and finally the Executive Relations team. Throughout this process, I received generic responses that did not address the core issues. Amazon breached its own Guaranteed Delivery Policy, its Late Delivery Policy, and its Fulfilment Policy by failing to notify me of the order consolidation.Furthermore, under Regulation 42 of the Consumer Contracts (Information, Cancellation and Additional Charges) Regulations 2013, I was legally entitled to a full refund of the delivery charge for the missed guarantee. Under Sections 49 and 54 of the Consumer Rights Act 2015, services must be performed with reasonable care and skill, and where they are not, the consumer is entitled to an appropriate price reduction.Amazon eventually provided the £1.99 delivery refund, an £8.99 goodwill credit applied by the first agent, and a one month refund on my Prime membership. I acknowledge this financial resolution, but it does not excuse the significant time and effort it took to reach it. The lack of proactive communication, the provision of false information by staff, and the exhausting process of dealing with multiple tiers of management who did not properly engage with my complaint have significantly eroded my trust in the company.Amazon should review its customer service standards and ensure its agents provide accurate information rather than telling customers what they may want to hear.

review_title: Amazon Knew My Account Was Suspicious and Kept Taking Payments
review_text: I’m extremely concerned about how Amazon has handled my account security.I was recently notified of “suspicious activity” on my account and had an order cancelled. After logging in and securing my account, I’ve now been told this issue actually dates back to January — yet I was never notified.What’s most alarming:1. Despite identifying “suspicious activity”, Amazon left my account active with my payment details stored2. Amazon continued charging my account for subscriptions, including Prime and Paramount, during this period3. My recent order was cancelled, but I still have pending charges in my bank account4. Customer Service has been unable to assist and advised I must wait 24–48 hours for an email responseTo be clear, Amazon flagged my account as potentially compromised months ago, did not inform me, continued billing my card, and is only now taking action when I attempt to place an order.This leaves me in a position where I don’t know:1. If my card details have been compromised2. Whether I need to cancel my card immediately3. When (or if) I will be refunded for cancelled ordersFor a company of this size, this raises serious concerns about how account security is managed. Users should be aware that even when suspicious activity is detected, accounts may remain active, payment details retained, and charges continue without notification.This is not just frustrating, it’s a genuine security concern.

review_text: Unfortunately, I would not even give Amazon 1 star after the recent experience I have had.I’m a loyal Amazon customer, and frequently order for the website. In October 2025, I purchased a car radio for my Fiat 500. Once received, I installed this into my car. However, after 1 day of use the radio was faulty and overall, it was poor quality. As a result, I returned this unwanted item within the required timeframe and was refunded £119.99.In December 2025, I was shocked to see the charge of £119.99 withdraw from my bank again. I contacted customer service multiple times to request a refund. At first, I was toldno return was received as “I did not send the radio back” I have to pay the full amount.After exhausting the use of their customer service, I had no choice but to contact my credit card provider for help, as there was no way I would pay for an item I do not have and returned. I submitted all evidence of my return, pictures of the radio received when it was installed in my car and when it was removed and packaged. I don’t know how I could have made this any clearer.Amazon responded to this dispute, now claiming a return was received, but not the correct item. If the wrong item was returned, then the wrong radio was sent from Amazon in the first place - to which they are denying this fact.It has been 6 months and Amazon are standing by their opinion that I should be charged for this item. It is absolutely shocking that a customer is being charged for a returned item. My family and I would have spent more than this on Amazon within that time frame, however we are cancelling all subscriptions and never buying from Amazon again.So disappointed in this service.

review_title: Data requirements for Alexa enabled products
review_text: Dear Sir/Madam,I write to you in a formal capacity to seek clear and unequivocal clarification regarding the operational data practices of your Alexa-enabled devices, specifically in relation to the collection, storage, and potential monitoring of in-home audio.As a provider of consumer technology marketed explicitly for use within private domestic environments, Amazon bears a heightened duty of care and transparency to its customers. Devices such as Alexa are positioned not merely as optional gadgets, but as integrated household systems—frequently operating in close proximity to families, including vulnerable individuals. As such, any ambiguity surrounding their data capture capabilities raises serious concerns.Accordingly, I require precise answers to the following:Whether Alexa-enabled devices are continuously listening to ambient conversations, irrespective of wake-word activation.What technical safeguards are in place to ensure that only intended voice interactions are processed or recorded.Whether any audio data is stored, reviewed, or accessed by Amazon personnel or third parties, and under what legal or operational framework.The duration for which any such data is retained and the mechanisms available to consumers for its deletion.Whether any data collected is used for purposes beyond direct service provision, including but not limited to profiling, targeted advertising, or machine learning training.I would also remind you that under applicable UK data protection legislation, including the UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018, customers are entitled to full transparency regarding how their personal data—particularly sensitive in-home audio—is processed. Any failure to adequately disclose such practices may constitute a breach of these statutory obligations.Given the seriousness of this matter, I expect a detailed and comprehensive response within 14 days of receipt of this correspondence. Should your response fail to sufficiently address these concerns, I reserve the right to escalate this matter to the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) and to seek independent legal advice.This is not a speculative enquiry but a legitimate request grounded in consumer rights, privacy expectations, and regulatory compliance. I trust Amazon will treat it with the seriousness it warrants.Yours faithfully,

review_title: ZXHQ 2026 Diary A5 Page a Day
review_text: ZXHQ 2026 Diary A5 Page a Day, Jan-Dec.Regrettably, I find myself with a life fuller than I'd ever imagined—though not in the way we dream about. My calendar tells a story I never wanted to write, filled with medical appointments and clinical trials for cancer treatments that consume my days.You see, a diary demands what I can no longer easily give. It requires two hands when one is often occupied by my phone, and even a simple spiral-bound book has become a challenge I'd rather avoid. Stomach cancer brings its own unwelcome schedule of frequent bathroom visits, and inevitably, someone will call right when I'm least prepared to answer.My germaphobic son certainly won't be offering assistance, in the bathroom! I crave whatever privacy remains as my health continues its unfortunate decline. As much as I love the idea of a proper diary—that nostalgic companion of generations past—the simple act of turning pages and writing has become surprisingly difficult with hands that no longer cooperate as they once did.What I actually need is modest: just the date, time, location, and a few brief notes. My medical team knows what they're doing; detailed records aren't necessary when professionals are guiding my care. A wall calendar serves my purpose far better than an elaborate diary with all those extras—day-per-page layouts, religious information, expense trackers, habit monitors, inspirational quotes, maps, and time zones. They're lovely touches, truly, but excessive for what I require.Yes, a portable diary would be wonderful in the hospital where medical staff could add their own notes, but the thought of losing it and needing a duplicate troubles me deeply.So I hope to gift this beautiful diary to someone who will treasure it as it deserves. Word travels quickly about such wonderful presents, especially when they arrive with a matching pen—sea green with sea green, purple with purple. A spiral-bound option would have been ideal, but what I really covet is a dictaphone.For now, I lumber back from my throne to the coffee table whenever the phone rings, the zimmer frame clanking loudly, hoping it's not another nuisance caller. Anyone who's survived this particular journey uses far different describing words for that awkward shuffle than I can print here.

They refused to give me the names of the people involved, and told me there was nothing they could do about the theft. They said I should call my credit card company. I later found out that Amazon had been hacked, and that the Kindle scam was widespread.  Warning: Do NOT save your payment method on Amazon. If you already have, delete that information immediately.

review_title: I am extremely dissatisfied with the…
review_text: I am extremely dissatisfied with the customer service I received regarding a missing order.My order was split into multiple shipments, and I successfully received part of it on April 8 using the same address. The remaining package has not been delivered.The tracking information is inaccurate and misleading. It states that the delivery address issue was corrected in Christiansted, VI, however USPS has confirmed they do not have the package and the last actual scan was in Denver on April 3 with no updates since.During a call on Friday, I spoke with an agent (name unknown) who told me—after checking—that my package would be delivered the next day. Based on that assurance, no replacement or further action was taken at that time.This information was incorrect.Had the correct information been provided during that call, a replacement order with expedited shipping could have been processed immediately, and I likely would have received the items in time for my daughter’s birthday.Instead, I was advised to wait, which resulted in lost time on a time-sensitive order.When I followed up, I was only offered a refund. I was then advised to sign up for Amazon Prime in order to receive faster shipping on a replacement, despite there being no guarantee that the item would arrive in time. This is not an acceptable solution, especially considering the issue did not originate on my end.While I appreciate that the refund was processed by a later agent, the overall experience reflects poor handling, inaccurate information, and lack of accountability.This experience involved:- Conflicting and inaccurate tracking updates- Misinformation from a customer service agent- Loss of valuable time due to incorrect guidance- No proactive resolution when the issue was first reportedDue to this experience, I have lost confidence in relying on Amazon for time-sensitive purchases.I request that this matter be reviewed, including the recorded call from Friday, and that steps are taken to ensure customers are given accurate and reliable information in the future.

Buying via Amazon Marketplace – A Lesson Learned the Hard WayI want to share my recent experience as a cautionary note for anyone purchasing from third-party sellers on Amazon Marketplace.Like many people, I’ve come to rely on Amazon for its convenience and generally positive track record. However, this experience highlighted the importance of taking extra time to research who you are actually buying from, especially when it comes to Marketplace sellers and their chosen shipping methods.I placed an order through Amazon Marketplace with TradeINN (Spain), but unfortunately, the item never arrived. The courier company used, GLS, proved extremely difficult to deal with. The tracking number provided did not work, there was no clear communication, no successful delivery attempts were made, and no alternative delivery date was offered. The situation essentially became a “dead end” with no resolution.Communication from the seller consisted mostly of automated, generic responses that did not address the issue or provide a meaningful solution. At the same time, Amazon customer service repeatedly asked for patience and extended waiting periods, which only added to the frustration.What surprised me most was that the Amazon A-to-Z Guarantee did not step in as expected. While it is designed to protect customers in cases of non-delivery or issues with third-party sellers, in my case it did not provide the support I anticipated, leaving me feeling let down by both the seller and Amazon.In the end, I had no choice but to contact my credit card provider, who resolved the issue promptly through a chargeback.To be clear, this is not a criticism of Amazon as a whole—I continue to use it regularly and appreciate its convenience. This was my first negative experience. However, it has been an important reminder that not all Marketplace sellers operate to the same standards.My advice to others:•	Take time to research third-party sellers before purchasing•	Read reviews on independent platforms like Trustpilot, not just on Amazon•	Pay attention to the shipping method and courier company usedA little extra research upfront can help avoid a very frustrating experience later.Lesson Learned

review_title: a DeLonghi Magnifica Next coffee machine not fit for purpose
review_text: In mid-February I purchased a DeLonghi Magnifica Next coffee machine from Amazon.  My trustworthy Nespresso coffee machines were both  on the blink and it was time to invest in a new machine.  My husband seemed to be favouring a bean to cup and although sceptical from my past experience I decided to go along with it.  At a cost of £500 my expectations were high.  The benefits were all thereAutomatic grindingHigh on my list was the integrated Crème latte/cappuccino frothing system,A great variety of coffee options at the touch  of the screen,The ability to save your personal favourites,Automatically filtered water, andReminders to carry out maintenance, refill water canister and empty coffee grounds.All in one compact, space saving machine.and, most importantly, Backed up by Amazon.  What could possibly go wrong.  Obviously no-one wants to buy a product and then find out it is faulty.Being able to produce a coffee to visitors personal tastes whilst at the same time producing coffee to our individual requirements, a bonus.My machine was delivered efficiently and as it seemed, in good working order, to my excitement,  and I was anxious to experiment the different coffee options.However, as with most things these days, there was no user manual.Looking forward to my cappuccino I set the machine  and pressed the screen setting.  The result - total disappointment.  My instant reaction was that I had obviously made an error but not to worry I will continue to practice.After a few weeks I came to the decision that  I was maybe doing something wrong and I would just keep practicing until I eventually got it right.  In the meantime I decided to top it up with my Nespresso milk frother.  Eventually I consulted my neighbour who had recommended it, only to be told she had sent her machine back because the frother wasn’t  working!  Alarm bells started ringing - I contacted Amazon as they had sold me the machine!  My machine is approximately 5/6 weeks old.I contacted Amazon chat to ask about returning it as it was not fit for purpose.  Explained problem to agent and explained could not carry to Royal Mail having arthritis in my hands.  The agent transferred me to a second agent to arrange “pick up“.At this  point the chat started to  deteriorate.   In all I was transferred  automatically and without any explanation whatever to 8 different agents, having to repeat myself and hold on until they checked or consulted someone else,  each one giving me differing information,   eventually ok being told I had to take it to Royal  Mail myself and I had to return it to the manufacturer  for “REPAIR”.  Really?!!  I   explained  each time it was sold not fit for purpose and I could not carry it and each time I tripped through to a different agent.  Amazon were not prepared to take any responsibility for product being sold by them not fit for purpose.  If I had purchased elsewhere and not fit for purpose,  it would have been exchanged.  I told them  my contract of purchase was with them and not the manufacturer.  They continued to insist the return period had been exceeded.  Eventually, the last agent cut me off!  Fantastic customer service.I have always had good service in the past and the customer service assistants have always been very helpful and considerate until now.  The first assistant was really helpful and when I explained she said she would pass me through to someone who would arrange collection.    Of course that didn’t happen.I then, in total exasperation, returned to my original order to check the detail, only to find that the information  for return seemed to have been  changed whilst I was on chat to show alternative as a repair.  Apparently, even though product was delivered faulty Amazon will not take any responsibility as it is out of their return period and the only alternative  is the repair o f a brand new just under £500 (expensive) coffee machine.  I was appalled.  Even more so when my neighbour told me how much trouble she had had trying to get the manufacturer to repair hers and that she had had exactly the same problem-frother not working.I use Amazon a lot for my purchases, and have done for some years, paying for prime delivery for obvious reasons.    However, I will certainly think twice about buying any  more expensive items in the future.My advice to Amazon, train your staff to give correct and consistent advice to customers,  whilst not passing them around from pillow to post,  having to repeat the same information over and over again when it’s already there to read. Nor should they  cut customers off - really bad customer service.As no manual is provided and the machine was delivered faulty I believe Amazon was under obligation to exchange it.  Who is to say that this is not a product which was returned by another customer as has happened in the past.

review_title: Amazon Doesn’t Care About the Working…
review_text: Amazon Doesn’t Care About the Working Man — And the Clock Is Ticking on People Like JoshPosted April 6, 2026I’ll be honest upfront: I missed the return window. I know. I owned it. I apologized. But what followed revealed exactly who Amazon is when policy is on their side and you have nothing left to leverage.The item — a CyberPower UPS, still sealed, never opened, never touched — sat in original packaging because life buried me. My grandfather passed away. My mother was diagnosed with cancer. I was fighting depression, drowning in school deadlines, managing financial pressure, and my car was dead in the driveway with a failed ABS brake module I couldn’t afford to fix. I wasn’t being careless. I was surviving.I asked for anything reasonable — store credit, account credit, a goodwill exception, something. I wasn’t demanding. I was asking a billion-dollar company to act human for sixty seconds.Josh, the supervisor I was escalated to, said no. Every way I asked. No creativity. No empathy. No attempt to actually help. Just policy repeated back like a shield.Here’s what Josh and people like him don’t seem to realize yet: the same technology that’s reshaping every other industry is coming for customer service management too. The idea that a supervisor role — one that amounts to reading policy and saying no — is somehow untouchable? That era is ending. AI doesn’t get tired. It doesn’t clock out. And frankly, in this interaction, it would have shown more flexibility and human decency than Josh did.Amazon calls itself customer-obsessed. But customer obsession apparently expires with your return label. After that, you’re on your own — grieving, sick with worry about your mother, broke, and being told there’s nothing anyone can do by someone who made zero effort to find out if that’s actually true.This wasn’t about the money. It was about being treated like a person. Amazon failed. Completely.

review_title: This complaint is about Amazon Prime service
review_text: This complaint is about Amazon Prime orders and how they resolve shipping issues. The references to USPS are part of the problem - however Amazon's handling is why this review is here.For reference - this has occurred on more than one occasion. I have an Amazon Prime account which I pay a yearly fee to be a part of.  Smart shoppers can place an order and take advantage of free shipping (and in some cases 1-2 day free shipping. To insure you benefit from this, its important to make sure the listing states "Prime" shipping in addition to placing your order in a timely manner to insure this happens.I placed an order that promised 2 day shipping (arriving today). When I placed the order, I confirmed that the seller offered prime shipping and item was in stock.Selecting the prime shipping option -  I am not given a choice of which shipper to use, and since the shipping is "free", I have  no remedy other than calling Amazon and hoping for the best.   Sadly - In several other cases, Amazon has credited my account only to charge me months later for the item I may have had a shipping issue with never gets returned or the order isn't properly processed (i.e. Amazon says I don't need to return the item).--THAT SAID - Onward to my recent order and most recent subject of this review . My order was listed via USPS as out for delivery.  Later on the same day - USPS claims "Customer has requested the shipment to be held for collection. Please collect your package once available for pick-up." - to be clear - I DID NOT request any such hold from USPS.  With USPS closed there is NO way to reach them, AND my only remedy was to contact Amazon to resolve this. An let us not forget the 2 day prime delivery is now NOT going to occur.-- Calling Amazon and escalating required 4 calls with requests for a supervisor. On each call I needed to request a supervisor 2-3 times before reaching  a Supervisor. 3 supervisors later  I was given a full refund of my product and advised Amazon will handle returning the item without my intervention.For the record - The first 2 supervisors claimed I could not get a return processed until 4 additional days passed (hmm... 2 day delivery failure yet no resolution for twice that amount of time)NO CUSTOMER should EVER have to do this. Shame on you Amazon.Finally - although my issue has been "resolved" - I now have to watch my credit card statements with a magnifying glass to be sure I don't get charged for this in the future. - Uggh.

The laptop was returned and acknowledged on 13 March, yet as of 1 April 2026, I still have not received repayment of R20 899.Over several weeks, I’ve spoken with multiple agents — and have two active case numbers (D409704577 plus another opened 31 March). I’ve spent hours on hold, repeatedly told the issue would be “escalated,” yet nothing moves forward. review_title: It's time to stop using Amazon
review_text: I would give a negative rating if at all possible. There is no accountability for their customer service reps. There is no ability to escalate a problem when the overseas call center cannot provide a solution. On March 10th, my business account was locked, stating that my credit card company was disputing charges dating back to November of 2025, when neither my credit card company nor I had disputed any charges from Amazon. On this one day, Amazon incorrectly stated that I was disputing close to 100 charges when these items were paid for and well received by me.I now have over 20 hours of writing emails and speaking with ineffective call center Representatives trying to regain access to my account. I now have over 1,000 AI generated emails from Amazon. They have several different variations but the one I love best beginsThe card ending in 00 that you approved us to charge has not been added to your Amazon account.I'm dying to know how they were able to charge my card if it has not been added to my Amazon account.I have responded thoughtfully by email over a dozen times, hoping that at some point someone might read my communication, but in each instance, I receive either an AI-generated, incorrect response or a human response from someone whose IQ is in the single digits.The recurring proposed solution is that I update my credit cards in my account, even though I've been blocked and cannot access it.In addition to the significant amount of wasted time on my part, this has resulted in a major disruption to my business's workflow, as we have been placing orders with Amazon at least once a week, if not several times, and have had over 40 Subscribe & Save orders. We have now had to find new vendors for all of these products.I think it's time for everyone to consider dropping companies like Amazon that demonstrate no business integrity or respect for their customers.And let's face it, from a social standpoint, they're a horrible company who act very much from a bully mentality owned by a world-class narcissistic a..holeThere are many alternatives to Amazon that place a strong value on customer service and the relationship they have with their customers.Over the years, I have purchased over 1,500 ebooks from Amazon, and I'm now switching to Bookshop.org as I explore the many alternatives to Amazon, which has made great efforts to provide great customer service and act with integrity.Amazon has a Trustpilot rating of 1.7, and no company should be in business whose customers hold them in such horrible regard.I hope the countless individuals who have been consistently and repeatedly strongly dissatisfied with their Amazon experience will stop giving any business to Amazon in the future.

review_title: Customer Concerns Don’t Matter to Amazon
review_text: “I am a longtime Amazon customer who spends a substantial amount of money each year, and I am extremely frustrated with repeated issues in their delivery, return, and customer service processes. Packages frequently arrive days late, and in some cases are reported as delivered — including notifications from Alexa — before they actually arrive. Returned items that I have followed all instructions to send back are consistently marked as not received. Weeks later, I receive notifications that charges will be reapplied to my account, despite providing proof of the return.Customer service representatives routinely fail to listen to what I am explaining. They often repeat questions I’ve already answered or ask irrelevant questions, and it seems they are following a script rather than actually listening. It is painfully obvious that the training Amazon provides their representatives falls far short — their employees are neither empowered nor prepared to resolve recurring issues.Amazon’s quality control and quality assurance processes are clearly failing, as I continue to experience late deliveries, mishandled returns, and unhelpful customer service. These systemic failures create unnecessary stress and inconvenience for customers who follow all proper procedures, and give the strong impression that, although Amazon is both big and convenient, it seems they don’t feel the need to worry about losing customers.”

review_title: Formal Complaint, Request for Investigation, Compensation
review_text: Dear Amazon Customer Service,I am writing to raise a formal complaint regarding two serious delivery failures that occurred with my recent orders, as well as to request compensation for the inconvenience, delay, and stress caused.First, my parcel scheduled for delivery today was delivered to the wrong address and handed to an unknown individual. I have no information about who received my item, and I was not contacted at any point. This is completely unacceptable and raises concerns about the security and accuracy of your delivery process.Second, the delivery for my order yesterday was left outside my front door without any attempt to knock or notify me. I only became aware of the delivery because I received a notification stating that the item had been delivered. Leaving parcels unattended in this way poses a significant risk, as anyone could have taken the item.Although I have been informed that a redelivery will take place within the next 24 hours, this does not resolve the disruption caused. I was expecting my items today, and now I am forced to wait due to errors that were entirely avoidable. This is the second delivery failure in 24 hours, which is extremely concerning.I would like to highlight the following Amazon delivery policies, which have not been followed:- Deliveries must be made to the correct address. If a parcel is delivered to the wrong address, Amazon acknowledges this as a service error.- Parcels should not be handed to unknown individuals. Handing a parcel to someone who is not the customer, a household member, or an appropriate neighbour is not permitted.- Drivers are expected to attempt contact by knocking or ringing the doorbell. This did not happen.- Parcels should only be left in a safe place, and only when the customer has designated or approved it. My front door is not a secure location, and I did not authorise this.- Amazon remains responsible for the parcel until it is delivered correctly to the intended recipient.Given the repeated failures and the inconvenience caused, I am requesting appropriate compensation. While a redelivery has been arranged, it does not address the delay, the stress caused, or the fact that your delivery standards were not met on two consecutive occasions.I also request that this matter be investigated urgently and that steps are taken to prevent this from happening again.For escalation purposes, I am aware that Amazon’s Executive Customer Relations team can be contacted, and I will consider escalating this matter if necessary.I look forward to your prompt response and a satisfactory resolution.

review_title: Amazon allows repeated fraud by third-party sellers and fails to protect buyers
review_text: I purchased a Lenovo Yoga Pro 9i laptop on Amazon Marketplace for $2,427.70. The product page displayed "Visit the Lenovo Store" — I had no idea I was purchasing from a third party. The laptop arrived from a seller called Montana Joe operated by Techdeals in Brooklyn NY. Before setting it up I checked the Lenovo warranty using the serial number. The warranty had been activated 31 days before my purchase date proving it was pre-owned and fraudulently listed as new. Amazon customer service confirmed there was no fraud department to escalate to and that returning to the fraudulent seller was my only option. Multiple other buyers have reported identical fraud from this same seller. I had to file an FTC report and an IC3 complaint to get any traction. Amazon needs to improve third-party seller disclosure and fraud handling policies to protect buyers.

review_title: ⭐ Amazon.com: A Manipulative, Broken, Infuriating Disaster of a Shopping Platform
review_text: Amazon.com  has turned into one of the most dysfunctional, misleading, and user‑hostile shopping experiences on the internet. The search function is absolute garbage. You can type in the exact product name, model number, or even paste the manufacturer’s description, and Amazon still vomits out hundreds of irrelevant, random items that have nothing to do with what you asked for. It’s not a search engine anymore — it’s a slot machine.And then there’s Rufus, their so‑called “AI assistant.” Rufus is a complete embarrassment. It doesn’t refine searches, it doesn’t clarify anything, and it doesn’t help you find what you’re looking for. It just spits out more useless junk and wastes your time. Calling it “unhelpful” would be generous. It’s a full‑blown circus of wrong answers.The entire site has become overcomplicated, bloated, and intentionally confusing. Every page is cluttered with sponsored garbage, upsells, hidden variations, and misleading pricing. Simple tasks now take twice as many clicks. The interface feels like it was redesigned specifically to frustrate customers into giving up and buying whatever overpriced item Amazon shoves in front of them.And let’s be honest — the whole system feels engineered to trick people into paying more than they think they’re paying. Hidden shipping, sneaky add‑ons, buried fees, confusing listings, and constant attempts to redirect you to higher‑priced versions. It’s manipulative, exhausting, and completely unacceptable.Amazon.com  used to be simple, fast, and reliable. Now it’s a chaotic, dysfunctional mess run by algorithms that don’t work and “assistants” that don’t assist. The user experience is a disaster, the search function is worthless, and the platform behaves more like a trap than a marketplace.If you value your time, your sanity, or your wallet, be prepared — Amazon.com  has become a full‑blown nightmare.
