review_title: ​If the system allowed me to leave zero
review_text: ​If the system allowed me to leave zero stars for this absolute circus of a transaction, I would do it in a heartbeat. I am writing this review out of sheer disgust and unparalleled frustration with what is supposed to be a world-class logistics company. I recently placed an order for the brand-new OnePlus 15 through Amazon US. Knowing that international shipping to South Africa can sometimes be a painful process, I deliberately chose to pay an exorbitant, premium fee for your so-called "Priority Shipping." I needed this device urgently, and your checkout page explicitly guaranteed that if I handed over the extra cash, the phone would be delivered directly to my door in Phoenix by March 19, 2026.​That date was a complete and utter lie. March 19th came and went without a single sign of my extremely expensive flagship smartphone. Instead of delivering on the service I paid a premium for, Amazon sent me pathetic, automated excuses about an unforeseen delay. Because I am a reasonable person, I swallowed my anger and accepted the new, supposedly rock-solid promised delivery date of March 24th. I gave you the benefit of the doubt that a massive, multi-billion dollar corporation could figure out how to put a box on a plane and get it to Durban without a catastrophic meltdown.​Well, today is March 24th, and guess what? I am still sitting here completely empty-handed. There is no package, there is no phone, and there is absolutely no excuse for this level of sheer incompetence. You happily drained my bank account for an expedited shipping service that you clearly had absolutely no intention, or perhaps no capability, of actually fulfilling. Charging a customer a premium rate for a guaranteed delivery date and then completely blowing past multiple consecutive deadlines is not just bad business—it borders on outright theft and fraudulent advertising. It is a disgusting way to treat paying customers.

review_title: Amazon - Don't think your card details are safe with them,
review_text: Sat in my living room watching TV and all of a sudden Alexa has reset. Orange ring like straight out of the box factory - unusual?Little did I know at the time that this was my saving grace that alerted me to a much deeper issue trust me you want to hear this.I logged on to my computer and my amazon account was showing an Amazon Business logo.My account was somehow showing as an Amazon Business Account with no order history. My standard buyers account that I have been purchasing from for over a decade has suddenly changed to a business account.I asked a friend who also has an Amazon account (suspecting a system wide Amazon issue) and he said no his was fine. At this point I decided to telephone Amazon Customer services who confirmed yes that my account had indeed been changed to a business account.OK I said but I didn't do this so can you explain what's happened and how I get my account back up and running. At this point my girlfriend who's card is attached to the account came in as said quote 'They've taken all the money out of my bank account'. Looking at the transactions the operator told me that yes in fact 26 transactions of 200 euros have been processed in the last 10 minutes and that these separate transactions were for Amazon GIft Vouchers - this adding up to £4651.14 in GBP.This put us into overdraft to the point no more transactions were possible. The operator logged a case with the cyber security team and told us that I would get an email withing 24hours that would explain the process to get my account back up and running and refund the charges.In the meantime we reported this to both the Police and the Bank. 24hrs later - nothing from Amazon and after 3 sleepless nights wondering what was going off I called them and called them, each time the excuse was we will be receiving an email withing 24 hr.RIght - Amazon are OK when you are spending money with them but if there is an issue, they just don't care. I still haven't heard anything from them and my account has still not been reactivated.Luckily the Bank managed to claw the money back which was a relief after nearly 2 weeks of stress. Still nothing from Amazon though.In short even if there was a password breech I have MFA setup on my account so someone being able to log in to my account should not have been possible in the first place. So to sum up, my long standing standard buyers account was changed to a Spanish Business Account under an Italian company name and 26 transactions for Gift Vouchers were taken out all in the space of a couple of hours. The changing of the account from standard buyers to a business account somehow has a flaw in it that bypasses MFA completely!Word of warning folks, make sure you don't attach a card to your account that has much money in as the security checks at Amazon and the ability of third parties to access your account is real. Amazon do not treat fraud as a serious matter and I still haven't heard anything from them, this wasn't small change - this was  over 4 and a half thousand pounds.I used to have a prime membership but to be honest I've found ebay is just as cheap albeit delivery is a bit slower but i'm a lot happier not having an amazon account anymore and they can stick it as far as i'm concerned because the lack of action and urgency they placed on a serious fraud case was criminal in itself.

My money is sitting in a system I can't access.Amazon operates multiple regional storefronts under the same brand, yet gift card balances are not transferable between them - and this is not disclosed at the time of purchase. Otherwise, your money may end up locked in a marketplace you don't use.

However, over the past week, my deliveries have been consistently delayed; I suspect this may be related to my recent requests for refunds on all the delayed items. During this period, I've noticed strange behavior on the website, such as slow loading pages and incomplete checkout processes. Despite these issues, I decided to give Amazon another chance, thinking everyone has bad days. review_title: Amazon ingores warranty obligations as a seller.
review_text: A KRK Subwoofer I bought from Amazon stopped working entirely when it was under 3 years from the purchase date, which would be eligible for warranty in the US. Now it turned out that the manufacturer (Gibson) doesn't handle warranty outside of US at all - it would have been a responsibility of a local distributor to cover for the warranty, but Amazon (as Amazon was the direct seller) absolutely refused disclosing any information on where they got the item. This way Amazon as a seller hasn't ensured that items they sell can be covered by warranty in the countries they deliver it too - thus, they must stop selling it in countries other than US. The Amazon support provided absolutely no help in resolving the situation and was just trying to get rid of me every time I contacted them. My suggestion to any viewers of the review is to never ever buy electronics from Amazon unless you ensure beforehand that the manufacturer offers warranty support in the country you reside at.

review_title: Why does Amazon offer delivery guarantees they can’t keep?
review_text: I have used Prime for years because of their guaranteed delivery experience.  I am the caretaker for my 85 year old Mom.  In addition, I have an elderly dog that has limited mobility due to being briefly paralyzed in her hind quarters several years ago.  I regularly turn to Amazon for both their physical  and medical needs. Frequently, I use Prime to purchase items for their incontinence needs.Having an 85 year old mother and a 14 year old dog means there are going to be times when my supply of adult pull ups or doggy diapers quickly becomes depleted.  Friday, I ordered disposable dog diapers.  I had enough left that Amazon’s promised next day delivery of 10 a.m. to 3 p.m. would be sufficient.  I was concerned when the promised timeframe was missed.  My concern grew when I saw that Amazon had a note stating a delivery was attempted that morning. I was home all day.  There was no way it was a missed delivery.  The note indicated the delivery person needed a gate code.  It also stated the order was being processed in a city 50 miles south of my residence at the time I read the notice.  I don’t live in a residence that requires a code.  And, how was a delivery attempted at 9:42 in the morning if it was still being processed in a city 50 miles away after 3 p.m.?I called Amazon to try to get clarification regarding my order and the missed timeline.  The inconsistency was not clarified, although the lady I spoke with tried her best to do so.  She did, however, guarantee that the order would be delivered by 8 p.m.It was almost 9 p.m. when I realized I hadn’t received a delivery notice.  This morning, still no delivery notice and still no diapers for my very wet dog.I give very explicit directions regarding delivery to my apartment because it’s located in an area where GPS frequently confuses people trying to find my exact location.  I also, explain where each delivery needs to be placed in front of my apartment door.  Out of every 10 Amazon deliveries, at least 3 deliveries end up placed by the mailbox just inside the door of my apartment building.  Another 3 Amazon deliveries, out of that 10,  end up placed outside on the step in front of the door to my apartment building.  But Amazon wonders why people complain about stolen deliveries.  And, I live in a decent area.My day is complicated enough in dealing with an ailing (and often wet) 85 year old mother and a sweet, but often wet 14 year old year old dog.  I don’t need Amazon deliveries to further complicate my already exhausting day by making promises that they don’t keep.

review_title: The Creepy cost of Alexa/Google and no, its not cash.
review_text: If you want to use multiple Spotify accounts on a smart speaker, be warned: it’s a data-mining trap.​To hear your own music instead of a roommate's, Alexa and Google "require" Voice Match. They market this as a luxury, but it’s actually the unnecessary collection of biometric data. There is no technical reason a simple command like "Switch to my account" couldn't work without a digital fingerprint of your vocal cords.​By forcing you to upload a unique voiceprint just to access a service you already pay for, these tech giants are building a database of exactly who is in your home and when. Don't trade your privacy for a playlist.

However, once the delivery was completed, the company refused to honor the agreed discount. Most notably, in an official written response, the company’s support explicitly admitted: "The promised discount was a false promise".It is unacceptable for a major retailer to induce sales through documented misrepresentation and then refuse to fulfill its financial obligations once the transaction is finalized. I have already formally escalated this case of misleading commercial practices to the European Consumer Centre and I am fully prepared to pursue the matter through the European Small Claims Procedure.Potential customers should be aware that written promises from this platform may be officially "false" by their own admission.

review_title: Counterfeit Frontline plus Flee Treatment, Chemical burns, hair loss, risk of long term health problems & possible sudden death of pet.
review_text: I have 3 cats. One of which is a Maine Coon cat. I used to purchase Frontline Plus Flee treatment from my local pet store, but due to the cost of living crisis, I decided to order a box of Frontline plus from a seller claiming to be the FRONTLINE  store on Amazon, as it seemed the more affordable option for me.To my dismay, within 48 hours, I discovered that all 3 cats had a negative reaction to the drops, I applied to their skin. All 3 of my cats experienced a chemical burn and bald patches where the drops had been applied. The drops appear to have also affected one of my cats breathing. She now laboured breathing.Unfortunately, my Maine Coon cat has lost a significant amount of hair from around his neck, due to the reaction he has had to this product.This is extremely distressing. I purchased this product in good faith, and it has caused my beloved pets pain and distress. My pets have been left traumatised due to the effects of this product!!!I do not believe this product is authentic at all, because this has “NEVER HAPPENED WITH FRONTLINE DROPS, I PREVIOUSLY BOUGHT FROM MY LOCAL PET STORE!!! I STRONGLY BELIEVE THE PRODUCT I BOUGHT, WAS COUNTERFEIT !!!“ I am so upset. This product has caused my pets harm. After further research I have now discovered, this product could even cause there deaths!!!After a recent visit to my local vet, I have now discovered that one my cats has now developed a life threatening Heart Murmur, which could cause there death literally at any moment. She was perfectly healthy before applying these drops. I strongly believe this is linked to the counterfeit Frontline Plus drops I dosed my cat with.I have tried repeatedly to do a review of the product via the Amazon website but they refuse to post my review, as they say my review is in breach of there guidelines.“PLEASE, PLEASE PLEASE DO YOUR OWN RESEARCH!!! “ PLEASE REFER TO the government website regarding urgent warning  to pet owners as to toxic chemicals found in fake flee treatments.For the purpose of this review I have given Amazon 1 star. If I had my way, I would have given absolutely nothing!!!I have reported my concerns to Amazon. I informed them that I personally believe this product to be counterfeit. Looking at other customer reviews and pictures, pets are coming to real harm!!! From the pictures in the reviews, other pets are experiencing chemical burns and hair loss, and flee infestations.I was assured Amazon would investigate the seller and if they where to be found to be selling counterfeit goods, they take down the seller and remove them from Amazon website. But months after reporting this product/ seller, Amazon have done absolutely nothing!!! This seller is still available on there sight and they also have the audacity to be making unauthorised purchases￼from my account, even though I had cancelled all subscriptions from my account. I have to be regularly cancelled unauthorised purchases, from this seller.After further investigation, looking around on the Amazon website and looking at other reviews for various different brands of Flee drops, for Cats and Dogs, I discovered owners complaining of there pets and home with flee infestations and even more pictures of both Cats and Dogs with Chemical burns and Hair loss, where these counterfeit flee products had been applied. ￼Amazon don’t appear to be doing anything about counterfeit products been sold on there platform. This could be doing real damage to reputable brands!!! “There are no warnings, no product recalls, NOTHING!!! Amazon are only to happy to take money from customers and clearly do not care about there customers or the welfare of animals!!! Due to my experience it has me questioning the authenticity of all products on the amazon website/platform. I will never purchase any form of medication or treatments from Amazon for my pets.  I will not take the risk!!!￼”

The Driver, a relatively new to Canada, a highly educated professional awaiting entry into a medical accreditation program, reported his pproblem to Amazon  and they cancelled his remaining deliveries and told him it was his problem. A very helpful young man was using the road on an ATV and stopped to offer assistance, then he called me as I have a tractor and have helped people in similar situations on this road.I spoke to Amazon Client Service Representative on behalf of the marooned driver ( his English is very broken) trying to get through to an Amazon Authority,  to correct the delivery route guidance provided to their contractors, since this was at least the 5th or 6th time I have been involved trying to help these stranded individuals. Towing and Recovery Services for this specific type of occurrence now costs in excess of $1500 per incident to get the marooned vehicle returned to a drive able road. This driver could not pay the costs and his vehicle is now abandoned on a desolate deep mud road.I spoke to The Client Service Representative (also very broken English appearing to speak from a script) was only able to suggest that I log into my account and was unable to connect me with any supervisor. Ruined World Book Day: 12 hours of lies and fake email addresses!​Amazon has reached a new level of incompetence and heartless deception. Today is World Book Day—a milestone event for my 7-year-old son—and Amazon’s systematic failure has completely ruined it for him.​I spent 12 hours, from 11:00 AM until 11:00 PM, fighting with "advisors" who did nothing but stall and lie. I was told to "calm down" and "be patient" while my son’s deadline approached. One advisor told me the item was delivered (it wasn't), another told me the driver was on my road (they weren't).​I was so desperate to save my son's day that I was back on chat at 6:00 AM. I was promised a "Rapid Delivery" by 8:00 AM and even received a confirmation email. It was all a scam to get me off the chat. One advisor, Manal, even gave me a fake, non-existent email address (same-day-delivery@amazonian) just to stop me from following up. While they were promising "Rapid Delivery," another department admitted the parcel hadn't even reached the delivery station.​Because of Amazon’s lies, I had to rush to a supermarket late at night to find anything I could. My son has gone to school for World Book Day in a Tesco costume that is two sizes too small (size 5-6) because that was the only stock left.​Being offered a measly £20 for 12 hours of stress, blatant lies from staff, and a ruined childhood memory is an absolute insult. Do not trust their "Next Day Delivery" or their "Prime Guarantee"—it is a total fiction.​Order #206-6483703-6703502Update 6 March 2026:I have now been contacted by Akash from the Amazon Executive Relations team.Despite my detailed complaint about 12 hours of lies and the fake email address (same-day-delivery@amazonian) provided by their staff, the response was a generic, scripted apology. They are pointing to a basic refund as if it solves the problem. A refund is just returning my own money for an item I never received; it does not compensate for a ruined World Book Day or the fact that their advisors lied to a parent for 12 hours straight.I have rejected their standard brush-off and am demanding a resolution that actually reflects the scale of this deception and the distress caused to my 7-year-old son.Update 7th March:I have now received a response from Nassima in the Executive Relations team. She has officially admitted in writing that my son’s World Book Day experience was ruined, that I was given inaccurate delivery updates for 12 hours, and—most shockingly—that the fake email address provided by their staff was "unacceptable."Despite admitting to this systemic deception and misconduct, Amazon is STILL only offering a measly £20. They have admitted to lying to a customer and sabotaging my son’s school event, yet they think £20 is a fair price for 12 hours of stress and staff fraud. I have rejected this insult and am continuing to hold them accountable.​Update 8th March 2026: The deception from Amazon continues. I have now been contacted by a third "Executive Relations" person named Laura. After the previous manager admitted that their staff gave me a fake email address and lied about the driver's location for 12 hours, Laura is now trying to backtrack. She is claiming these failures were due to "weather-related disruptions" and "inventory issues."​It is an absolute insult to my intelligence to blame the weather for a staff member making up a fraudulent email address (same-day-delivery@amazonian) to stall a parent on World Book Day. They have admitted to the misconduct but are refusing to provide any real compensation, hiding behind a scripted "final offer" of £20. Amazon clearly thinks that 12 hours of a customer's time and a child’s ruined milestone are worth nothing. I am now taking this evidence of deceptive trade practices to consumer rights regulators and the press. Do not trust this company’s "guarantees."Update: March 10th - Amazon team have now plain simply avoided responding to my last email sent.Update: 16th March - The situation has reached a stalemate. I have now heard from a fifth person, Stefanie from Executive Relations. Despite Amazon’s previous admission that their staff used a fraudulent email address (same-day-delivery@amazonian) and lied to me for 12 hours, Stefanie has officially refused to provide a resolution that reflects this misconduct. I even offered to resolve the matter and remove this review if they provided a fair gesture of goodwill for the time and stress caused, but they declined. It is now clear that Amazon's "Executive Team" is more interested in protecting deceptive staff than in being customer-centric. I am now proceeding with a formal complaint via Citizens Advice for breach of the Consumer Rights Act. Do not trust their "guarantees"—they admit to the lies but refuse to fix the damage.

review_title: Amazon’s Return Detection Model Needs Context — Not Punishment for Legitimate Returns
review_text: Amazon’s return fraud detection system relies on machine learning patterns designed to fight organized refund abuse, but it often misclassifies normal customer returns as suspicious. Amazon publicly states it uses advanced models to analyze “signals and data points” to identify refund fraud, yet these systems do not consider the realities of online shopping—such as high return rates in apparel, inconsistent sizing, or quality issues from third party sellers. Instead, customers who return items within Amazon’s own policies may receive warnings simply because their patterns resemble statistical fraud models. [autods.com], [limitlessinsider.com]This problem is made worse by Amazon’s own refund first policies, which attract return fraud and trigger stricter algorithmic enforcement that affects innocent buyers. Low quality or inconsistent items—often sourced from direct from factory marketplaces—create legitimate returns that Amazon incorrectly attributes to buyer misuse. Rather than addressing listing quality or seller sourcing issues, Amazon penalizes customers whose return activity is normal, policy compliant, and unavoidable. I request that Amazon implement category aware, context-based review and offer a clear appeal path for customers wrongly flagged by automated systems.

review_title: I have been a Prime Member for 8 Years
review_text: I have been a Prime Member for 8 Years. To begin with, Amazon discontinued "Archive an order" out of the blue for no reason and never told anyone about it.Secondly,  I received an email about adding members of the family to your household. It said that they would get free Amazon Prime Shipping and could share your wallet.  The benefit allows the family member to not be able to see anything you have purchased but be able to have their own account and order their own items. This was great because my husband would not be able to see anything I ordered.I opened an account for my husband under his name and email, went back to my account and tried linking him to the account. When he accepted the invitation and tried to order something the Prime free shipping did not come up.  He also had to add his own credit card to the account.Now! here is where it gets good.  HAVE YOU EVER TRIED TO CONTACT CUSTOMER SERVICE???? It is impossible to get to them in one shot. In fact they make it impossible, it is so obvious they do not want to be bothered. I finally found a link through CHAT GPT and got to Amazon Chat.  It lands in INDIA.   I explained my problem with what felt like hours. I kept getting passed along to the next agent.  The specialist was really NOT a specialist.  I think they have index cards to read how to remedy a problem because it took forever to get reponses back.  Eventually I did exactly what I was told and still it does not work.So, now I am at the point where it will not even allow me to create another invite.  It says: Something went wrong. Please try again later with a big fat RED Exclamation mark.I give up! I don't understand how Amazon got as big as it did. It started out GREAT, then through the years declined terribly.  Frankly, the India customer service sends me over the edge.  I can't understand a word they say when they phone you back and Chat is totally useless.I also want to add I had purchase something in November, did not use it until January and it was defective.  It was an Amazon Product.  They finally agreed to credit me and take it back after fighting for days. They provided a UPS-Pick-Up label.  When they got it back they charged my account for the shipping.So deceitful.Walmart is now doing everything Amazon is doing but without a yearly "Prime" fee. Basically everybody’s selling on Amazon is also selling on Walmart so you’re getting the same exact sellers and the same exact products.I would suggest everybody moving  their business over to Walmart.I

review_title: Best online shopping site I have used.
Horrible Customer Service and Delivery services
review_text: Can't give it less than a 1.  I'm using excerpts from written correspondence to write most of this.We've had an ongoing issue where Amazon stating that they delivered an order to our front door or porch and then photographic the inside of a mail room up the street.  Finally got through to a live person this weekend and got this:Received this note on Sat, Feb 28, 6:26 AM (2 days ago) and at 6:27 (2 days) ago - they cared enough to send it twice!"AmazonHello,This is Masrath, with Amazon's Shipping and Delivery Support Escalations Team.Firstly, I want to thank you for your time and efforts reaching us today about your concern.I am grateful for the warm approach that you gave, I apologize for the unsatisfactory experience with a delivery driver.I have passed your message on to the appropriate department in our company for immediate action to be taken.We value this kind of feedback, as it helps us continue to improve our standards and provide better service to our customers.If you have any other question feel free to contact us.Hope you were still satisfied with how I handled your concern today.We hope to see you again.Best regards,Amazon.com"Remember this - It won't let you highlight text so please allow me to extract a sentence from their ORIGINAL message to us - "If you have any other question feel free to contact us.".  Where would you direct your response to?  After some soul searching, we replied to the email address from which the response came from with this:"Firstly,Thanks for even contacting me.  Never thought it would happen considering how long we’ve been dealing with.We moved to this property in late December physically but were on holiday until 02 January 2026.  That’s when we began ordering, in earnest, to finish up the little touches.Initially we just dealt with all of the packages being delivered to the clubhouse, despite the specific instructions for delivery in our account.I had experienced some strange neck pain and on 06 January was diagnosed with a cracked vertebrae (pedicle fracture of my C7) which was going to involve a fairly complicated and serious surgery (see attached new hardware added to my cervical / thoracic spine.  Now I’m not able to lift more than 10lbs, bend my neck and several other restrictions.At that point we drew the line (or I did), and notified Amazon that if it wasn’t delivered per the delivery instructions, it was considered “undelivered” my wife continues to drag a 6-wheel dolly (that we got from Amazon) because we are now in a 2-story unit.  The 6 wheels are in a 3x3 configuration to help going up stairs.Once I had voiced my displeasure most recently, whoever makes the delivery is careful to make the statement that it was delivered to the front door or near the front door but then, they finish up with a picture of the common area mailroom (filled with boxes) as proof of delivery.  A blatantly false statement (see attached as well)We believe that yesterday 27 February, was the first day that EVERY Amazon package was in fact delivered to our front door.This is great, now we just need to ensure all of the delivery personnel get the message.I understand that they aren’t  ‘official Amazon employees’ but they are the ‘face’ of the company since they are 99.999999% of the time customers have physical interactions with Amazon.Thanks in advance for what I hope can be a teaching / learning moment for everyone."It won't let you highlight text so please allow me to extract a sentence from their ORIGINAL message to us - "If you have any other question feel free to contact us.".Yes, some of this is pretty personal information, even provided a cp[y of the X-ray with my new hardware and a picture of a small corner of the mail room (AKA our front door or porch if you are AMAZON).Ran out of space - stay tuned to more stupidity from Amazon when I'm allowed to add to this.

review_title: Amazon Has Become Unsafe, Unreliable, and Full of Low‑Quality Junk products lately
review_text: I’m genuinely shocked — literally and figuratively — by my recent Amazon orders. One extension cord I bought actually gave me an electric shock, which is a serious safety hazard. Then another order arrived and it wasn’t even the right item. At this point, the marketplace feels completely overrun with third‑party sellers pushing low level garbage, and the reviews are so obviously manipulated that you can’t trust anything you read anymore.It’s exhausting trying to sort through low‑quality products, fake reviews, and sellers who clearly don’t care about safety or accuracy. What’s worse is that Amazon doesn’t seem to be doing anything to protect customers from dangerous items or misleading listings. The platform no longer feels safe, reliable, or worth my money.I’m honestly considering leaving Amazon altogether because the experience has gone downhill so badly. Customers shouldn’t have to risk their safety or waste their time dealing with problems Amazon should be preventing.

I'm done. I wont watch another thing on Amazon Prime Video. I noticed multiple times in the past that if I dont finish a show episode after watching one of the ad sets, another ad set mysteriously appears when I get back, even a couple hours later. Today I proved it. I watched one of three remaining ad sets, plus a few minutes after, then got distracted and when I came back, it had added another closer to the end of the episode, making for a ridiculous total of five ad sets for one garbage episode. Not to mention they routinely end your episodes before you've finished them if you pause a couple minutes before the end and go to bed. The next day it will start on the next episode... So first you watch an ad there, then realize its the wrong episode, then go back and you have to watch two more sets of ads just to fast forward back to where you were, then it starts the next episode back over making you watch a fourth set of ads all because they decided you were done with the episode two minutes early.I've been a heavy amazon user and prime for most of the time from 2007 til now. Nearly 20 years. But i am fed up with their abusive money grubbing tactics at this point. I wont be renewing my prime this year.  I'd rather pay for more for Netflix than support a company doing shady ish like that. (Hulu also does this kind of nonsense, so I won't use them anymore either)

It had no power and produced no steam completely unusable.I initiated a return within the return window. However, because I am located in Switzerland, the only return option provided was “self-ship,” requiring me to pay international shipping and export/import duties. Reimbursement for postage was capped at £8.09 far below actual international return costs.I escalated the matter through multiple written contacts and live chat. I was eventually connected to a manager. I clearly requested either a refund without return (given the defective nature of the item) or a fully prepaid international return solution.The manager by the name of "Ali" confirmed in writing that neither option would be provided and stated this was Amazon’s “final position.” employing such ignorant people who don't care about customers seems to be the Amazon way of treating their customers in my case it seems, Instead of taking responsibility as the seller, I was redirected to the manufacturer under the banner of “sustainability.” Warranty referral is not a substitute for the seller’s obligation to provide a reasonable remedy for defective goods.This experience shows how Amazon UK’s policies appear flexible until cross-border customers are involved. At that point, rigid internal rules override fairness and proportionality.For UK-based customers, returns may be simple. For international customers, defective goods can become the customer’s financial burden.After many years as an Amazon customer, this experience has led me to close my account, I hope Jeff Besos is reading this get rid of these useless employees and get your AI machine working because these people do not have a clue, what they are doing

review_title: Amazon, even with prime delivery routinely uses some of the worst couriers, giving us little to no say in the matter
review_text: I buy a fair amount from Amazon, and from time to time use Prime Delivery. One recurring problem is that more and more items on Amazon are being shipped directly from China. While I have no problem with Chinese goods, the shipping from China is awful, and often it seems that the need for adequate packaging is not understood, as fragile items are routinely shipped in flimsy paper or single layer bubble wrap. As though this is not bad enough, many items are then picked up by Evri, arguably the worst courier in the UK. I currently have one item, supposedly being delivered by Evri which shipped in the UK to my UK address. I live close to the Humber Bridge, the parcel was picked up by Evri and taken to the Barnsley hub, then for some reason only understood by Evri, it was taken to Southampton, Evri described this error as a 'delay' what they meant by delay, is 'monumental screw-up' and this is their speciality, they are very good at being exceptionally bad. Next the parcel came back up the country to Rugby, then on to Sheffield; so now it is almost back where it started, but so far, it is late, and it is not any closer to reaching me.I also have two parcels from China being delivered by CNE Express, a Chinese courier, who are actually worse than Evri, their tracking just never changes, once or twice maybe, it did update almost a week after the goods where finally delivered. I have not received either of these parcels, and have now been refunded, though I was not asked if this was what I desired, and nothing on Amazon, or CNE Express shows if the orders have been cancelled or what. I left AliExpress because of chronically late deliveries coupled with totally inadequate packaging, and now it seems that Amazon has begun to embrace the same awful setup, but charges significantly higher prices, for the same terrible service. Buying on Amazon is getting to be like putting your hard earned cash in a fruit machine, sometimes you win, but mostly, you don't. From here on in, I will only use Amazon when there is no other alternative. Even then I will really question if the item I am looking for is truly a requirement, because in all honesty, I can do without the stress of chasing up on late deliveries, and items arriving damaged because of completely inadequate packing.Due to health issues, I am stuck with buying online, but if I was well enough, I would buy from local shops, which provide an all round better service.Useless couriers, along with companies and individuals who send out badly packaged goods are slowly killing online buying. There are some decent reliable delivery services, but eBay, Amazon and many other online sales platforms seem drawn to the worst of what is available. More and more, our right to choose the delivery service we want, is being removed, and replaced by what the likes of Amazon and eBay think are the best choices, but they do not have the right to dictate how our paid for items are delivered, with online buying, the delivery method is part of the transaction, and in the same way we choose the items to buy, we have the right to choose how the items will be delivered. After all, we are the ones paying for the delivery service. Don't be fooled by 'Free postage and Packing' there is no such thing. The couriers always charge, and as the buyer, we always pay that charge. So we have every right to choose what we pay for.

This is a blatant return-cost trap with Amazon Marketplace sellers.I had two return cases:• An €80 item with a return address in the UK — return shipping from the Netherlands cost €23+.• A €40 item with a return address in China — return shipping was €35.No prepaid return labels were provided in either case — only an address. That forces customers to arrange and pay for international shipping themselves.I returned the UK item. I did not return the China item, because paying €35 to return a €40 product is absurd.This setup isn’t an accident. It effectively blocks returns while pretending a return policy exists. None of this is made clear at checkout, and the financial risk is quietly dumped on the customer.Calling this “buyer-friendly” is misleading. It’s a deterrent strategy, and customers should know exactly what they’re walking into.

I’ve had Amazon Amazon Prime for years now, and it’s easily one of the most consistently useful subscriptions I pay for. It’s not just about convenience—it’s about reliability. Prime has become something I use weekly, often multiple times a week, whether I’m ordering household essentials, electronics, pantry items, or last-minute gifts. The consistent usage alone justifies the membership.The quick delivery times are where Prime truly stands out. Two-day shipping is standard, but in many cases I receive items the next day or even same day. When you need something urgently—whether it’s a replacement charger, tools for a project, or everyday necessities—Prime eliminates the stress of waiting. The tracking is accurate, updates are clear, and deliveries arrive when promised.What impresses me most, though, is the customer service. In the rare situation where there’s an issue—damaged item, wrong product, or late package—the support team responds quickly and professionally. Returns are simple, refunds are processed fast, and representatives genuinely work to resolve concerns. There’s a sense that they want to make it right, and that level of accountability builds trust over time.Beyond shipping, the added benefits—like streaming, exclusive deals, and Prime Day savings—provide additional value that feels like a bonus on top of an already strong service.Minor complaints? Occasionally an item marked for fast shipping may take an extra day, and not every third-party seller matches Amazon’s service standard. But those instances are rare compared to the overall positive experience.Overall, Amazon Prime isn’t just a convenience—it’s a dependable service that consistently delivers value, speed, and customer support you can count on.

review_title: Amazon's Rep, admission of unfair and deceptive business practices
review_text: Most recent order placed, was deceptively marked by the driver as being delivered today, 2/18/26 @ approx 3.45pm. However, it was not of course. In fact, there was no Amazon truck in the complex area, per ring cam videos. After heavy research, it was determined no deliveries were made to the complex locker box room as agent 1 attempted to share. Locker box deliveries are accompanied by a personal access code via text and/or email to retrieve items. The deception arises after contacting Amazon of the "common practice" that the package was marked delivered, but was not, agent #2 blatantly shared, drivers will mark packages delivered before they actually are. Essentially, deceiving consumers, sending them on fruitless wild goose chases, bothering neighbors about packages, Amazon drivers never delivered. After the customer service agent attempted to use the phrase "accidently marked as delivered," I informed her, not only was her statement misleading, but how is a delivery marked in error, if the driver was not in the complex area at that time documented. In furtherance, no confirmation photo of the delivery was taken by driver, further demonstrating the deception of the falsified delivery entry. review_title: Seller Financial Risk Due to Refund and Support Practices
review_text: In many cases, Amazon’s current policies appear to disproportionately favor customers, creating significant financial risk for sellers. For example, full refunds are often granted for claims of non-receipt, refunds may be issued without requiring a return, used or damaged items are accepted for full refunds, and refunds are processed even when incorrect items are returned. These situations ultimately place the financial burden on sellers.Additionally, I have had multiple frustrating experiences with Amazon Support where commitments made during chats were not honored. It often feels as though responses are given simply to conclude the conversation, without proper follow-through or resolution. This lack of accountability makes it difficult to rely on the guidance provided.

review_title: Amazon took $77.02 from my gift card…
review_text: Amazon took $77.02 from my gift card balance without any warning, authorization, or explanation. When I discovered the missing money, my account simply showed “withheld for investigation.” No email. No notification. Nothing.I contacted customer service multiple times. Every single time I was told, “We raised a ticket. Wait 3–5 days. You will receive an email.” I never received any email. No update. No transparency.After escalating to a supervisor, I was suddenly told my gift card was “flagged and locked” and that I should contact the store where I purchased it. That is completely unacceptable. The card was successfully redeemed and the balance was loaded into my account. The store cannot refund a redeemed card. This is not the store’s responsibility — it is Amazon’s.If the gift card was truly “suspicious,” why was I not notified immediately when the funds were frozen? Why was my money taken silently? Why was there no written explanation? Withholding customer funds without proper notice is unacceptable and highly questionable.This experience shows a serious lack of transparency and accountability. Customers deserve clear communication and lawful handling of their funds — not silent deductions and endless excuses.My advice to everyone: Be extremely careful. Don’t trust your balance is safe.

They never did.• The Infinite Loop: I had to contact them twice just to get an update. Both times, they acted as if the previous conversation never happened and forced me to start the entire claim process from scratch.• Moving the Goalposts: After failing to prove their insulting accusations of fraud, they are now hiding behind "policy," refusing to help unless I provide a physical activation receipt. Who gives an activation receipt along with a gift?I provided clear evidence of my intended purchase and the physical state of the card, yet Amazon prefers to protect a thief and insult a loyal customer over a technicality.Be warned: If you buy or receive an Amazon gift card and it is tampered with, Amazon will treat YOU like the criminal and then bury you in red tape to avoid a refund. Absolutely disgraceful.

review_title: It is a profound irony that after…
review_text: It is a profound irony that after fifteen years of market dominance, this company remains incapable of mastering the basic logistics of its own promises. One would assume that over a decade and a half, the evolution of a service-oriented entity would trend toward precision; instead, we are met with a "new normal" of astronomical delays and a recurring void where expected items simply vanish.The infrastructure is clearly failing, but the failure isn't for a lack of capital. When an organization chooses to pivot its vast resources into high-profile, external investments like the Melania partnerships rather than refining the fundamental experience of the people who built them, their true nature is revealed.This is the hallmark of a predatory establishment. It is an entity that has grown so large it no longer feels the need to honor the basic contract of reliability. They are not "struggling" with delivery; they are choosing where to direct their energy, and it isn't toward the customer. If you value your time and a company that respects the energy exchange of a transaction, look elsewhere. This is a system that refuses to change because it no longer believes it has to.

review_title: Review: The "Customer Obsession" is Dead – Amazon Has Lost Its Way
review_text: From "Day 1" to "Day None": A Masterclass in How to Alienate Your Best CustomerIt used to be a point of pride for Amazon to be the "Earth’s most customer-centric company." Yet, despite my loyalty, Amazon has managed to hit a new low. In a scenario with five possible mistakes, they somehow managed to score a perfect ten. Congratulations! 🥳The "Service" (or lack thereof):• Incompetence at Scale: They made the error, yet they refuse to fix it. It’s like talking to a brick wall.• Total Illiteracy: I’ve written detailed explanations of the issue, and it’s obvious no one is actually reading them. I get canned, bot-like responses that don’t even touch upon the problem.• The Overseas Blackout: If you are traveling or living abroad, good luck. Their "customer support" phone numbers are unreachable from outside their specific zones, making it impossible to get a human on the line when you need them most.The "Customer Obsession" has been replaced by "Process Obsession." You’ve become a bloated, disconnected machine that has forgotten who pays the bills.Consider this my final order. I’m taking my business elsewhere.You can take your "convenience" and go where the pepper grows (dra dit peppar’n växer)! 🌶️

review_title: Amazon clearly states most returns are…
review_text: Amazon clearly states most returns are credited back to the account in about 2 hours. This is what I am used too when I return one of their products. But wait!!! After experiencing my first ice storm of the season last week I was very hopeful the electronic di icers would work. Oh foolish me! I had them fully charged on the day before the storm,for over 5 days the di icers did nothing. Of course I wanted a refund. I dropped the product off on a Saturday at UPS. I waited until Monday , because Nashville was still recovering from the storm, before I called Amazon to inquire where was my refund. I spoke with someone and was told it could take up to 14 days. I said I was not familiar with it taking so long. It plainly states most times the credit is issued within 2  hours. The CS informed me it could take up to 14 days .  She first told me it would be credited back to my credit card but then corrected herself and said gift card. The next logical question is why so long. Oh by the way the CS was not aware that 14 days = 2 weeks. She couldn’t tell me why but could confirm that I did indeed returned the item. Because she couldn’t tell me why the long wait I asked for a supervisor,  I also felt she was just trying to talk over me and avoid answering my question. I also believe she should have been the one to voluntarily transfer me to a supervisor. I think that is what good communication and customer service skills would dictate if you don’t know the answer to a customer’s question get advice from the superior . I was quite frustrated with her and her command of the English language. She keep changing her story. Finally when I was connected to a Supervisor, my account was immediately credited and I was given an apology which I accepted. A couple of days later  I receive a threatening letter from Amazon reprimanding me of my communication with the CS. There was of course no mention of her inability to answer my question. I was threatened with possibly suspending my account but what ever happened to customer service. I stand on why was I not just simply transferred to a supervisor in fact when I requested a supervisor the CS assured me I would get the same response from a supervisor as she had given me. Luckily I didn’t believe her. When a customer’s money is involved the situation should not be handled lightly or haphazardly. I work hard for my money and it was not my fault Amazon sold a product that just inefficient  and ineffective. It was a scam and that is what I told the CS. Again I took this situation as a threat!!! But what about the untrained CS who I believe purposely gave me false information in hopes I would get off the phone. She showed no interest whatsoever in showing me customer service or support and definitely not customer satisfaction.

review_title: Yesterday I closed my Amazon account…
review_text: Yesterday I closed my Amazon account for good.  When a corporation tells you they don't care about you, you need to believe them.  I won't go into all of what brought me to this moment but it was a lot.  I took all of the Amazon devices controlled by Alexia and tossed them.  I no longer trust having them in my home. I had two Fire TVs that I now just use as monitors.  I will no longer spend hundreds a month at Whole Foods.  I'm vegan and even in a college town the deli never makes anything vegan?  But it is so much more than that.  I felt so good after making the decision this time, as I had closed my account before, to toss out the Amazon devices.  It's freeing to know I'm done with them.  Just as it was deleting Facebook a long time ago.  I was spending thousands of dollars a year with just Amazon and even close to $5,000 per year at Whole Foods.  They really don't care if you close your account as they still believe people will stay and they can treat you any way they want...and you will stay?  Everything I used to buy with Amazon, someone else has the same thing.  If they don't, I don't need it anyway.  It feels like greed is the common denominator for the heads of these corporations that eventually become some of the wealthiest in the world and then they just don't care about anyone but themselves.  I feel bad for the employees as they are just trying to make money to live on.  Many, but not all of the Whole Foods employees are amazing.  Being vegan it makes it more difficult but I will do whatever it takes to be done with Amazon and anything associated with them.  This time was different and I know I will never go back.

review_title: I have been with Amazon for 30 years
review_text: I have been with Amazon for 30 years.  Although what some have written may be true...  ask yourself this question how did they get so may customers so quickly and why are these customers still faithful buying customers like me.This list of the various item they sell is astounding, you must however read through the reviews and decide if the item you want has the quality you want. Cheap is not always a good buy.  In the many years of buying from this one store I have bought many long lasting items. Only a few items had to be returned. This is the only store I shop from. They are the "everything store" selection is huge.Amazon has been constantly scammed by some of their millions of customers. These are sometimes the same customers that are complaining.  I have been in retail sales and dealing with online customers is brutal.Amazon has had to change the rules of how it handle this minority who "take advantage Amazon".  Not all of the rules I liked but I understand.I have done online research both from reviews left by customers and from other stores.  I always bought high quality items. Shipping has been fast, however with the shape of UPS  FedEx and USPS shippers, independent shippers can be rocky.  However with Independent Drivers, if you are nice... shipping can be quick.I have talked with Amazon a few times and they have always resolved my problems often with refunds or by shipping a new item. Customer Service Agents deal with many rude customers. However they are quite nice when treated nicely.I am thankful for the many years of Service by Amaozn.

review_title: We used to use Amazon for pretty much…
review_text: We used to use Amazon for pretty much everything, but I don’t know what’s happening with the service recently. Every parcel seems to end up delayed, the items that are delivered are just chucked in the driveway, often never to be seen again, and their customer service makes you want to pull your hair out. Today was the final straw. Placed an order for delivery today-it contained restricted items which need age verification so we ensured to select items that would be delivered on a Sunday so we could ensure we were home. Waited in all day only to get a message saying Royal Mail attempted delivery. Royal Mail? The postal service that have never delivered on a Sunday? (we have cameras, no one has been near the house today) Yep, Amazon chose to send a parcel that needed to be delivered on a Sunday, with a carrier that does not work Sundays. But very helpfully, my parcel is being held for me at the depot, ten miles away from my house, for me to collect. I mean, of course, they don’t open on a Sunday either, because Royal Mail don’t work Sundays. I asked for a refund because this item is never actually going to arrive now, but nope, can’t have a refund either. Not until I’ve collected it-a 20 mile round trip-and then returned it. Or wait for it to time out. So to be clear, I don’t have my parcel, or my money, because Amazon chose to use a carrier that doesn’t work Sundays, to deliver a parcel on a Sunday, but none of that is Amazon’s fault. The chap I spoke to offered me a £5 credit, which of course hasn’t materialised either (they never do), after claiming he would definitely make sure Royal Mail delivered my parcel today. Which of course is absolute ballcarp because ROYAL MAIL DONT WORK SUNDAYS.So yeah, I’ve given up at this point, will be contacting my card issuer for a refund instead. And may as well cancel Prime too as, well whats the point?

review_title: Order Never Delivered – Courier Never Showed Up
review_text: The courier never showed up. No doorbell, no phone call, no message — nothing. Yet the delivery was falsely marked as “customer not found at home.” That is simply a lie. I was at home the entire day.This kind of fake delivery attempt is unacceptable. If the courier didn’t want to deliver, they should say so — not fabricate excuses in the system.What made this even worse was Amazon’s response. After I explained the situation, Amazon suggested that I go pick up the package myself from the nearest courier point, which happens to be 15 km away. That’s not a solution — it’s shifting Amazon’s delivery failure onto the customer.I did not pay for a “drive across town and fix our courier’s mistake yourself” service. I paid for home delivery.Between the courier lying about the delivery attempt and Amazon treating this as normal, I’ve completely lost trust in Amazon logistics. I will be avoiding Amazon for physical deliveries in the future, especially for books and important items.

review_title: Amazon shows Lack of transparency and systematic failure to meet delivery dateszero concern for the…
review_text: Amazon shows zero concern for the customer. The consumer is treated like a fool, with requests and concerns about orders and purchases consistently ignored.Delivery dates are changed unilaterally, without any consent. We purchase a product with a clearly stated delivery date, and when that date approaches or is exceeded, Amazon simply pushes the delivery further and further back. There is even a notice stating that after a certain number of days the customer can file a claim — but when that date arrives, all that happens is that a new delivery date is automatically assigned. This creates an endless cycle that misleads the consumer.This is not an isolated issue. Over the last three months, there have been numerous deliveries with this exact problem, clearly showing a pattern rather than an exception.An even more serious case occurred last year: I placed an order in May, clearly marked as available and with delivery scheduled within a few days. After completing the purchase, the delivery date was changed to December of the same year, without prior notice and without allowing immediate cancellation of the item. This is simply unacceptable.Transparency, respect for the customer, and honoring delivery commitments no longer seem to be priorities. A huge disappointment from a company of this size.

But you can’t return items over half the time without packaging. UPS will NOT accept that and neither will USPS. Not everything can be conveniently returned to Whole Foods without packaging. When I explicitly and specifically tell them to “ship inside Amazon packaging to hide what’s inside” I expect it to be done and when it’s NOT, I don’t appreciate being punished and being inconvenienced, jumping through hoops to try to return the item, because of their carelessness and laziness in not following SIMPLE directions. Last year an Amazon driver hit my parked car while trying to park with 5 car lengths of room to park. How the F do you hit a parked car!? They drive medium sized vans. It’s not like they’re driving fricking big rigs and 18 wheelers for Christ sake!! If it weren’t for me opening the front door and not having a security camera, he would’ve just driven away and my insurance company wouldn’t have reimbursed me for the damages. You have to go through 24 questions from an AI robot before being connected to an actual human being, who will most likely not help you. So back to the issue of mistakes that Amazon makes and returns become a nightmare when they should just be refunded immediately, no questions asked. Especially when dealing with long-term paying Prime members. Prime is $139 a year and this is what we pay for and have to deal with!!? If Costco sold as many things as Amazon does, I would just exclusively shop at Costco because returns are seamless, but that’s not reality. I don’t think Amazon will ever improve at this point. review_title: I have mixed feelings regarding Amazon
review_text: I have mixed feelings regarding Amazon. The whole searching and ordering something that you need is pretty straightforward, most of the time the items arrive when stated, but recently I've had a few delayed deliveries which isn't really acceptable when you're specifically paying for prime next day delivery. Customer services when you can eventually get hold of them, as it does take you around the houses when trying to speak with an actual human, do usually help solve any issues you have. My issue at the moment is I am starting to notice fraudulent sellers, and Amazon will do nothing to help and support you with this. I purchased an item before Christmas which claimed to be on sale, however when I came to review the item after Christmas I noticed the item now no longer on sale and considerably cheaper than what I purchased it for. When trying to flag this in my review I am told by Amazon the review cannot be posted as it does not review the actual product, therefore I need to leave seller feedback. My doorway is in a corner with another unit beside me. So the delivery picture proof should show a corner and two doors. Mine and my neighbors.The delivery pic showed a long hallway with no corner and the picture was very odd for a proof picture because they normally show the floor where the packages are and our door/unit number in the upper half of the image. The image they shot showed the packages at the top of the picture and the rest of the photo was the hallway carpeting.So there was no way to tell which door they actually dropped my packages. Amazon said to make a refund claim I'd need a police report. Fine I filed for one online. Got an officer to review my case and my picture proof of my doorway vs their delivery photo. I got a case file number and an incident file number. I was told by the officer to provide those two numbers to Amazon to make my claim.Amazon became d*cks about it and then claimed the format of the police report is not acceptable? All I got was two numbers. No actual document. So I tried again and had the police send me another link to the online page with both report numbers. Then from Chrome I pressing Print to PDF and uploaded that to Amazon. Again, they claim that format is unacceptable.That back and forth banter went on for 6 months so finally I approached my credit card (Mastercard) company. Gave them the police report and the screenshots and they instantly gave me a refund in full. They told me its not the first time they've heard that Amazon was being a d*ck about not accepting Canadian Police reports.Even their bots are screwy. Like the time I purchased a Queen Mattress, Queen sheets, Queen bedding and even Queen frame. At the checkout a bot of theirs popped up and claimed it found a cheaper mattress from the same brand with the same parameters (10" Foam, Med Firm) - so I clicked it and waited for it all to arrive.When it all arrived I began unboxing it all. Of course the mattress was vacuum sealed and exploded open when I cut the vacuum bag it was sealed in. I laid it out on the floor and began assembling the frame. Got the frame up and put the mattress on it. It looked smaller than the frame, but I know vacuum packed mattresses can take a full day to fully expand so I left it till the next day.Well the next day and to my surprise the mattress in the blank plain box was a Double and not a Queen. Well DUH!!! OF COURSE IT WAS CHEAPER YOU ST*PID BOT - it was a smaller mattress.Then trying to get Amazon to take responsibility and come pickup the expanded mattress was another matter. But the pickup company (CEVA) has a signature from the Amazon warehouse that they received the mattress. But Amazon claims they don't have it. That was another teeth pulling exercise I had to contact Visa this time. I have both my Mastercard and Visa as a backup on Amazon but because I was in dispute with the police report at the time, instead of charging my Mastercard for theQueen purchases, they automatically went to my backup card even though my Mastercard was not locked. Long story short I too had to contact VISA and make a claim against Amazon because they were being stupid about a return we have proof they received.

review_title: I am extremely disappointed with Amazon
review_text: I am extremely disappointed with Amazon and how this situation was handled from start to finish.I placed an order on Amazon.com (US) for four physical books, which were delivered to me in the European Union. When the books arrived, they were severely damaged. The pages were curled, warped, and clearly ruined due to inadequate packaging. I immediately contacted Amazon customer support and provided photos of the damage, as requested.During that conversation, the Amazon support agent explicitly informed me that the damaged books would be replaced free of charge and that I would not be required to return the damaged items. This was not just said verbally. It was later confirmed in writing in an official email sent by Amazon.The email clearly stated that the return had been “disarmed,” that I would not be charged, and that I was authorized to keep or dispose of the damaged books. Relying on this written confirmation from Amazon, I disposed of the damaged items exactly as instructed.Two months later, without warning or explanation, Amazon charged me $95.79 for the replacement books. When I contacted customer support again, I was told that the previous agent had made an “error” and that Amazon could not honor the written confirmation because the concession was not properly finalized in their internal system.At that point, I was told that a return was required in order to receive a refund, despite the fact that Amazon had explicitly instructed me to dispose of the items. When I explained that I had followed Amazon’s own written instructions, support agents refused to take responsibility and stated that they were unable to escalate the issue further due to internal limitations.This is where the experience became unacceptable. Amazon attempted to retroactively invalidate a written commitment made by its own support agent, after I had already relied on it in good faith. Internal system failures or internal policy issues should never be the customer’s problem.This is a matter of basic trust and billing integrity. If a company confirms in writing that a replacement is free and that no return is required, customers must be able to rely on that confirmation.Being charged months later and then told “we can’t fix it because our system doesn’t allow it” is not acceptable for a company of this size. This experience has seriously damaged my trust in Amazon, and I strongly advise others to keep every email and screenshot and to be extremely cautious when relying on Amazon customer service assurances.

review_title: 🚨 WARNING: iCarsoft - A Digital Trojan Horse? 🐎💣
review_text: 🚨 WARNING: iCarsoft - A Digital Trojan Horse? 🐎💣Rating: ⭐☆☆☆☆Review Title: Mechanic’s Warning: Fake Facades, Security Risks, and Environmental WasteAs a certified mechanic (Class of 1993), I’ve dug deep into the iCarsoft "rabbit hole." What started as a simple broken screen on my CR MAX ended with an investigation into systematic deception and a serious threat to your vehicle and privacy.Before you plug this into your car, read these facts:• ❌ The Fake U.S. Facade: iCarsoft claims a headquarters in Washington D.C. In reality? It’s a "Virtual Office" (a rented mailbox). My investigation proves the real strings are pulled from Shenzhen, China, outside EU/US regulations.• ❌ High Digital Risk: You are connecting closed-source Chinese code to your car’s brain (OBD port) via WiFi. IT specialists warn that this grants unmonitored access to your vehicle’s systems and your private home network.• ❌ Economic Russian Roulette: Cases from across Europe (including Germany) show this software causing complete dashboard system failures. Repair costs? Often exceeding $1,500 / €1,400.• ❌ Environmental "Throw-away" Culture: iCarsoft and its distributors refuse to sell spare parts (like screens) for devices costing hundreds or thousands of dollars. They force you to scrap the whole unit instead of a simple repair. It’s intentional overconsumption.My Professional Advice: Be extremely careful what you plug into your car’s "brain." What looks like a professional tool is a low-quality gateway with zero local support.Don't be fooled by the polished Amazon listing. This is a "Mister Bearnaise" facade.

My experience with Amazon in England has been extremely disappointing. Since arriving for a long stay, I have relied on Amazon for basic essentials, and it has been nothing short of a nightmare.There have been repeated shipping delays, drivers ignoring clear delivery instructions, and a noticeable lack of effective customer service channels. Even more frustrating is the inability to leave meaningful feedback after items have been delivered, which removes accountability from an already broken process.The final straw was an order advertised as next day delivery. It was delayed with an updated delivery date several days later, only to then arrive unexpectedly after 10 pm. The delivery driver repeatedly pressed the buzzer, waking the entire household, despite detailed delivery instructions clearly outlining building access and where the package should be left. The interaction was rude and completely unprofessional.People often say Amazon is too big to fail, but judging by the volume and consistency of poor feedback, customers should seriously reconsider where they spend their money. Convenience is no longer a valid excuse when service quality has declined this badly. I cannot say the end is near for Amazon, but the cracks are large and increasingly hard to ignore.

review_title: I wanted to buy one book
review_text: I wanted to buy one book. Instead, Amazon tried to turn it into a data-harvesting exercise.For a simple, low-value item shipped to the EU, Amazon demanded government ID and aggressively pushed me to store my card details. This is neither legally required nor reasonable. Czech/EU customs do not require personal ID for importing a book, and storing payment details for a one-off purchase is completely unnecessary.This feels less like compliance and more like corporate paranoia and data hoarding. A retailer should sell products, not pressure customers to surrender sensitive documents and financial data “just in case.” Under GDPR, data minimization is a core principle - Amazon’s process ignores it.Plenty of EU retailers manage to sell books without copying passports or nudging customers into permanent payment storage. Amazon’s rules are overreaching, intrusive, and customer-hostile, especially for people who value privacy.I cancelled the order and bought elsewhere - easily, safely, and without being treated like a risk profile instead of a customer.

Despite contacting customer support multiple times, no effective resolution was provided. There was no compensation offered, no concrete resolution, no coordination with the seller to address the issue, and no complaint reference or status update was shared.If customer support is unable to offer timely resolutions, transparency, or any form of customer benefit, it raises serious concerns about the purpose and effectiveness of the support system itself.

I rarely write reviews, but my experience with Amazon customer service has been so frustrating that I feel I need to warn others. I ordered a Chemical Guys spray and a cloth, and when it arrived, the bottle was cracked—likely damaged during shipping. Naturally, I contacted Amazon right away. They initially offered a refund, but I asked if it could just be replaced instead. They said yes, and I explained that I wouldn’t be able to return the leaking bottle. I even offered to empty it or clean it if necessary.Amazon assured me this wouldn’t be a problem, and that I could receive a free replacement without returning the damaged items. I felt relieved and thought the issue was resolved.A little while later, I received an email saying I needed to return the items. Confused, I reached out again, and they apologized, telling me to disregard the email. I assumed everything was settled, and I continued with my day.Fast forward two months, and I was shocked to see that I had been charged for the “free” replacement. I contacted customer service again, which turned into a grueling two-hour back-and-forth. Multiple representatives and even supervisors came and went, and in the end, they refused to refund me, despite my having clear proof of all previous conversations confirming that I was not supposed to return the damaged bottle.In short, I ended up paying twice: once for the original damaged order, and again for the replacement of the same damaged item. I followed all of Amazon’s instructions and acted in good faith, yet I was still left out of pocket.This experience has been extremely frustrating and disappointing. Their policies may seem helpful on paper, but in practice, their customer service is disorganized, inconsistent, and unreliable. If you have the option, I would strongly suggest avoiding Amazon for anything where customer support may be required.

review_title: Easy way to shop, difficult way to seek a solution!
review_text: Good platform for price checking but not a good platform to buy.Resolution for problems are limited and defined by “the system”. Human agents are trying their best but restricted to one option only: REFUND (after return).I bought a 3D booknook, for those who aren’t familiar, imagine a 1000piece puzzle. I assembled 99% of it, final instruction is to connect the electronics to light it up. Found out the sensor does not work on the thickness of the material…Amazon offered a full refund after I sent it back, imagine if the 1000th puzzle piece is missing and amazon wants you to send the whole thing back just to get you that 1piece?As Amazon is a 3rd party distributer, I assume they are unable to send out individual parts such as: missing parts, faulty elements. That’s why a full refund is required (so they can send it back to the manufacturer for replacement).In the case of a puzzle piece like this, a return is not feasible. If a small part is faulty or missing, we can’t just send the whole thing back for them to replace.Now I’m stuck with a light-less booknook which takes away so much of the charm. I’m too attached to throw it away as I’ve spent days on it.Amazon may provide an “easy” platform to buy, but in the case that something is faulty… they can’t do much for you. Shop with the main manufacturer will save you much headache.Amazon is the definition of making a rod for your own back. Easy today, difficult tomorrow.

review_title: Amazon Executive Relations: $149 Promised Refund Withheld by Account Lock
review_text: I am writing this 1-star review because Amazon Executive Customer Relations has failed to honor a documented refund promise. My account was locked immediately after Amazon issued a $99 refund and a $50 apology concession. I have verified my identity via phone multiple times from the number on my account. Despite being told by a phone agent that the account was 'unlocked' and that a manual refund of the $149 was being pushed to my debit card (ending in 7415), the money never arrived. Every order I try to place with the $99 credit is automatically canceled, proving the account is still restricted. I have recorded the phone calls where these promises were made. I am requesting that an Executive Specialist manually drain the balance to my original payment method today. I will not stop escalating until the funds Amazon acknowledged they owe me are returned. "I have been promised an account restoration and refund since December 28th. Every time I call, I am told to 'wait 24–48 hours,' yet nothing happens.“ The Executive Team is now claiming they cannot verify my identity via email because my account was created using only a phone number. This is a circular trap: they tell me to email them to verify, then reject the email because it’s not on the account. I have already verified my identity multiple times through the phone number on file, yet they refuse to honor the $149 refund ($99 balance + $50 concession) promised by agents in recorded calls. I screenshot and sent them proof of the promises by both supervisors through chat, as well and at this point I just want my refund back to my cash app card ending in 7415! I am done waiting for 24–48 hour windows that never result in action. I am requesting a manual refund today to my original payment method so I can close this non-functional account. "I have fulfilled every verification request and provided every piece of evidence. If this $149 refund to my card ending in 7415 is not processed within 24 hours, I will be forwarding my call recordings and chat screenshots to the Better Business Bureau (BBB) and filing a formal consumer complaint with the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) for withheld funds. I expect a confirmation of the refund today." Update as of today: I’ve updated the email address, and now being told to wait 72 hrs longer!

The customer service team does nothing. Each time a problem occurs and I have to speak or have a chat they always “transfer” you. It will take more than an hour to handle mistakes. Do not recommend at all.

Amazon Customer Service has evidently changed.  I used to be able to talk with a human if I really needed to.  This last time I was made to chat with a bot.  When it was unable to resolve my issue it referred me to a third party site called Just Answer.  This site popped up on the screen next to the Amazon bot and it was the only alternative presented by Amazon to speak with a live person.  The Amazon bot said I would be charged $5 for the service but it would be refunded.  It required that I put in my credit card information in order to speak with a person.  This was the only alternative presented by Amazon to resolve my issue.  I required human customer service help since my issue didn’t fit into a pre-populated answer.  Amazon only offered me human help if I signed up with the Just Answer service.  Since the bot first said it was only $5 and it would be refunded I decided to do it in an effort to resolve the problem I was having with my Amazon credit card which was  why I reached out to Amazon customer service in the first place.I happened to ask the bot when I would receive the refund of the 5 dollars or any other amount I might be charged if I went ahead and signed up.  It replied I would actually be charged $65 and that I could request the refund now which I did.  Just Answer ended up referring me to a direct Amazon toll free number (below) through which I could talk with a person.  The Amazon chat bot could have done this itself but instead had told me that Just Answer was my best and only alternative and had automatically brought up the Just Answer signup screen as if this was all Amazon could do.I finally resolved my Amazon credit card issue by speaking with a live Amazon representative through this toll free number furnished to me by Just Answer: 888-283-1190.If I had not asked the Amazon chatbot for a refund before I signed up with Just Answer I don’t know if I would have ever got one or known that I would be charged $65!  Note that Amazon facilitated the refund as if this service was part of Amazon.  I’m thinking Amazon must be making money off this, as a way to charge its own customers for customer service!This entire experience seemed other worldly.  I had never had such a convoluted experience before trying to get basic human customer service from Amazon.  Amazon’s behavior seemed entirely misleading and unethical.  But this is all they offered.  I guess a company who is a monopoly can treat you however it wants.  What alternative do you have.  Leaving this review is my only way to communicate with others all the time I wasted and the misleading information Amazon gave with the horrible customer service they offered.
