review_title: Amazon’s Decline Is No Accident — It’s a Choice
review_text: Amazon is actively accelerating its own decline through an overreliance on automation, a steadily eroding customer‑service model, and a corporate posture that appears increasingly indifferent to the people who fund its success. What now passes for “support” is a labyrinth of scripted chatbots, low‑cost outsourced labor, and intentionally obscured contact pathways that make reaching a competent human representative nearly impossible. This is not an operational oversight; it is a deliberate strategy.Attempts to escalate legitimate concerns through the once‑publicized executive email channels — including jeff@am... and primary@am... — now routinely result in automated rejection notices. The message is unmistakable: Amazon has no interest in hearing from its customers unless they are silently completing transactions. The company has insulated itself behind layers of bureaucracy and automation, creating a system in which accountability is not merely absent but structurally avoided.For years, Amazon has relied on the complacency of consumers conditioned to prioritize speed over scrutiny. The promise of rapid delivery has served as a convenient distraction from the steady degradation of service quality, transparency, and basic respect for the customer. But convenience is not a substitute for competence, and it certainly does not excuse neglect. As more consumers begin to recognize the widening gap between Amazon’s marketing narrative and its actual practices, the company’s dominance will inevitably erode.If Amazon continues to prioritize cost‑cutting over customer care, opacity over accessibility, and automation over accountability, its decline will not be speculative — it will be inevitable. The company is not merely failing to meet the standards it once set; it is actively dismantling them. Consumers would be wise to reconsider whether the illusion of convenience is worth the reality of being treated as an afterthought.

review_title: Over a Month Waiting for Refund – Lost Item & Terrible Customer Service

What followed was one of the worst customer service experiences I have ever had.Because the order was placed using an old email address I can no longer access, I asked Amazon for help. Instead of offering a solution, I was told there was “nothing that could be done” and that another department would contact me. No one ever did.After days of stress, I eventually discovered myself (through online research and tutorials) that I could easily change my email address—something Amazon support could have told me immediately. This lack of guidance and accountability is unacceptable.I then provided my ID as requested, yet more than one month later, I am still waiting for my refund. There has been no apology, only delays and extra steps while Amazon continues to hold my money.I have spent nearly £1,000 with Amazon, but this experience has completely destroyed my trust. Unless this refund and fair compensation are processed immediately, this will be my last purchase.Very disappointing and unacceptable service.

review_title: Amazon’s review system is a waste of…
review_text: Amazon’s review system is a waste of time. This creates a one-sided system where customers absorb the risk but are silenced when things go wrong.Transparency is being sacrificed, and the credibility of Amazon’s marketplace suffers as a result.

review_title: The worst Christmas present from AMAZON
review_text: My husband wanted to buy a PlayStation for Christmas time, so we can have a game or two for New Year's to celebrate. He wanted to use my profile, as I am a Prime Member, but wanted to attach his debit card. Once he placed the order, he realised he forgot to attach his debit card, so he cancelled the order and placed it again.At this point, all of a sudden, the account was locked and logged off, and he couldn't access it. He works night shifts, so he couldn't sort this out at the time.On 25th at 4.38 am, I received an email stating that my account will be closed due to suspicious activity, and all orders will be cancelled, and afterwards the account will be deleted. Obviously, I am surprised as my husband is, and we try to call customer service. They advise us to wait 24 hours for the Account department to send us an email with steps on how to recover access, but that email never came.From the 26th onwards till the 29th, every day we contact customer service explaining we received nothing and are asking why it is taking so long, and will the orders we made be cancelled, really, or is this a system error? The representatives tell us every day to rest assured that they will solve our issue, and we need to wait for 24 hours for that email, which never comes, and they are being super empathetic only in words, but no resolution is made whatsoever.Today, on the 29th of December, I tried to call another representative to follow the state of the situation, and the lady advised me to delete my account and create a new one. I am obviously puzzled and surprised, and am asking her what happened to the situation of sorting the account, to which she hesitates to answer and advises me to delete the account and create a new one, as it is faster than waiting. I am asking her if it is guaranteed that my 6 months free membership will be saved, and she tells me to stay on the phone as they will solve this while we go along. I asked her to send me an email with proof that this will be the case, and she replied that she can't do that, and a phone conversation is the only way.Obvioulsy I say I don't trust that, and she said she can't help me if I don't trust her.I asked at this point for my husband to step in, and he is extremely surprised and annoyed, as he has been a customer of Amazon for almost 10 years. He calls customer service, is asked to check the account via an email sent, but he doesn't see any email sent. He explains the representative the issue, and she asks him to wait for another 24 hours.Today is the 29th of December, and we finally got an email with suggestions on how to recover the account, but some of the steps do not exist on the website at all.At this point, we are left with no Amazon account whatsoever, and the only thing we can do is wait, as Customer Service does nothing to change this issue.I believe this is the worst Christmas I have ever had due to so many orders and gifts being done via this platform, but we can't have that luxury, because the Account Team doesn't consider it important for somebody like us to have a festive season.Very disappointed and surprised with such treatment from such a reputable company.I will definitely try to complain elsewhere, in the hope that the people who are really responsible at Amazon can hear this out.Hope Anyone reading this had a better experience with Amazon and won't go for this issue ever.

review_title: Returns to Amazon Italy not reimbursed! Pure thieving and breaking of EU LAW
review_text: Returned an item to Amazon Italy in September. Amazon confirmed they received the return and asked for a copy of the return postage receipt (France to Italy), which I provided.Nearly three months later, I am still being told that the returns department has “not yet processed” the item. There is no way to move past this block in their system, despite repeated follow-ups.This appears to be a systemic failure in Amazon Italy’s returns process.—Ho restituito un articolo ad Amazon Italia nel mese di settembre. Amazon ha confermato di aver ricevuto il reso e mi ha chiesto una copia della ricevuta di spedizione del reso (dalla Francia all’Italia), che ho fornito.A distanza di quasi tre mesi, continuo a ricevere la stessa risposta: il reparto resi “non ha ancora elaborato” l’articolo. Non esiste alcun modo per superare questo blocco nel loro sistema, nonostante i numerosi solleciti.Sembra trattarsi di un problema sistemico nella gestione dei resi di Amazon Italia.—J’ai retourné un article à Amazon Italie en septembre. Amazon a confirmé avoir reçu le retour et m’a demandé une copie du justificatif d’envoi du retour (de la France vers l’Italie), que j’ai fournie.Près de trois mois plus tard, on me répond toujours que le service des retours « n’a pas encore traité » l’article. Il est impossible de dépasser ce blocage dans leur système malgré de nombreuses relances.Il s’agit clairement d’un dysfonctionnement systémique du processus de retours d’Amazon Italie.

review_title: I ordered a hygrometer to maintain my expensive cigars
review_text: I ordered a hygrometer to maintain $400.00 worth of cigars I purchased online. I made myself available today to receive the item, which was to be delivered today. I set the timer on my phone to alert me every 10 minutes, so that I could check for notification of delivery, as the item I purchased was 'Out for Delivery'. While eating lunch, I did notice on my laptop, a notification of delivery on the Amazon website before the timer alert on my phone sounded, so I estimated that delivery was made 5 to 6 minutes prior. I went downstairs to pick it up, as usual, but no package was there. There was no usual photo presented with the notification on the website. I also did not get a text notification on my phone. So, I called Amazon. Their Gateway Robot would not allow me to speak with an agent. Instead, it suggested that I "check with my neighbors to see if someone picked it up by mistake", AS IF I am going to run around to 20 condo units in my building and question them as to whether on not they have my Amazon package! My expectation is that Amazon respects my request to have my bell rang by the driver so that I can answer the bell, verify it's Amazon, buzz them in, so that they can leave the package inside the security door, and I'd come down right away and get it. They used to do that regularly, which eliminated these kinds of problems, but they stopped ringing the bell. So, this screw up is on Amazon, and not on me! They do not seem to want to accept responsibility for their own mistakes and instead, they expect the customer to fix what they screwed up! Finally, I did try the Chat Feature to ask What If the driver does deliver it today. How would I know, since the system says it's been delivered? The answer was "Wait until tomorrow and if it's not delivered by 4pm, a refund may be requested." Well, that is not a solution to fix the problem today. That is DODGING the problem and hoping it will go away! In addition, if I can't get a human on the phone for help NOW, how can I get someone on the phone LATER to give me a refund!? Needless to say, I am irate over the bungling of this simple transaction, and I'm thinking I need to search for companies who take responsibility of their mistakes by resolving problems they cause as quickly as possible instead of showing the indifference that it takes to suggest that customers fix what they screwed up!!!

review_text: Amazon recently added a new feature that was usually only used with Amazon Fresh, which is the blue add item to order button. It's pretty useful to get items earlier, or get better pricing without having to place a larger order.Unfortunately there is a problem with it after they updated their application.  It will add the item or items, and select a seller other than Amazon without telling the buyer, which almost turned disastrous! I used this feature for same day shipping, when purchasing several nintendo titles. Shipment came, but not the games I paid for. As soon  as the shipment was marked delivered,  the games that were supposed to be in that box updated to (delayed, new date 12/28). How can something marked as shipped with order, suddenly not be in it? They never shipped to begin with. An issue with Amazon because they use those extra funds to buy hot stock items, then either ship very late or refund weeks later. In this case the date changed today to January 6th. No automated apologies,  nothing. It was when I went to cancel that I discovered Amazon without my permission,  selected one of their worst sellers with 20% complaints. They not only had a bad reputation of selling damaged goods and blaming it on the delevery,  they had every red flag warning that no sane person  would choose them, unless desperate. Their rules are they record keys and serials, which means they open the product, or possibly sell used as new. Cancel, you risk losing 10 to 50% restocking fee. Amazon at least canceled that part of the order, and I'll update the stars once I. Verify full refund.

review_title: amazon.com, The big company had no record
review_text: I purchased this GoveeLife space heater last year. After I already owned it, the product was later recalled, but I was never directly notified. Even before the recall, it struggled to heat the room properly, and eventually it stopped working completely.When the heater failed, I had to urgently order a replacement because I couldn’t be without heat. I scheduled another heater to arrive on Sunday since I’d be home to receive it. When the recalled unit fully went out, I contacted Amazon to see if the order could be expedited. I was told it couldn’t be, which forced me to place an additional same-day delivery order just to stay warm.During a customer service chat, I explained that the heater that failed was later recalled and originally purchased from Amazon. The agent asked for the model and purchase date and told me I qualified for a refund. I specifically asked for the refund to be issued to my current card on file, not the original payment method, since that card is no longer valid. The agent confirmed this.It has now been 12 days and I have not received the refund. When I contacted Amazon again, I was told there was no record of the original customer service interaction, which is extremely frustrating. I have email records, yet Amazon claims there’s no trace of the chat where the refund was approved.Between the heater failing, the lack of direct recall notification, and Amazon not honoring a confirmed refund, this experience has been unacceptable. I canceled my Prime membership because of it. The issue isn’t just the product, it’s the poor follow-through and customer service breakdown.I would not recommend this heater or relying on Amazon support when problems arise.

review_title: Amazon ruined my Christmas – Wasted my time, money, and ignored my ADHD
review_text: ​I am beyond frustrated with the level of incompetence I’ve experienced. I ordered a Christmas gift for my mum for one-day delivery, which was delayed. On Tuesday, a driver turned up at my house empty-handed, telling me he had no parcel and that it was still at the depot.​I spoke to customer services numerous times every single day. After the failed Tuesday delivery, I was told I could pick it up myself. The agent gave me the address and said they were expecting me. When I arrived at the depot, I was able to just walk right through the main gate, but was met with even more hostility. The man working at the makeshift reception was incredibly rude and arrogant, making an already tense situation much worse. I was told they don't allow collections and that the parcel was actually marked as MISSING.​To make matters worse, the final customer service representative I spoke with was completely dismissive. I explained that I have ADHD and needed him to slow down so I could process the information, but he showed zero consideration or patience. To top it off, he offered a "goodwill" voucher of just £5. I pointed out that the wasted journey to the depot alone cost me £10 in fuel, but he didn't care.​Because of this failure, I had to leave for the North to spend Christmas with family without the gift. It arrived on Wednesday after I had already left, meaning my mum now has no Christmas present from me. I was flat-out lied to by Amazon support, wasting my time and money. Their total lack of empathy or accessibility for customers with ADHD is unacceptable. Amazon’s logistics and support are a total shambles.

⭐ 1 Star – Delivery Promise Was a ScamThis order was placed because the product page clearly stated that it would arrive 13 days before Christmas, which at the time of purchase meant delivery in around five days. That delivery promise was the only reason I placed this order.I am now sitting here on Christmas Eve with no product. After taking my money, the delivery date suddenly changed to 27th January.This is a scam.Advertising pre-Christmas delivery, taking payment, and then quietly pushing the delivery date back by over a month is not a “delay” — it is a delivery scam. The promise of delivery before Christmas was used to secure the sale, knowing full well that customers are shopping for Christmas presents.Had the real delivery date been shown from the start, I would never have placed this order. Instead, the product page showed a pre-Christmas delivery window, which now looks like nothing more than a bait-and-switch scam.This feels like a scam designed to exploit Christmas shoppers. The information shown on the product page was misleading at best and a scam at worst.Avoid this listing. Avoid this seller. If timing matters, this is a scam.

review_title: I have been an Amazon customer for…10 plus year
review_text: I have been an Amazon customer for years, but this recent experience has completely changed how I view their customer service and accountability.My Echo Show 8 began placing unauthorized purchases on its own — not once, but six separate times. I personally witnessed the device announce a purchase without anyone speaking to it. I followed every security step Amazon suggested, including password resets, two-step verification, and reviewing all connected devices. Despite this, the device continued to malfunction.One of the unauthorized purchases went through and withdrew money I needed to pay a bill, creating real financial hardship. I provided proof of the charge and even allowed Amazon tech support to remotely access my device.After two weeks of back-and-forth, Amazon still failed to take responsibility for the malfunction. Instead of a thorough investigation or meaningful resolution, I received contradictory information, was subtly made to feel at fault, and was even told incorrect information about my warranty — despite having a valid 2-year protection plan through 2026.What was most frustrating is that not everyone is financially stable. Some of us live paycheck to paycheck. I am a single parent with two children, and I cannot afford money to just disappear from my account unexpectedly. Losing funds without authorization is not “minor” — it affects real people in real ways.In the end, Amazon offered a $50 gift card, which felt less like accountability and more like an attempt to quietly move me along without addressing the malfunction, the financial impact, or the stress this caused. There was little acknowledgment of how disruptive and upsetting this experience was.For a company this large, this level of carelessness and lack of empathy was extremely disappointing. No customer should have to fight this hard for basic support — especially when a device malfunctions and directly impacts their finances. This experience has seriously damaged my trust in Amazon and its products.

review_title: I am beyond frustrated with the…
review_text: I am beyond frustrated with the consistently negligent behavior of Amazon delivery drivers. Time and time again, they fail to perform the most basic requirement of a delivery, knocking on the door. Instead, parcels are either abandoned at the side of the porch, approximately five meters from a main road, or casually dumped over a side fence as if they were worthless.This is not a secluded countryside lane. This is a visible and accessible area where anyone could steal these packages within seconds. The complete disregard for security, common sense, and customer property is astonishing. What is the point of ordering items online if they are treated with such indifference once they reach the final step of the journey?No knock. No doorbell. No attempt to ensure the delivery is actually received. Just a notification hours later informing me that my parcel has been delivered, when in reality it has been abandoned.At this point, it feels less like a delivery service and more like a liability. Customers should not be expected to constantly monitor their front property like a security guard just to ensure their purchases are not stolen due to driver laziness or rushed targets.Amazon prides itself on efficiency and customer satisfaction, yet this repeated failure at the delivery driver level completely undermines that claim. Until basic accountability and professionalism are enforced, this aspect of the service deserves nothing more than one star, and that is being generous.

If they want business, they have to earn my respect back again, because for months now I've been dealing with the same crappy service and nothing improves with them.As a footnote, Amazon have started to outsource their deliveries to third -rate couriers who don't do their jobs properly, if at all. Most of their delivery drivers are careless idiots who have zero respect for the needs of their customers. Nothing improves with this company. I will be purchasing items at retail stores from now on.

Amazon has lost multiple packages of mine recently, and their response has been consistently awful. Every time, I’m sent to a chatbot that insists my package was “delivered” and points to a photo taken by the driver—except the photo is clearly of someone else’s house.When I report this, Amazon doesn’t investigate. Instead, they tell me to do my own investigation—check around, wait a set number of days, then come back at a specific time to confirm that the package is still missing. I already know my package is missing. That’s why I contacted support in the first place. Amazon should be the one investigating delivery failures, not pushing that responsibility onto the customer.The result is that I’m now taking time out of my day to do Amazon’s work, following up on their timelines, just to get basic resolution for a problem they created. There’s no accountability, no real human support, and no urgency to fix repeated delivery mistakes.For a company this large, the customer service experience has become shockingly bad. Lost packages are frustrating enough, but being brushed off by automation and then asked to prove—again—that the package never arrived has completely eroded my trust in Amazon.

review_title: If I could give a 0 for a Review for…
review_text: If I could give a 0 for a Review for Amazon Customer Service I would. My daughter purchased an Apple IPhone a just over a month ago through Amazon. The phone just stopped working for no reason. My daughter had not long set up an account with Amazon and could not remember her password. We tried to reset it but it kept locking us out. We have contacted Amazon Customer Service just about every night this week as it is not letting us reset the password. It keeps saying too many attempts. We keep getting told to wait another 24 hours. We contacted Amazon yet again tonight, we were on the phone for over an hour but yet again to no avail. Firstly they tried to send us a code to her mobile number, to be told numerous times that this is not going to work as her phone is not working. Finally 40 minutes later after being passed from pillar to post at least 3 times they said we have reset her password so she should be able to reset it. I managed to access her emails on my phone to try and reset the password. No, same thing yet again. All we are trying to do is access the returns label to print it off to return the phone back to Amazon. We asked them to send the returns label to her dad’s account but they said no they could not do that. After nearly 1 1/2 hours the Customer Service man was really rude and said that we would have to call them back tomorrow. The phone is a used one but was still £300, all we want to do is return it to get our money back. Amazon is a multi billion company who are quick enough to take your money but not quick to rectify their crap goods and to a leave a poor student in the lurch in a big City.

Never ever give delivery instructions or provide a safe place to any courier company on a web site or app, especially Evri, Amazon, Hermes etc. if you do you are accepting delivery without you being present. You will have no claim for replacement or refund from the SELLER who is totally responsible until the parcel is in your arms. Amazon took a false photo yesterday of an alleged delivery at my address. The photo was not the parcel that was alleged by them to be delivered on their website. Speaking to customer services is a waste of time. You will go through to a call centre in India, have a unintellgible conversation in English (usually) and find they cannot access any photos of your parcel being delivered by the Amazon Courier. They cannot record your audible call and they cannot make any note or written record of your call. Amazon now are barely better than the worst Evri and its going to get far worse over the Xmas period.  No wonder £1.6 billion pounds of parcels were stolen from doorsteps in the last 12 months. Worse, because the Black Friday promotion has ended, the item is now €140 more expensive.When I asked them to honor the original price or provide a credit for the difference so I could re-order, they refused. review_title: Amazon is refusing my refund despite providing an official police report
review_text: I am extremely disappointed with how Amazon has handled my missing delivery case. Amazon claims my items were delivered and even shows a photo of the package at my door. However, when I arrived home, only one of the three packages was actually there. The other two — a PlayStation 5 Digital Edition and Apple AirPods Pro 2 — were missing.I contacted Amazon immediately, and they told me that to get a refund, I must file a police report.So I did exactly that.I contacted Greater Manchester Police, they investigated the situation, confirmed the packages were missing/stolen, and issued an official Crime Report (CRI/06KK/0030787/25).I submitted this report to Amazon multiple times — in PDF, in images, even through Google Drive links — and Amazon keeps rejecting it automatically, claiming “missing information” even though every required detail is clearly in the official document.I even contacted the police again, and they confirmed in writing that the report is valid, official, correctly issued, and includes all required information. I sent THIS confirmation to Amazon as well — and they still refused it.At this point, it is obvious Amazon is simply trying to avoid refunding my stolen items by hiding behind automated responses and refusing to acknowledge official documents from law enforcement.I have wasted weeks sending documents, chatting with support agents, repeating myself to bots and human agents, and receiving the same copy-paste rejection emails.This is unacceptable, unethical, and feels like Amazon is intentionally blocking my refund.I will be reporting Amazon’s behavior to the appropriate regulatory authorities, including consumer protection agencies.Avoid ordering expensive items from Amazon — if something goes wrong, they make YOU responsible for their delivery mistakes.

review_title: Unreliable, Insecure Delivery Service – Theft Risk is High
review_text: My experience with Amazon's delivery service has been extremely disappointing and resulted in the loss of my package.Vague Delivery Times: The delivery notification only provided a single day, forcing me to wait indefinitely. Unlike other major carriers, there was no estimated time window, pre-delivery call, or text notification, making delivery coordination impossible.Highly Insecure Drop-off: The delivery driver left the package unattended in the common entrance hallway of my apartment building. This area is accessible to anyone and is not a secure location. This negligence directly led to the package being stolen.Unhelpful Delivery Confirmation: The driver noted the package was left with a 'resident.' In a large apartment block, this information is useless and does not provide proof of safe delivery. When the item went missing, I was advised to 'check with neighbors' and 'wait a few days,' which is an inadequate response to confirmed theft caused by insecure placement.The delivery system is flawed, prioritising speed over security. If you live in a multi-unit dwelling, be aware that your package may be left vulnerable to theft. Amazon needs to urgently update its protocols to include secure placement, customer contact, and specific delivery windows.

Amazon has effectively established a monopoly, delivering millions of parcels every day, yet showing little regard for the customers who sustain it. The customer service is perfunctory at best; while they do respond, the agents appear trained more to frustrate than to assist. The company employs some of the poorest couriers, who frequently leave parcels unattended, and obtaining a refund in such cases becomes almost impossible. Instead of taking responsibility, Amazon diverts customers with automated emails instructing them to report the item as stolen, even when it was left in a public or communal area.Returns are equally problematic. Items are sometimes not registered upon arrival, leading to customers being charged again for products they have already sent back. Amazon behaves this way because it knows people will continue to buy from them: it is quick and convenient, even when identical items can be found elsewhere,  such as on Temu,  for a fraction of the price. In many cases, Amazon charges ten times more for the same cheaply manufactured goods.It is time for consumers to reconsider their reliance on this corporate giant, which prioritises profit over people. Despite spending £6,000 on Amazon last year and returning fewer than 7% of my purchases, I still received an email warning that my account might be closed due to “excessive returns”. This demonstrates precisely how little the company values its customers.

review_text: I purchased an electric standing desk that failed in under six months with very light use. After I accepted the refund without addressing the review suggestion, the seller emailed my personal account and told me to change the review first before they would send the refund. This violates Amazon’s policies on review manipulation and off-platform communication.I reported everything to Amazon with screenshots. The first chat agent said the issue would be escalated, but I never received any follow-up. In a second chat, I was transferred through multiple representatives, had to resend screenshots, and repeatedly heard that the “return window had closed” — even though this was not a standard return issue. review_title: The Amazon Vine Uses YOU to Write Off Full Retail
review_text: While my experience with Amazon has been superior to Tamu, Light in the Box and others who pedal Chinese products. I want to warn you about Amazon/vine and the DEBT being TRANSFERRED  through this vine TO YOU. The Vine allows Amazon to write off products at full Retail value by giving you product "for services" that IRS recognizes as TAXABLE INCOME.Above and beyond the fact people are getting paid to write reviews that get rejected if they are critical or "racist" ("made in China" is racism), I feel like I have been party to a money laundering scheme. I even had a former IRS agent tell me flat out, this sounds like money laundering, and YOU are party to it.You see, INSTEAD of WRITING OFF the WHOLESALE value of unsellable items (perhaps items that have been returned in unsellable condition) they pay people "viners" "for services" by giving them these products which the IRS considers as cash payment at the RETAIL VALUE of the item.Not only does this mean Amazon can now write off the full RETAIL VALUE (around twice as much) the viner has to pay Income Tax on the full retail value of the item. I wrote to Amazon several times without reply about this. I called customer support and the person had never even heard of Amazon Vine. (I tried to return defective items and the agent always says, "Sir, I cannot give you a refund or replacement because you did not pay for this item." I found that if I insist on returning something without replacement or refund, I was able to return items for a ZERO DOLLAR RETURN. but it did not change the amount shown on the running total listed in my account under form 1099. In other words, Amazon just got something back they paid nothing for and can once again write off for full retail value. I don't know if it is money laundering, but it is one heck of a money making scheme. Amazon has tons of stuff that doesn't sell that they have to store, move, and return to make room for more product. They have turned this wholesale loss into Retail Profits.BTW, after a review is posted, you cannot return the item because it is "used." It wasn't "used" by the person who put it back in the box improperly or without some of the parts, but after you reviewed the item, it becomes used. You cannot return it. You are stuck with it and you have to pay INCOME tax.I was told by Amazon at sign up, "You MIGHT have to pay tax on some of these items." I thought they meant SALES TAX or USE TAX. I did not get a paper 1099 EVER. So how would I know? They posted one in my "account" at Amazon.com at the end of the full year telling that I would likely have to pay around 20% (which there is no way they could possibly know what other income I might have which would greatly affect the amount of tax I owe on ALL of my income back to the first penny I earned that year). BTW, If I were to sell this stuff (as used because I had to use it to review it) I have to pay income tax on the full amount including shipping and commissions or other fees. $216k in product paid to me and reported to the IRS as "payment for services" which the IRS records as CASH MONEY paid as Income.As the year was coming to an end, I increased the fervor of my attempt to get information. About two weeks into this, I got a notice while I was writing reviews that said, "We have noticed irregularities in your reviews. All of your reviews have been removed and you are no longer allowed to review items. You will no longer be able to select items. I never got an explanation of the irregularities.I would caution the general public about the reviews on Amazon. As you will find in the reviews here on Trustpilot, people get banned from writing reviews without being told why they are removed.. Even saying "lower quality made in China product" would get the review rejected as "racist." and writing a few more reviews like this will get you banned, and they do not tell you why. Even when they reject your reviews the first time they are often so vague about it you have to guess what they want removed and a lot of reviewers just remove anything negative in order to save time.To the people being solicited for the vine, I caution you. I read warnings from people going back several years and I thought I was better and smarter than they were. But when you consider that once you review the item it is considered to be "used." You cannot return it and you cannot sell it as new. Any warranty will be rendered null and void by transfer to another person.And when you realize you have to pay INCOME TAX (between 20 and 30 percent) at full retail value when you get paid with this item , and you pay taxes (between 20 and 30 percent) on the full price you sell it for PLUS tax on shipping and commissions and fees. All this is income to the IRS. But when you take all this into account (and there is more) when you get to the point where you pay Amazon their fees and any advertising you do, there is nothing left

review_title: Amazon couldn't care less about its…
review_text: Amazon couldn't care less about its customers.  It just wants their money.  I ordered this item on the understanding that I would receive it today.  I received an email saying it had been delivered when it hadn't.  The person I spoke to on the phone wasn't helpful and why do BRITISH customers paying BRITISH money have to talk to someone from INDIA.  BRITISH customers should have a BRITISH call centre staffed by BRITISH people irrespective of their ethnic origins. That just adds insult to injury.  Anyway, that person said that the item had been delivered to Number 5 which is two doors away from me (number 3).  That person was so unhelpful that, despite having severe mobility issues, I had to drag myself down my street to number 5.  No one was in but there were two parcels from Amazon left on the open door step which anyone could steal.  Neither parcel was for me.  When I placed my order, which included delivery today, a legal contract came into being which Amazon has broken but huge companies don't care about legalities, they just do what they want.  Why should I have to wait until Amazon can be bothered to sort this mess out?I decided to ring Amazon again.  This time, still with an INDIAN call centre, I was given a different story.  This person was claiming that the item had been marked as delivered but not actually delivered and was still with the driver.  Amazon themselves don't know what is going on.  It's absolutely pathetic and I shall do my best to avoid Amazon in the future.  They have come into the UK undercutting other retailers until they got a stranglehold then started putting their prices up having got rid of most of the competition.  It's a repeat of what the Japanese did to the British motorcyle industry but just on a much, more massive scale.  Amazon will now be my last place to look to buy anything and I hope many others follow suit.Shop local in real shops staffed by real people who really care about their customers.

review_title: Months-Long Lockout and Incompetent Customer Service: Access to Lifetime Photos Denied
review_text: I have contacted support via phone and chat several times, and each interaction follows the same broken pattern:Initial Triage: A representative attempts basic troubleshooting (e.g., "reset your password," "clear your cache"), which fails every time as the issue is an account-level lock.Escalation Promise: I am told a high-priority ticket is being created or escalated to the "specialist team" (often referred to as the Drive or Photo team), with a promise of a resolution in 24–48 hours.The Dead End: The promised callback or email never materializes, or I receive a boilerplate, unhelpful email that merely restates the TOS violation without specifying what violation occurred, or how to fix it. Subsequent calls confirm that the previous tickets were either closed without action or are stuck in a queue with no internal visibility.The core issue is that I cannot access or download years of family memories. Amazon Photos has failed its primary purpose as a reliable cloud storage service.WARNING TO POTENTIAL USERS: Do not trust Amazon Photos with irreplaceable data. When an account lock occurs, there is no effective customer service path to regain access, and you risk losing access to your files indefinitely. The support agents are unable to resolve account-level issues, leaving customers in permanent limbo. I urge Amazon to address this systemic failure in their escalation process immediately.

review_title: Amazon is complicit in a service…

With the recent policy change of the USPS eliminating residential package deliveries, Amazon is fraudulently accepting payment for Prime memberships knowing that the packages can not be delivered under the advertised 2-day shipping promise. The USPS implemented this new policy due to an increasing frequency of carrier accidents when driving onto residential properties. They will not enter a driveway of a residence. Now you are required to go to the Post Office to pick up your parcels, effectively eliminating the Prime membership 2-day delivery benefit.

review_title: It’s not that I never respond
review_text: It’s not that I never respond. It’s that it is extremely frustrating how Amazon never takes responsibility for the merchandise sold on its platform and for the mistakes that are made that are not the buyer’s fault. We are Amazon’s customers… not the independent companies Amazon partners with and allows to sell through its platform. At the end of the day, we continue buying from Amazon. And we are also not responsible for the misleading way their affiliates list their products, making buyers believe that if there is an issue, they will provide a prepaid return label—only to then send a label that is not valid in the U.S., only in China, as if the product had been sold in China. This makes Amazon’s customers responsible for a return shipment that costs more than the item purchased.To me, this is called fraud, because none of that is specified at the time of purchase.Unfortunately for me, Amazon has lost credibility and trust for many reasons, including the fact that it has become a platform where its affiliates can deceive and scam Amazon customers, while Amazon refuses to take responsibility because of how their policies are structured. They make the process difficult and long until the customer gets tired of calling and asking for help, having to repeat themselves over and over again, only to finally receive an email saying that they cannot help and that the merchandise does not qualify for X reason—and now another email saying they will close the case because there was “no response.”What response were they expecting? ……This doesn’t only happen with third-party sellers; it also happens with Amazon Prime. They list a product with next-day delivery and then, once the payment is processed, they change the date, delaying productions and causing losses—because in my specific case I even use Amazon for my businesses. And I have never, in any situation, seen Amazon take responsibility for any of its mistakes, whether in logistics or in any other area. Packages delivered to the wrong address, late shipments, agreements with fraudulent third-party sellers.In the past month alone, I have ordered over $2,000 worth of products from Amazon, which I am waiting to receive so I can return them, because I do not plan to continue doing business with Amazon. My money and my businesses have no guarantee or protection on your platform, and even as a Prime member, nothing that is offered through that membership is truly guaranteed.As for the issue with the dress, I will look for a legal way to resolve it myself, because with Amazon there is never a real solution—and it’s not about the money. It’s about honesty, respect, clarity, and the security that all Amazon customers deserve.

review_title: I am writing to express my deep…DISAPPOINTMENT
review_text: I am writing to express my deep frustration and disappointment with Amazon’s service over the past three weeks. I have been a loyal Prime Member for years, but the repeated failures in delivery—especially at the Brooklyn Park, Minnesota facility—have left me questioning whether Amazon can still be considered a reliable option.On November 8, I placed a large order that included time-sensitive items I needed by November 21. Despite being shown guaranteed delivery windows, that order was delayed again and again at the Brooklyn Park facility. Ultimately, on November 15, I cancelled the order.I placed a second order on November 14, again needing them no later than November 21. Yet the exact same issue occurred: on November 17, the package arrived at the Brooklyn Park facility and then simply sat there with no movement. I cancelled that order on Friday, November 21, when it became clear the package would never arrive.Still needing additional items, I placed another order on November 21, only to see every item in that order delayed—again at the Brooklyn Park facility. Since then, I have placed seven additional orders, and most of those items are now either delayed or marked as no longer deliverable, despite being listed as available with guaranteed delivery times at checkout.In the past three weeks, I have ordered 19 items from Amazon, and only four have actually been delivered.This is unacceptable. It appears that being a Prime Member no longer carries any meaningful benefit, as guaranteed delivery dates are simply not being met. More concerning is that Amazon has taken no visible action to resolve these issues despite the clear pattern of delays tied to the same facility.Amazon has long been a dependable company, but at this point I no longer feel it can be trusted for time-sensitive or even routine orders.The only word I have left is DISAPPOINTING

This company is HORRENDOUS. The way they do business is disorganized, disrespectful, and really screws people over. To begin with, if you decide to sign up for an Amazon account subscription and you apply that to one specific bank card or account, if it's time to pay for the sub, and the payment fails for whatever reason, Amazon automatically starts shuffling through and trying to charge every bank or card linked to your account. They also don't run the charges for your purchases the same day, or even within 72 hours unless you also subscribe or pay extra for express shipping. Every order I've placed that I didn't purchase express shipping, they didn't charge my bank account until 2 to 3 days before the product was supposed to be delivered. And these products weren't supposed to arrive for 2-3 weeks! By the time they actually charge you for the product, the money is gone and you can no longer make the purchase. This has happened on multiple occasions. To be honest it appears that this company isn't at all concerned with customer satisfaction, let alone if the customer can even PURCHASE the products, and is only concerned with how they can manipulate people into funneling more money into their pockets via subscription abuse. Really disappointed in the way this company has started conducting itself. Used to be better than this. Please stop giving this company subscription money. Because the more money they generate from subs, the less they have to be concerned with service quality.

review_title: I placed an order with Amazon (US) and they did not fulfill their delivery
review_text: I placed an order with Amazon (US) and it got 'lost' in transit. They could not explain why, but suggested cancelling and re-ordering, which I did. I was given a specific delivery timeframe, but it later changed. I contacted them to ask why, and was told that it was for 'unknown' reasons, which is beyond ridiculous. I was then told that it wasn't really Amazon's fault because they were just a platform, it was down to the sellers. When I pointed out that the products were 'fulfilled by Amazon' as per the website, I was told that maybe it was something to do with the driver, or the delivery company, anyone but Amazon itself. When I was put on the line with an AI assistant, they told me it would arrive that day by 8 pm The extremely unhelpful 'real' person said that was not true, so I don't know why they let the AI say it. Ultimately their way to deal with the problem was to say "We can't do anything to help with this," and then pretend they could not hear me on the phone anymore so that they could hang up. Without doubt, the worst 'customer' service I have ever experienced. It wasn't about me as the customer at all; it was about Amazon trying to defer responsibility for their inability to provide the service I was promised. I found the whole experience shocking. Shame on Amazon. Oh, but wait, they are going to spend 3 weeks 'investigating' what happened. Thanks, I'll just wait while you 'investigate' then.

review_title: Still the best shop that could be!
I No Longer Trust Amazon — This Experience Was Absolutely Infuriating
review_text: I am genuinely shocked at how poorly Amazon handled my order. I waited almost two months for a chair that Amazon repeatedly claimed was in stock. Throughout this time, I contacted customer service many times, only to receive the same scripted, robotic replies with zero real help. On several occasions, I was even advised to “just cancel the order” — as if the inconvenience and wasted time meant nothing.It felt like Amazon was simply waiting for me to give up.Not once did anyone take responsibility or proactively update me. If I hadn’t kept pushing for answers, I would still be waiting indefinitely, believing the item was on its way.Finally, after demanding escalation, a representative assured me the order would be dispatched and created a ticket. I felt relieved for the first time — only to receive an email THAT SAME NIGHT saying my order was cancelled due to no stock. Two months of waiting, countless chats, and all along the item apparently never existed in the first place. The level of incompetence and misinformation is unbelievable.To make matters worse, I had purchased a seat cover from Amazon two months ago specifically for this chair, trusting their “in stock” updates. Now I’m left with a completely useless product and money wasted — all because Amazon kept giving me false information.This entire experience was frustrating, exhausting, and incredibly disappointing. Amazon used to be a reliable company I could trust. After this incident, I honestly don’t feel that way anymore. I will think very carefully before ever ordering from Amazon again.

review_title: never buy Apple products from Amazon
review_text: never buy Apple products from Amazon -Apple Products and Consumer Laws in the United KingdomUnder consumer laws in the UK, consumers are entitled to a free of charge repair and (depending on the circumstances) may be entitled to a replacement, discount or refund by the seller, of defective goods or goods which do not conform with the contract of sale. For goods purchased in England or Wales, these rights expire six years from delivery of the goods and for goods purchased in Scotland, these rights expire five years from delivery of the goods.When you purchase Apple hardware products, you will also receive coverage from the Apple Limited Warranty. This coverage operates alongside and in addition to your statutory rights under UK consumer law. review_title: I bought a couple of items from Amazon ( Order #111-3705065-9424225 )…
review_text: 111-3705065-9424225I bought a couple of items from Amazon US as I do on a yearly basis. These items included a Smartwatch which ( main ) button seized to function 10 days after I'd received it. I told Amazon this and they said to send it back. When trying to find the local courier through whom to send it back via, I encountered some difficulties as Amazon could ( or would ) not tell me. Instead saying they would refund me in up to 90 days. The watch cost USD36.9, sending it back at my expense costs about USD40 ( our ordinary SA Postal services are currently defunct ). The Amazon representative I was chatting to informed me she had instituted a refund request citing my circumstances and that it had been approved.I later got an official ( Amazon.com ) e-mail informing me of this and to expect my refund to my Payment Method within 5-7business days ( this was on the 3rd of November 2025 ). When I didn't receive a notification from my bank at least on the 13 November, I enquired with Amazon and their reps said to ' hold on while they look at my correspondence'. After 'looking' in less than 2minutes, they informed me I had been provided with incorrect information, even offering an 'apology'. When I asked how it was my fault that their employee made this promise, they skipped answering this question and told me 'policy'. Eventually, after much back and forth I literally spoke to the Supervisor. This lady ( I know her name ) told me 'policy' and that they wont even extend the last date I can mail the item back. Later e-mailing saying she understand that I had 'disposed the item' ( I obviously haven't, but suspect this is to deflect the fact that she would not even extend the date I can mail this 'thing' back to them ). It appears gaslighting has entered Customer Services. Sad times indeed.

They say if your order is more than a certain amount (which changes from order to order) that you will receive next day delivery free, but then your order doesn't arrive the next day all they say is it was delayed ( sometimes for several days or even weeks) with no further explanation provided. I have had orders that they said would be delivered next day and bought something unrelated later that day and to save themselves the added expense they did not deliver anything until they were ready to deliver everything in one trip (which is sometimes a few days longer). I live in a large senior mobilehome community and Prime is here every day delivering to multiple people, so it would only take the driver three minutes or less to deliver another order anywhere in this community. It makes no sense at all, unless Amazon has become so big and arrogant that they simply don't care about even trying to improve they're shoddy performance. The other thing that is really bad is I no longer can even request my deliveries to be delivered to my carport door instead of my front door which is only five feet from the street and not safe from porch pirates. Even when I had the option of side door delivery most of the drivers still left the delivery on the front steps, because they didn't care about checking customer's preferences. Before I retired I was a delivery driver and I always took care of the needs of my customers. Unfortunately most drivers now are totally unprofessional and don't consider the job as their career but only a job for now. My hope with posting is that it will against all odds wind up being seen by someone in Amazon that actually cares and will be made aware of the sad state of affairs that has befallen the company and will fix the problems before some other company rises up to take Amazon's place. It happens all the time, just ask all the many giant companies that thought they were untouchable and no longer exist, we can all think of dozens of them.

review_text: What on earth has happened to Amazon? They used to be a great company, but now they’re a complete joke.They have no idea what they’re selling anymore — I’ve had to return several items because of various issues, and every single refund has turned into a battle. They tell me there’s a problem, then when I call customer service, they insist there isn’t one and promise the refund will be processed within 14 days. Well, it’s been well over 14 days now, and I’m still waiting!The customer service is awful — rude associates who interrupt you, put you on hold without warning, or transfer you from one person to another without actually solving anything. And now they’re asking for my ID to issue a refund? They didn’t need it when I made the purchase, so why now?Amazon used to be reliable and easy to deal with, but these days, it’s just frustration after frustration.I spoke to a friend of mine who works in legal and this is what they have said over asking for ID if you're returning a regular product and there’s no issue with your payment, they should be able to process the refund without requiring an ID. If they’re insisting on one, it’s worth escalating the issue and requesting clarification on why it’s necessary. If this is happening repeatedly, it’s an RED FLAG about their customer service process.

review_title: Sold me a dangerous product resulting in amputation
review_text: I bought a play pen from Amazon Basics and it resulted in a serious injury where my dog had his toe amputated. Amazon began investigating this through their A-Z guarantee after a week of investigations due to “pain and suffering”. This absolutely never should have happened and they’re taking no accountability: even deleting my reviews on the playpen.This is my story: While I was at work, something horrific happened to Frankie (my miniature dachshund). I came home to find that his paw had become trapped between two bars of his playpen: the same one that has over 50,000 five-star reviews on Amazon. The pen, clearly not sturdy enough for a pet, had also fallen over and his toe was completely wedged.Despite its glowing reviews, I hadn’t seen the one-star reviews, many of which describe the exact same thing happening to other dogs. If I had, I never would have bought it.Frankie’s injury was severe: three bones were dislocated and displaced from his phalanges, and ultimately his toe had to be amputated. Since then, both of my dogs have become incredibly anxious and reactive. Frankie, in particular, now needs specialist training to help him cope with noises and rebuild his confidence.I am heartbroken, angry, and determined to raise awareness so this never happens to anyone else. A product meant to keep our pets safe should never cause this kind of trauma. It’s not fit for purpose, and I do not want it to remain on the market.I am currently seeking compensation, but so far has shown very little sympathy and has done nothing meaningful to address this issue in a timely or empathetic way. I urge anyone considering this product to please read the one-star reviews before buying.Take accountability and stop selling your dangerous product!!!

We ordered (using Prime) on the 4th, item due to be delivered on the 6th. At about 5 AM on the 5th, Amazon tracking said the package was still in route but may be delayed. (It was being shipped by UPS.) Since then, tracking has not changed and is showing the item as "being processed at a carrier facility." I tracked the package through the UPS site and UPS has no record of the tracking number, saying they never received the package from Amazon. I contacted Amazon last night and asked to cancel the order and refund my money. They are saying they cannot do anything for another week. They do not know where the package is, will not send out a replacement, and will not cancel and refund. The only thing they will do is sit and wait for another week. (It is also interesting to note that we ordered the exact same item a week prior. It was shipped via Amazon and there were no issues. Because we knew it wqs shipped by Amazon and nota different carrier (which seems to create more issues), we tried to order it again. Only this time they switched to using UPS as the carrier...and here we are with a lost package and Amazon refusing to do anything about it for another week. Time to shop elsewhere.

review_title: Refund Promised but Not Released – Poor Resolution Process
review_text: I was promised a same-day refund three times for a Nut Milk Maker I returned. Instead, the wrong item was refunded first, and I had to contact Amazon multiple times to correct it. I was repeatedly told the refund had been sent, but no valid ARN (Acquirer Reference Number) was provided, which means the refund has not actually been released to the card network.My bank cannot trace anything without an ARN. The only numbers I was given were internal Amazon codes, which confirms the refund has not been sent to my bank, despite agents telling me it was. I was passed from one chat agent to another, given conflicting information, and told to wait 5–7 days after being promised same-day processing.This has caused unnecessary stress and financial inconvenience, as I had to borrow money due to the delay. Customers deserve accurate information and proper handling of refunds. This should have been resolved correctly the first time.I am still waiting for the actual release of the refund funds.

If they did, they’d realize half their so-called improvements are useless and the other half just make things worse.For six years, I’ve had a family member on my account. They still get hit with shipping fees unless I place the order myself. So much for shared benefits. Prime Pantry used to be a gem, and Prime Video was decent until Amazon got greedy and started slicing up the perks.Don’t even get me started on Subscribe & Save. It’s a scam dressed as convenience. I subscribed to a few essentials during winter, only to find the prices dropped significantly in spring and summer. Did Amazon adjust my billing? Of course not. They kept charging the inflated winter rates. Now I must manually check prices every month and swap out items to avoid overpaying. And while they’ll gladly notify you when prices go up, they stay silent when prices drop. Funny how that works.Amazon has morphed into a greedy, tone-deaf giant. They should be ashamed but they’re not. This is America, after all. Greed isn’t just tolerated it’s branded.

Hey!I very rarely order from Amazon.com. As this was my 2nd time ordering. On October 12th I've ordered 6 books. 1 got delivered 2 weeks ago, 4 of them last week, but one of the books got damaged.I've contacted amazon support who said that I will be issued a free refund without having to return the book or pay additional cost. An hour later I got an email saying that I need to return the items and they will send another one.I contacted support again asking and explaining my situation. They said " the charge has been disarmed and please ignore the return label". I thanked the guy for helping and waited.On October 28th I've got an e-mail which looked like a scam. Had a {bit.ly} link and it was screaming urgency. " Immediately give us this and that information" . So I got cautious and decided to confirm the email and contacted the Hungarian UPS through their support number. They confirmed the e-mail and the number in it and told me to call that number because they can't help with it as it's not their job to solve that part.I've tried to call the hungarian UPS over 10 times and never got an answer. Wrote them an e-mail. Never got a reply. So I waited maybe they will reply by Oct 30th, which the delivery was supposed to happen.UPS changed ( at least I think they did ) the amazon order to 'attempted delivery on this day ', but no one came to ring, knock or call me to deliver it. So at this point I got in contact with amazon support again. The guy said that it will be attempted again the next day.October 31st. Again nobody came. So I contacted support again and explained my situation again from the beginning. The guy said sorry for the inconvenience and issued me a refund.Yesterday night, 3rd of November, I got charged again. They took back the refund and charged me for the book again. Contacted amazon support and they rotated me through 4 agents. They wanted me to call the US UPS and talk to them which is expensive to call them from Hungary. As far as I can tell it would be a long call and I do expect that I have to hold the line like in most support services. So I refused.Amazon support avoids and kind of responsibility and as far as I know they failed Directive 2011/83/EU — Article 18. Yet they refuse to contact UPS to clear up the address problem and at this point I can't chat because they end the chat with:"I can't offer more insight into this matter. I must disconnect this conversation now"and they just quit the chat.Now I contacted them again and the first agent said that they will refund me, but they need to hand me over to a different agent.Now they need me to return the item, otherwise they won't process the free replacement and my refund got denied.

Customer care person told like after checking, they will call to me directly, so no need to call again customer care. I waited four days, and called again customer care service, this time they are saying first we need to find product then only they can think about this issue and I asked about why you are making self return by customer, then told that its third party agreement, then I asked you should highlight this during product purchase only, he doesn't have answer for that, he simply say sorry. Mean while the product return process started back to my address due to delivery failure.I received the product back to my address and I called customer care. Customer care team asked me to return once again via different courier service, but this time I have asked for mail confirmation about refund for product and shipping charge. I got mail confirmation from Amazon and I sent it again with the different courier service.Meanwhile this time I didn't make any calls to customer care, but I got calls from customer care higher level management about this issue. Again this time seller refused to collect and I updated the same with customer care. As promised immediately they refunded the product amount and shipping charges as well.There are few good customer care people also to help us with better serviceSo better to avoid this refund/self-return process and make sure you always have Cash On Delivery. you can check your parcel and pay the money. review_title: Horrible delivery experience, Questionable customer service
review_text: Never, ever trust Amazon to deliver one of their products on time, especially if it relates to a medical need. I ordered a walker for my elderly mother on two day delivery. On delivery day, there was no notice that it would be late, only a new delivery date. I spoke to four (yes, four) Amazon customer service reps asking for more information. One told me that there was an issue in shipment. I had received no notice of this. And the product was in a local warehouse to start with. Two reps told me they put in notes to expedite the order. The walker was 30 miles away on the promised delivery day. As of , they the next day, they had moved the item 10 miles closer and it was sitting in another facility yet to be put on a truck. That was after the request to expedite delivery. Minus my four calls to customer service, I have no doubt the walker would have sat in that facility another day. I'll say it again. Never, ever trust Amazon to deliver one of their products on time, especially if it relates to a medical need. It will late and they will do nothing to let you know why. review_title: Amazon is no longer a decent marketplace
review_text: Amazon as a marketplace has gone from productive and beneficial for sellers to unproductive, highly complex web of conflicting information and terribly uneducated CSR's who don't even comprehend written policy.  3rd party sellers are required to file a Safe-T claim each time a customer returns an item that is, "customer fault."  Customer fault means the return is due to no error on part of the seller and thus the customer is responsible for shipping charges.  Amazon has recently begun a campaign of denying SafeT claims that are legitimate and fall directly under the policies on their site.  The CSR's are uneducated on their jobs and provide zero context or information regarding the claim, only a copy and past link to Amazon return policies.  The Amazon marketplace has become burdensome for sellers and if not for the sheer volume of people using it, it would fail overnight due to poorly conceived  customer biased policy that penalizes sellers for buyer abuse of the system.

Was told by amazon rep I'd get an email within 7 days with refund update, and was provided a refund request ticket ID. Called again Oct 21st as refund hadn't arrived yet. Was reassured the hold had been removed and promised the refund would be issued in 24-48 hours. Called again today , Oct 27th, now allegedly the item returns have been manually processed and have once again been promised the refund will arrive in 5-7 days. A complaint has been filed with amazon by today's rep at my request. They are holding over $2300, contrary to their own refund policy, for no reason other than gross incompetence. They did manage to refund one of the items, but not the other 9. After so many years and (conservatively) tens of thousands spent on Amazon without any real issues to speak of, this is extremely disappointing, frustrating, and unacceptable. To be fed the same line of "it's policy", despite each rep having  different info on what the policy actually states, is concerning.

review_title: Constantly Morphing Site, FRAUD, AND BAD RETURNS
review_text: Man, Amazon is a weird beast, I could crap on them for the obvious, but objectively, they're the only non-ALIEXPRESS site for all my extremely weird and niche cable requests. I am a TECH GUY, love HARDWARE, and a lot of times, have extremely random cable requests... and Amazon's the only place to find those odd cables; a multi-ended barrel connector/splitter? CHECK...The site itself gets annoying since they're always pushing a new look, layout and sometimes MASSIVELY new function, which I don't know how they can make such drastic changes over night, that within like 3 days goes back to the old layout, which gets super confusing and frustrating for us users.DELIVERY EXPERIENCE:OH BOY, this is where Amazon gets CRIMINAL. And a class action suit needs to be filed against this ACTUAL FRAUD. I'm not talking about buying from shady companies on Amazon, that I accept those risks. I'm talking about the "FULLFILLED AND SHIPPED BY AMAZON" backed products. 90% of what I buy, I intentionally buy NEW, yet, maybe 50% of the time, I'll get something with an "LPN" label on it (Loss Prevention Number), aka a RETURNED ITEM. Sometimes those returns are OPENED AND USED. That's not what you promised or what I paid for. They do this a lot. The three grossest examples was, a dog muzzle covered in fur, earBUDS (as in things that go inside your ear canal), and the grossest by far... watch straps, with other person's skin and sweat flakes on them since they were silicone banded ones.RETURNS:Also the returning process has gotten INFINITELY worse, and they've STOLEN over 5,000 USD on "LOST" or falsely claimed "NEVER RETURNED" items. So, keep them return slips from The UPS STORE if you drop off there. Then to make matters worse, the return time has also gotten infinitely longer. I returned an AC window unit, since by the time it came, I just caved in and had a new Central unit installed. BRAND NEW, IN BOX, STILL STRAPPED, had to have UPS PHYSICALLY pick it up, and still took 30 days to process that return.What I started doing, was with multiple returns and ONE BOX, printing out MULTIPLES of that QR code that they give you and taping one on each item I'm returning, since too many times have they falsely claimed and recharged me for "not returning items". WHICH, mind you, NOT A FOOLPROOF SOLUTION EITHER. As I found out. I was returning these thermal barrier curtains for doorways, since my house be a little drafty due to its age... it was two separate items with their own QR, and I was returning them since they were our friend "PREVIOUSLY RETURNED", but in the most offensive way, one had a literal RAZOR BLADE SLASH TO IT, and the other was opened missing the velcro mounting strips. I documented in pictures the process of opening the box for the first time and the product coming out of the box. That was probably the WORST fraud they committed, most of the time it's "opened and nothing more, everything still there". SO, I taped them together, put their QR CODES ON EACH INDIVIDUAL ITEM, and they had the gals to be like "YOU ONLY RETURNED ONE", I PHYSICALLY TAPED THEM TOGETHER, PUT IN A WALMART GROCERY BAG TO WATERPROOF IT, TAPED THAT CLOSED, AND THEN TAPED THE BOX CLOSED... HOLY COW... They did that with some PU Leather adhesives I bought, too, where they were multiple but one qr code. I bought like 5 rolls, and they split the order up into two, since why not be annoying... So, two Return QRS, and they're like; "YOU ONLY RETURNED 2..." The problem is with AMAZON DROP OFFS, you don't get an RMA number until the receipt print out.RETURNS ARE A NIGHTMARE, is the short of it. They lose stuff, lie, slow on refunds...If its a drop off that doesn't spit out a shipping label, you have to put your trust in the drop off point, which goes into a bin, they have this fun new scam... Where they put your drop off item in a plastic bag, and then the employee gingerly presses the label on the bag so it barely sticks and is easy to fall off. SO, always ALWAYS tape the RMA QR to the item you're shipping.I wished Amazon didn't have a monopoly on online shopping, especially for my weirder items... BUT without Radioshacks and Fry's anymore, it's hard to find my niche electronics connectors. THERE IS ZERO EXCUSE TO HOW THIS COMPANY IS RAN, and now since they OUTSOURCED customer service to India or Pakistan and the people on that end barely speak English, getting justice for the fraud they committed is nearly impossible.

I then try to find a telephone number for customer services for Amazon but unfortunately there is none you can only contact them via their app and eventually find your way through to a telephone number call back by Amazon if it does not address the issue. On the Amazon app. It did not offer me for a replacement product so instead I had to take it to a drop off point and being disabled was very difficult. I then read terms of conditions as I bought the item on my credit card and Amazon's terms for refund is 14 days from the time Amazon receive the faulty product back in addition to this another 7 days for a credit to be made and in addition to this it's stated under their policy it could take longer. This would have resulted in me paying interest to my credit card company as from the terms and conditions written by Amazon it could take 21 days or longer which is a complete and utter disgrace as they bill you the same day you purchase but when it comes to refunds it's 21 days or longer. Because I had purchased the extended warranty from the company mentioned above I also called them to say that Amazon had refunded or will should I say refund the item cost of the product but not the extended warranty and I asked the insurance company used by Amazon and sold by Amazon can they look up my details and was told the following. The insurance company mentioned above stated the following they could not find and were unable to find my order unless I gave them the Amazon order number and were unable to trace my details from their database via my name or address. I was told after a lengthy conversation that the call centre based in Portugal could only use numeric digits to find my policy which is very concerning because for my house insurance and car insurance you can find a customer's details by name and address and not just by order number. Secondly, imagine if your computer was to break down or you lost the data the insurance company supplies an email and no hard copy and if you forget your order number let's say 3 years down the road then they will not be able to find your policy and if the product goes wrong you are in quite somewhat a compromising position. This warranty offered by Amazon states that the product is covered for 3 years parts and labour accidental damage while this is true Amazon do not tell you that the product I bought is indeed covered by 2 years warranty by the manufacturer. So what you are actually buying which is not clearly stated on the Amazon website or by the third party insurer is that the manufacturer covers 2 years manufacturers warranty and what you are actually buying is 1 year manufacturer extended warranty and 3 years accidental damage. review_title: Stay away - a poor imitation of what it used to be
review_text: There as been a significant decline in quality across the various Amazon services I subscribe to, including Amazon Music, Prime Video, Kindle, and Prime Delivery. What was once a premier, customer-focused service has, in my experience, become unreliable and poor value for money.My specific grievances are as follows:Amazon Music & Prime Video: The concept of digital ownership has been completely undermined. Albums and films that I have explicitly purchased have been removed from my library without notice. Furthermore, I have experienced difficulties downloading my purchased music for use with other applications, despite holding a license. The recent introduction of advertisements to Prime Video, coupled with fee increases, is an unacceptable degradation of a paid service.Kindle Platform: While I have purchased numerous books, I am locked into the Kindle ecosystem. This restrictive Digital Rights Management (DRM) means I don't truly own the books I've paid for; I am merely licensed to view them on your proprietary platform. This makes it impossible for me to consider leaving the ecosystem without losing my entire library.Prime Delivery & Returns: The core promises of the Prime subscription are no longer being met. Same-day and 24-hour deliveries are now rarely fulfilled on time, and new fees have been introduced for services that were once standard. Deliveries are often left in insecure locations, such as over my gate, rather than at my front door. Additionally, the cost of returns has become so prohibitive that it can exceed the original price of the item.Due to this consistent failure to meet expectations and the decline in value, I will be cancelling all of my subscriptions and ceasing to use your services at the end of 2025.

review_title: Down the Amazon, without a paddle
review_text: On 14/10/25, I ordered a washing machine from Amazon, £289.00, plus £40,00 to have it installed, by UDSRetailUK. I was given a delivery time of between 0700 and 1900, on 16/10/25. There was no tracking updates after 11.41am. At 1935, there being no delivery, I cancelled the order. Being in our 80's, we don't answer the door after dark. I received a refund of £289.00. There wasn't a CANCEL option for the service charge of £40.00. Trying to contact AMAZON is proving to be difficult, if not impossible. Even trying to contact UDS, if they exist, is the same, I just get sent back to Amazon 'messaging assistant'. I sent this message; As the washer wasn't delivered, I don't need the service provided by UDSRetailUK, order No. 205-3169661-6347563. Please refund my £40.00. I received this reply; 'Sorry, I was unable to identify the seller that you would like to message or find recent orders in your account. If you still need help, please contact me through the your orders page or from the seller detail page that is available by clicking on the seller’s name in the “sold by” section of the product page.'Is there anything else I can help you with?As if they'd actually helped.I now seem to be in a loop, like something from the Twilight Zone. As a disabled 80+, and a wife with dementia, this gives me something to focus on. I shall, like Lone Waiti, in the Josey Wales film, 'endeavour to persevere'. At least, I won't waste any more money, funding a well known billionaire's lifestyle.
