Der Artikel wurde laut DPD angeblich mit meiner Unterschrift zugestellt – aber die Unterschrift wurde gefälscht. Ich habe das Paket nie erhalten.Amazon lehnt die A-bis-Z-Garantie ab, obwohl ich eindeutige Beweise für Betrug geliefert habe. Der Verkäufer verweigert jegliche Rückerstattung. DPD weist jede Verantwortung von sich. Der Artikel ist nicht mehr in meiner Bestellübersicht sichtbar, nur durch manuelle Eingabe der Bestellnummer – das wirkt wie eine gezielte Verschleierung.Ich habe eine Strafanzeige wegen Betrug (§263 StGB) und Urkundenfälschung (§267 StGB) gestellt. Auch die Verbraucherzentrale und die Schlichtungsstelle wurden informiert.⸻Title: Deliberate Fraud – Package Never Received, Forged Signature, No HelpI ordered an e-scooter from the Amazon seller dongguanshiweiqikejiyouxiangongsi. According to DPD, the package was allegedly delivered with my signature – but the signature was forged. I never received the package.Amazon rejected the A-to-Z Guarantee claim, even though I provided clear evidence of fraud. The seller refuses to issue a refund. DPD denies any responsibility. The item has disappeared from my order history and can only be found by manually entering the order number – this feels like intentional concealment.I have filed a criminal complaint for fraud (§263 StGB) and forgery (§267 StGB). The consumer protection center and the dispute resolution body have also been informed.

review_title: Restricted without Cause Unjustly Labelled and Unfairly Treated by Amazon
review_text: My Amazon account has been restricted from submitting product reviews, and all of my previous reviews have been deleted. Additionally, a red warning notice has been publicly placed on my account, falsely claiming "unusual reviewing activity." Despite contacting Amazon Support multiple times for clarification, I’ve only received automated responses accusing me of violating community guidelines—without any specific evidence or the opportunity to speak with a representative.After filing a complaint with the Better Business Bureau in May 2025, Amazon removed my previously approved A+ marketing and promotional videos. In June 2025, I was rudely informed that, due to the proprietary nature of their business, they reserve the right to remove, cancel, or edit content at their discretion. In other words, it's their platform and they can do whatever they want, when they want,  to whomever they want with no accountability.My only request at this time  that I continue to pursue,  is the removal of the unsubstantiated and defamatory written red notice placed on  the books that I have published on their platform, which everyone can see.   This defamatory label serves no constructive purpose, especially since I no longer purchase products from the platform and have been unjustly banned from leaving reviews anyway.This public defamatory statement does, however, impact the sales of my books as it implies that there is something untrustworthy  about me, my books and my  account.Defaming client/customers by publicly labelling them without proof is not reflective of good customer service and damages client/customer trust in the platform on many levels.

review_title: Amazon refuses to make good known…
review_text: Amazon refuses to make good known fraudulent charges made by someone else, probably an Amazon employee.  Amazon admits it was a fraudulent charge, but refuses to reverse the charge.   Someone hacked my Amazon.com account; all signs point to an inside job by someone with access to internal Amazon systems.   Someone, unknown to me, ordered stuff from my Amazon account charged to my credit card.  There is no reason to believe anyone else had access to my credit card number.I was forced to go to my bank and cancel that credit card for my bank to issue a refund.  That caused me many hours of work to change the credit card at every merchant I use.   Amazon should have just issued a credit for the fraudulent charge from Amazon, but did not.

review_title: Received Empty Box and No Proper Support from Amazon
review_text: I had ordered boAt earbuds from Amazon a few days ago. The delivery was handled very irresponsibly. The delivery person left the parcel at the reception of my hotel without taking OTP directly from me, and later called me asking for the OTP, claiming he already handed it over to the reception.I trusted him, collected the parcel from the reception, and gave him the OTP over the call. However, when I opened the box in my room, I was shocked to see that the earbuds were missing from the packet. The sealed box was empty.To make things worse, the parcel was actually delivered around 6 PM, but in the Amazon app, it kept showing “Out for Delivery” until 11 PM. Because of this delay in status update, I couldn’t raise the complaint immediately.When I finally reported the issue to Amazon customer support, I received no helpful response or resolution. Till now, I have not received any refund or replacement.This was a very disappointing experience. I’ve always trusted Amazon, but such carelessness in delivery and lack of support has broken that trust. I hope Amazon takes this feedback seriously and improves both its delivery and customer support systems.

review_title: I had one of the most frustrating…
review_text: I had one of the most frustrating experiences returning a simple item (Invoice date / Delivery date 06 June 2025, Invoice # GB500OBI41WYNI)After booking a Royal Mail pickup for the return, I waited all day, but no one came. When I checked the return status, it bizarrely said the collection was "rejected by the carer." I reached out to Amazon Customer Service and spoke with a representative named Rodolphe, who was kind and helpful. He apologized, acknowledged the issue, and issued a refund without requiring a return — saying I could keep or donate the item and that the money would be back on my bank card within 3–5 business days.A week later, with no refund received, I contacted Amazon again. This time, I dealt with multiple representatives who seemed completely unaware of the prior conversation. I was asked the same questions over and over, and told that I now had to return the item. When I tried to explain that I’d already been promised a refund, I was met with contradiction and confusion. The process was re-initiated, and I was told it might take up to 37 days to receive my money — a ridiculous time frame for something that wasn’t my fault in the first place.What really pushed this from frustrating to unacceptable was the final representative, who was dismissive and borderline rude. Instead of acknowledging my concern, they repeatedly said:"I repeat, the refund will be issued once you return it."This robotic response, ignoring everything I’d gone through and previously been told, came across as condescending and completely unhelpful. No empathy, no resolution — just scripted deflection.Amazon's customer service used to be exceptional, but now it's inconsistent, slow, and at times downright frustrating. I feel let down by a company I used to trust. I’ve saved screenshots and will be escalating this further.

review_title: As a small business owner who depends…
review_text: As a small business owner who depends on timely and reliable deliveries, my experience with Amazon has been increasingly frustrating and detrimental to my operations.Over the course of numerous orders, I have consistently faced severe delays—many shipments arriving well past their estimated delivery dates, and a significant number not arriving at all. This level of inconsistency is unacceptable, particularly for a platform that promotes itself as a reliable business partner.Even more concerning is the response I have received from customer service representatives. On more than one occasion, I have been told, “If you don’t like it, we can show you how to close your account.” This kind of dismissive and unprofessional attitude is completely inappropriate and indicates a troubling level of disregard for customer concerns. It’s clear that Amazon’s scale and market dominance may have led to a decline in customer care and accountability.I strongly urge Amazon to investigate these complaints seriously and take immediate action to improve both the logistics and the customer service experience for business account holders like myself.

review_title: Rude and Disrespectful Customer Service Experience
review_text: I’ve been a loyal Amazon customer for years, but what I experienced recently was beyond shocking — downright insulting.On July 14, 2025, I reached out to Amazon Customer service to cancel Prime subscription that I didn't subscribe. I tried to cancel the membership myself (which didn’t even appear in my Amazon account). Instead of receiving help, I was treated with blame, deflection, and arrogance.The first agent, Santhosh, kept insisting the subscription came through Google Play, despite me confirming multiple times and with screenshots — that there was no such subscription listed. Still, he refused to assist.Things escalated when I was passed to his supervisor, Vyshnav, who made the entire situation worse. He was rude, arrogant and condescending. Not only did he accuse me of dishonesty by saying (“So you were not aware that you signed up for a free trial? You were.”), he arrogantly shut down my request to escalate further, saying:“I am the highest point of contact you can be connected to.”The entire tone of the call was intimidating and hostile, when all I wanted was clarity and cancellation. For the record, I never asked for a refund.In stark contrast, Google Support was respectful and helpful — and confirmed the charge didn’t come from their end. So it clearly originated from Amazon.This was hands down the most humiliating and disrespectful interaction I’ve had with any company.Amazon, do better. This isn’t just poor service — it’s customer mistreatment. Such a Shame.

review_title: Amazon lies do not purchase from them
review_text: I was an Amazon prime member for 3 years, I ordered thousands of dollars worth of things from Amazon that I simply could not buy in the town I live in. First year was great, but for the last while now, last 6 months in particular, I have had many bad experiences with Amazon, the worst that ended it for me making me cancel my subscription was when I ordered through a third party seller on Amazon, I ordered a 12 inch rough finish hinge, and I got sent a 1 inch smooth finish hinge. This was already very inconvenient, but I calmly went on to start a return process for the useless item I received. The seller sent a label that would require me to pay postage, and a 20% restock fee out of my own pocket! After they sent the entire wrong item. So I emailed and got customer service and they said it would be dealt with. They lied, I had to call the next day and ask them to send me a full and free return label. They said they would get the seller to. The seller did not, they only hassled me for pics of my time which I sent, and then they asked for even more pictures even though I already sent a bunch. I was already extremely annoyed at this point. After 3 calls over 4 days with Amazon , each time they promised to resolve my situation even offered an a-z claim where I wouldn't have to do anything or return the item and they would just send full refund. THEY LIED ABOUT THAT TOO! TWICE! at the end of a super stressful week, I got emails about the a-z claim asking for even more information about my problem even though I had spoken and told 3 different representatives who supposedly made notes and assured me this would be dealt with properly. I still have no refund, I am out and overpriced hinge that is useless to me and I have cancelled my Amazon and telling everybody about this. From what I have heard this is becoming increasingly for frequent, I do not advise to buy from Amazon and beware especially if third party sellers on there. I suggest to order from the source website of whatever you are trying to purchase. This was the most painful buying and returning experience I have ever had and I buy lots as I am a builder.

This means that products with better reviews are more likely to appear at the top of search results, influencing visibility and sales. My personal experience has been a positive one. However, I like taking advantage of the 30-day money-back guarantee. So, yes, Amazon is for me. Yay!

had a $359 item (Bugaboo Donkey 5 Mono) in my Amazon cart during a lightning deal. Before I could finish checking out, the price jumped to $1,749. I immediately contacted customer service. A representative explicitly promised me that I would still receive the original price. I have the chat screenshots to prove it.I was told to wait for an email confirming the discount. It never came.I followed up multiple times — waited on hold, spoke to supervisors, and eventually escalated the issue to Amazon’s Executive Customer Relations team. They admitted the representative gave me incorrect information, but still refused to honor the promise. Instead, they gave me a $200 credit, which doesn’t even cover the price difference, let alone the time I spent chasing it.The most frustrating part is this: Amazon is saying that their own employees can make written promises to customers — and then Amazon doesn’t have to honor them. That’s not a pricing error. That’s a failure in trust and accountability.I’m sharing this so others are aware: even if an Amazon rep guarantees something, there’s no guarantee Amazon will stand behind it.

So I've been in the market for a cycling computer. Amazon had a good deal and the shipping would have made it delivered by the 10th of July, so I ordered it. The 10th of July came around and no order. Item didn't even ship it. So I contacted Amazon's customer service right off the bat. The customer service rep apologized for the inconvenience said it would be escalated and a ticket would be put in for it to be delivered immediately by the 11th of July. The 11th came and still not even shipped yet. So again getting frustrated I contacted Amazon's customer service. Again they apologized for the inconvenience and said they would put in another ticket and had contacted they shipping management for it to be immediately shipped and delivered first thing in the morning on the 12th of July. The Customer Service rep also stated that as soon as the item was delivered that I should contact them and they would issue me a full refund as a token of their apology. So I gladly accept it. Here we are at the 12th of July with the cycling computer still not having even been shipped yet. Contacted Amazon's customer service again and this time I just kept getting switched between customer service reps. No one wanted to investigate what happened with my order. One rep said it got stuck somewhere and another just forwarded me to the next rep. Finally someone connected with me and said that the item wouldn't be delivered until Monday the 14th of July! I could bought this thing from a different online store and probably gotten it after than that. The rep then asked for "proof" that it was supposed to be delivered by the 10th of July and I showed them the screenshot. The rep said that they didn't ask for a conversation screenshot but for a screenshot of the actually item stating it's original delivery date (I don't know how does that, but I guess I will from now on). I also asked this rep about getting a refund after delivery as promised by the other customer service rep and they said "Sorry you received misinformation". I ended up cancelling the item and still haven't received my refund yet. Amazon sure really knows how to bait and switch their customers. As well as not honoring a single word that any of the Customer Service reps say.

review_text: I’m an independent author and publisher whose books have been trapped in Amazon’s broken Kindle Direct Publishing (KDP) system for over a month, with no way to reach a human being or correct a critical account error.Amazon linked my published titles to a defunct personal email instead of my verified business account (Noble Heritage Press). I’ve submitted multiple support tickets, forms, and follow-up messages — every response has been automated, misdirected, or irrelevant.There is no escalation process. No working phone number. No human oversight. As the sole author and rights holder, I am being locked out of managing or promoting my own work.This is not an isolated glitch — it’s a systemic failure that treats authors like data points rather than partners. Amazon profits from our work, yet refuses to support the creators behind it.I’ve filed a formal BBB complaint and urge other authors to demand accountability. If Amazon wants to keep claiming it supports independent publishing, it must start acting like it.

review_title: KDP Closed My Account After Approval – No Explanation, No Response
review_text: My name is Bilal Amirat, and I am a former KDP author. Two years ago, my KDP account was suddenly closed without warning or clear explanation — despite the fact that my book had been reviewed and approved by the KDP content team.Before the closure, I was in communication with KDP support. They asked me to change my account email, which I did — from bboybilly1942@ gmail.com to bamirat3@ gmail.com. Shortly after that, my book was approved and confirmed to comply with KDP’s content guidelines.However, just days later, my entire account was terminated. I was shocked. I had followed every instruction from Amazon, acted in good faith, and respected all publishing rules.I have sent dozens of emails to Amazon and KDP support since then, trying to understand what went wrong — but I have received no replies. The Contact Us form won’t let me submit anything because my account is locked, and the email addresses I used to contact them no longer accept messages.I even filed a complaint with the BBB, but still no direct response from KDP.I have all the proof, documentation, and email conversations that show I did nothing wrong — yet I was treated like a rule-breaker and completely ignored.This is not how authors should be treated. I am asking for one simple thing: a fair review, and the chance to publish again.Please, Amazon: respond.My current email is: bamirat3@ gmail.com

review_title: Do not buy books on Amazon!
review_text: I have purchased over 40 books through Amazon in the last month. FORTY. That's a lot of my hard earned money. I've been filling my home library with my upcoming surgeries to keep me busy plus... I can never have enough books. Now typically I order my books from other places or go into the stores but I thought Amazon would be a nice easy way to get them given my current health issues so that way I don't have to leave home. However due to their absolutely terrible packaging of books many of my books have came damaged. I also had an instance where right before the holiday I placed an order for some I wanted to take with my out of town to my families house and noticed my Amazon driver had not moved in HOURS. I reached out to customer service concerned because I knew I'd be away for a couple of days but they assured me they'd be delivered that day by 10. They weren't. They were delivered the following day when I wasn't home and the package was stolen from my door. Amazon refunded me which was nice. But then I got an email saying I'm in violation of their terms because I've had too many issues lately. I kind of shrugged it off and placed numerous orders during prime day sales and more books arriving damaged. And now I'm stuck in a position that I can't even reach out to Amazon asking for help in fear of losing my account I've had for YEARS.after I opened my package tonight and saw one of my brand new books completely bent because they were thrown carelessly into a brown paper packing envelope I was done knowing I can't do anything about it unless I want to risk losing my account.I have done some research online to discover I'm not the only person with this issue. This has been an ongoing problem with Amazon poorly packing books and them arriving to customers damaged. We pay for a brand new, intact item, we should get that. And if we don't, we should not be getting punished for Amazons mistakes.I will not buy another book from Amazon until I hear they've improved their packaging process for books. There are too many other websites out there that ship books with care.Amazon... Do something about your awful packaging of books. You know packages get tossed into bins, and sent through assembly lines. Do better. It's such a waste!! No one likes receiving damaged products. And stop telling people they're in violation of your terms because they're pointing out that you've messed up.

I was told to wait a few days before they could even look into it.This isn’t the first time either — a previous delivery was also left at the wrong address. Amazon Prime is a premium subscription service offered by Amazon, designed to enhance the shopping and entertainment experience of its users. With a wide range of benefits, it has become one of the most popular membership services worldwide, especially among frequent online shoppers and digital content consumers. One of the most appreciated features of Amazon Prime is free and fast delivery. Subscribers get access to free one-day or two-day delivery on eligible products, which is a game-changer for people who shop frequently. In many cities, same-day and morning delivery options are also available. This alone makes the membership worthwhile for many users.Another major highlight is Prime Video, Amazon’s streaming service that offers thousands of movies, web series, and TV shows. It features both international content and regional entertainment, including Amazon Originals like The Family Man, Mirzapur, The Boys, and Reacher. While the library is not as vast as Netflix, the quality of content and consistent new releases make it a solid competitor in the streaming market.Prime Music is another benefit that offers ad-free music streaming with offline downloads. While it may not be as feature-rich as Spotify or Apple Music, it’s a great value addition, especially for casual listeners.Prime Reading and Prime Gaming further enhance the value of the subscription. Prime Reading allows members to borrow eBooks and magazines at no extra cost. Prime Gaming offers in-game content, free games, and a free Twitch channel subscription each month, appealing to the gaming community

review_title: Amazon Prime? More Like Amazon Eventually… If You're Lucky
review_text: Oh Amazon, where do I begin? I pay extra for Prime so I can enjoy that sweet “next-day delivery” — you know, the one they love to plaster all over their site. Except, surprise! My stuff almost never arrives the next day. In fact, I'm starting to wonder if “next day” is just a fun little inside joke over there.And when you try to get help? You’re treated to a riveting performance from customer service reps reading straight from a script with the passion of a cardboard box. They say a whole lot of words and yet manage to say absolutely nothing. It's like magic, but the really bad kind.Then there’s the compensation — ah yes, the glorious $5–$10 credit. But don’t get too excited! It’s only good for Amazon-sold items, which is code for “we’ll give you a coupon you probably can’t use.” Generous, right? That should totally make up for late shipments and broken promises.Honestly, it’s wild how a company with this much money can be this bad at basic logistics and customer care. But why try harder when you’ve got a monopoly and nowhere else for customers to go? Gotta love that “too big to care” energy.Here’s hoping someone finally gives them real competition, because Amazon needs a serious wake-up call.

I’m very sad that a big company like this is punishing their loyal customers instead of getting their house in order.

review_title: Urgent Complaint Regarding Faulty Product and Poor Customer Support Experience
review_text: I am writing to formally express my strong dissatisfaction with a recent purchase experience and the subsequent customer support I received through Amazon Egypt.Order Details:Product: Lenovo Slim i7 Laptop with Windows 11 (clearly written on the product page)Order ID:405-7938611-5597108Order Date: 22 June 2025Upon receiving the laptop and powering it on for the first time, I was met with a critical system error:“Default Boot Device Missing or Boot Failed.Insert recovery media and hit any key then select Boot Manager to choose s new boot device or to boot recovery media”This clearly indicates a hardware failure or missing operating system, rendering the device completely non-functional out of the box.I contacted Amazon’s customer service immediately. Unfortunately, I was met with a lack of accountability and empathy:The representative refused to provide any viable solution, such as replacement, expedited return, or compensation for the disruption this caused.I was told the only option is to return the laptop and wait up to 30 days for a refund, despite the product being defective upon delivery.When I requested to file a formal complaint, the representative claimed he could only file an “internal complaint” which I would not be able to track or follow up on.Additionally, the representative refused to provide his full name, effectively shielding himself from accountability. I was only able to know its initial from an automatic follow up email: (Mr Nour W.)This Is Unacceptable for the following reasons:I chose Amazon over other vendors expecting reliability and customer-focused service.Being left with a defective product and no resolution for a month directly impacts my professional work and obligations.The dismissive attitude and lack of transparency by your representative only amplified my frustration.What I Expect:Immediate escalation of this issue to Amazon’s customer satisfaction or quality assurance team.A clear and trackable complaint process with reference number and follow-up.An expedited solution—either:Replacement with a working device within a few days, orImmediate full refund upon return and a compensation credit for the inconvenience, in order to buy another laptop on time for my workInternal review of the customer service interaction, especially the conduct of the representative I dealt with

review_title: You’ll never get what you paid for.
review_text: I stopped buying from/via Amazon (I hear people now use the term “Scamazon”), after a series of incorrect items & delays, several years ago.  I lost at least $100, in the process, and thought throwing money away was unacceptable.But, recent circumstances happened, where a favourite item is ONLY sold on Amazon, and before I move abroad, I wanted to buy some replacements.Since I last bought these things, Amazon ordering has changed dramatically.  Now, multiple sellers sell this brand, often based on size.  This meant that my order of 2 of one size, and 2 of another size of this brand, were fulfilled by two different Amazon sellers, and not even the brand store I used to buy from.One package arrived on time but, when I opened it, they were clearly the wrong items.  (I ordered a pattern, and these were solid black.)  They were not in original packaging.  I opened one to see if I could tolerate it, and I kid you not, it was COVERED in lint and animal hair, AND there was a small piece of dried lettuce on the fabric, as well?!?!!!!!!Gagging, I quickly snapped a photo, and stuffed it back into the Amazon box.  Gross.  Even if the seller told me to keep them, I wouldn’t be able to donate them to charity, for sanitary reasons.  Ew.The second package never arrived.So, making this exception for one final purchase from Amazon, it was another complete failure, and I lost more of my hard-earned money.With that, I’m reminded why my decision to stop buying via Amazon was a good one.  (It cost me $25 to return the wrong, disgusting items that were delivered.  Still waiting for that refund.  Just started the refund process for the items never delivered.)  It also blew my last window to order similar items elsewhere, and have them delivered before I move abroad.  (After waiting 2+ weeks for the Scamazon non-delivery, it was then too close to my move-out date to risk it.  So, I’ve been harmed in multiple ways!)What a pathetic company.  It used to be really good.  Now, you’ll never receive what you paid for.

review_title: Increase in Cost of Household Products & Returns
review_text: I have been a customer of Amazon for many years. In the last few weeks noticed cost of household cleaning products, paper towels, deodorant, etc. have gone up by $1 or more. In addition to this, a blouse I returned (tags still on) via "live person" at Amazon Kiosk inside Whole Foods 10133 Louetta Rd, Houston 77070 took weeks for delivery back to Amazon (or so they claimed). Initially, (as they have done with most items), my Amazon account would be credited upon Whole Foods receiving. This was the case and I received email confirmation from Whole Foods same day I returned.  A few days later Amazon sent email stating they had not received blouse and therefore debited my bank account. I provided them with receipt noting where I had returned 3 weeks prior and they credited my account.2 days ago I returned an item and received email confirmation from Whole Foods. This morning, there was an email from Amazon noting I should return item as soon as possible or they would charge me. I called Customer Service and the rep said she could see where item was dropped off but it could take up to 14 days before my account is credited.I find it strange that it now may take 14 days vs in the past whereas once returned at Whole Foods, Amazon account was credited. Pay close attention and keep records for items returned. Had I not provided email confirmation most likely they would have not reversed debit. Both times upon calling Amazon, their reps said items were received.

review_title: Amazon = overpriced, bad quality products and unreliable shipping
review_text: To be honest I used to be an ebay girly, then I started paying for prime and thought it was great!! I used to recommend amazon to everyone! But latley I've noticed the product quality from sellers is terrible! everything even expensive things look like they're cheap quality, don't work properly or are defective/damaged. In the past 2 days alone, I've had: a set of saucepans that were damaged (saucepan lid handle snapped off and not in the bag with the lid, which leads me to belive it was damaged before being sent, not In Transit. A thermal printer which didn't work at all. It said it only had 3%battery so I charged it and still after an hour being charged it still said it only had 3%battery. And a staple gun which was made of flimsy and bendy plastic and the staples bent into an 'm' shape evertime they went into things. after all these problems, i realised that from what I can see, they don't have an option on there site to for complaints with amazon itself, it only gives u the option to complain about sellers. I understand amazon don't sell these products the sellers do, but amazon is allowing so many faulty and rubbish products to be sold on its site! The quality of amazon as a business had gone right down in my opinion and I'm considering leaving amazon. Why pay all that money a year for next day delivery aswell, when alot of the time it's not next day delivery it changes once you have paid for it to the day after or a couple of days later. Also I emailed amazon prime video and music asking if I was entitled to a refund for either of them as I pay for it when u get the basic version free with prime membership. They explained that I have an upgraded version so I'm not entitled to a refund, then they canceled my crunchy roll subscription and my amazon music and amazon video  upgraded stuff, It felt like I was being punished for asking if I was entitled to a refund or not.

review_title: If I could give 0 stars I would
review_text: If I could give 0 stars I would. This has been the worst experience ever. I have been having an ongoing issue with Amazon that started June 14. Customer service is horrible. What’s the point of customer service if they don’t do anything? Every customer service agent I’ve talked to has given conflicting answers and has done nothing to help the situation. They only say we’re so sorry you’re having this experience and we value  your feedback. If you value the feedback then do better. I ordered an item Wednesday June 11 that was supposed to arrive Friday June 13. I needed it for a last minute event Sunday June 15. I live in the city and have never had a problem with 2 day delivery. However, starting Friday, they started pushing my delivery date out. When contacting Amazon I was given false delivery information. They said I’d receive the item by 8am Sunday the 15th. However, not only did it not ship but when I tried to cancel it, they said I couldn’t because it has already shipped. BUT IT HADNT! They pushed the delivery date to Tuesday June 17 and when it still hadn’t shipped they told me I could get a refund the following day if it didn’t arrive by EOD Wednesday. Not only did they not issue the refund or allow me to request the refund, they pushed delivery to the 20th. When I contacted Amazon, they said they processed the refund. Is anyone surprised that nothing happened no updates were given. It was as if I never spoke to customer service at all. Unfortunately, It arrived June 19th. But I have no need for it. So I did return it as soon as it arrived. Let’s hope they actually do right. Which leads me to my next issue. In that same order I ordered another item that fortunately came on time but unfortunately didn’t fit. I returned it the day it arrived, Thursday June 12. However, not only is Amazon not allowing me to track the return but the first time I contacted customer service about the tracking not updating, they sent me a tracking slip with confirmation that it arrived at the seller on June 16. When recontacting customer service on why my account is still saying I need to return the item, the rep I spoke to said for security reasons they can’t provide any form of tracking and that I just need to trust that it was returned to Amazon. Very conflicting resolutions and very disappointing and frustrating. Customer service at Amazon is a joke and crappy. They have done nothing beneficial or to actually resolve any of my issues. Very upset and contemplating deleting my account, not that I’m that big of a difference but this is just a horrible experience that shouldn’t be an experience

And, that amazon have the money.So, now that amazon have taken responsibility and said in an email they would reverse the charges into my account.I waited.for the money going in, to which it never happened.I was so appalled with amazon I wanted to speak with their supervisor.VIRENDRA - who said he was a supervisor said over a recorded phone call " amazon have your money, but take it up with your bank "I asked for a transcript of the phone call and he diverted this conversation a severe amount of 7 times.After VIRENDRA admitted amazon had my money, he then went on to reverse the conversation and said he would get me a phone call from his manager, within 48 hours.It takes 48 to get a phone call - what an absolute joke.I asked on numerous times, for a transcript of the phone call, then VIRENDRA, kept saying take it up with your bank.Why, would he admit that amazon have my cash, then say on the RECORDED CALL, that I should take it up with my bank.So, I will wait the 48 hours , to get a phone call, to which I won't from his manager, as this has been said prior to the phone call.It's an absolute joke.I have been a premium member with amazon for a very very long time and to be treated like a 2nd class situation, was appalling.This money was for my MEDICATION, and I was worried, because now I have to go without medication, that I really, really need or my health will be deteriorated .They did not show any compassion, and said chase it up with your bank, once again, when.in.a recorded call, they said that they have my money.I wanted a transcript of the phone call and VIRENDRA, dismissed me about 7 or 8 times and was changing the conversation.I'm actually within my rights for a copy of the transcript, but he would not.say he would give me one and to wait yet another 7 times for an email, that I was entitled to.If someone says Amazon has my money  then do I not have the right to have my money back and a transcript of the phone call???So, I shall await 48 hours for a phone call, that I won't receive.I will also get an email stating take it up.with.my bank, when they know I raised a dispute with my bank.VIRENDRA,kept saying , "give me a copy of your email ", which is private and confidential to myself.But, he was adimit that he wanted a copy of my banks email, that I had received from them. In fact I would even go as far as to say, he pressurised me to get a copy.This is all recorded in a phone call and I want a transcript of the phone call, to which I will highlight, that he said amazon have my money.Anyway, I will wait on a phone call within 48 hours lol, we shall see.The supervisor was very angry with me and kept pushing that amazon has my money, but to take it up with my bank.AMAZON - I WANT A TRANSCRIPT OF THAT PHONE CALL, SO I CAN TAKE FURTHER ACTION, WITH YOURSELVES.

review_title: Amazon Destroyed My $18,500 Inventory and Withheld $10,250 Without Proof
review_text: I am a small business owner who registered a legitimate LLC in Wyoming and sold authentic products on Amazon, backed by complete supply chain documentation, invoices, and brand/distributor communications.Amazon falsely accused me of "fraudulent or deceptive activity" without providing a single piece of evidence. Over a year, they changed accusations multiple times — from "unsuitable inventory" to "potentially counterfeit" to "invoice risk" — with no transparency.Despite submitting all required documents multiple times (many of which Amazon had previously accepted), they:Disposed of $18,500 worth of my inventory.Withheld $10,250 in earnings.Ignored my direct questions and appeals.I reached out to the Better Business Bureau, the FTC, and the Washington State Attorney General’s office. Still, Amazon refuses to justify their actions or return what rightfully belongs to me.Amazon is a trillion-dollar company treating small sellers with arrogance and impunity. I want the public to know that this platform is dangerous for honest small businesses. If it can happen to me, it can happen to anyone.Muhammad Abdul SamadstopNshop365 LLC

review_title: BEWARE! DECEITFUL ITEM INFO IS OK!!!
review_text: Purchased a toilet riser from Amazon. Needed right away, so ordered with PRIME. SIX days later, I was issued a refund because UPS lost it, and I was advised to reorder.Took a week, item refunded. Repurchased item. I AGAIN have screenshots of where the product listed the item as “fits most toilets” AND “open box-like new” which was described as “minor cosmetic damage”.The item arrived within 24 hours this time. Great, because I NEEDED it a week ago!Opened package, after noticing that it is CLEARLY stated on the box that it is for ELONGATED toilets. I picked the “universal” seat because I have both types of toilets, plus I need this for travel.Continued to open, only to discover DRIED FECES & URINE all over the item!! This is NOT ONLY what was described, but it’s LITERALLY A FREAKING BIOHAZARD!!Spent over an hour on the phone with “customer service”. Was transferred 3 times, because apparently there isn’t a department that specifically deals in CRAP and BIOHAZARDS.Uploaded multiple photos, which even the rep agreed were DISGUSTING!!Resolution? “Return for refund, and NOT TO ORDER “open box” items, NOR items from AMAZON RESALE”. [Full disclosure: I DID click the link for “lower prices from other sellers”, to save over $15. HOWEVER, I DID DO due diligence, screenshot ALL relevant descriptions, and it was described as “Open Box - LIKE NEW” and as “fits MOST toilets”, and BOTH were BALD-FACED LIES!!] I did not notice that the seller was Amazon Resale,  BUT that does NOT mean they can BLATANTLY LIE and SEND BIO-HAZARDOUS MERCHANDISE TO CUSTOMERS!!So there you have it, DIRECTLY FROM Amazon “customer service”: “DON’T BUY OPEN BOX OR AMAZON RESALE ITEMS! ONLY BUY NEW, INFLATED PRICE ITEMS”, or BUYER BEWARE!!Also, I found it interesting I was NOT able to leave a review on Amazon once the BIO-HAZARDOUS item was in a return status. So, if you receive something that is TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE, you might review it FIRST, before initiating the return. No guarantee they won’t still take it down, but that’s why sites like TrustPilot are here!!

review_title: Failed to commit on their delivery

That commitment was broken—not due to weather, delays, or logistics, but because the delivery driver “couldn’t locate” the clearly marked main entrance and refused to complete the delivery.I immediately brought this to Amazon’s attention, expecting them to uphold the level of service they promise millions of customers every day. Their response? A mere $5.00 concession for a failed delivery, wasted time, and outright refusal to fulfill the order—despite the fact that the address was accurate and accessible.Let this be a message to Amazon and a warning to the public: when a trillion-dollar corporation treats customer service as a checkbox and compensates failure with pocket change, it exposes a disturbing truth—profit comes before people.If Amazon cannot deliver what it promises—and chooses to minimize the impact on loyal customers—then it’s time consumers take their business elsewhere. We deserve better than indifference.
